review_text: juegazo de lo mejor que e terminado

the best metroidvania i've played, even better than HK  the map is huge and an absolute joy to explore challenging and with cool new movement mechanics which you rarely see in other metroidvanias various interesting attack techniques, unlocking as you progress the combats are fresh and skill-based  those who leave negative reviews are just casual players with skill issues  game for those who enjoys path of pain !

An excellent platformer and metroidvania, with a solid focus on challenging platforming. The best (hardest) 2D platformer I've come across that isn't just pure finnicky platforming. Highly recommended for people who enjoy metroidvania's and always find themselves wanting more out of the platforming.

Bon jusqu'au 3/4 du jeu, après ça plus aucun plaisir à jouer. Le royaume des rêves, visuellement moche, qui est série de puzzle tirés par les cheveux. Le cosmos où j'ai d'abord failli désinstaller mais qu'en persévérant j'ai finalement trouvé assez fun à parcourir, mais quand même beaucoup trop grand pour un niveau gimmick. Puis viennent l'ascension finale et le château qui sont une série d'innombrables +- grandes salles sans intérêt niveau platforming, avec 10 ennemis spammers d'attaque au mètre carré, donc à l'image du jeu dans son ensemble : à rallonge et artificiellement difficile.  Sans parler des boss complètement illisibles parce que salle [couleur1] + boss [couleur1] + projectiles [couleur1]. Qui a eu cette idée...? Vraiment dommage...

Dont waste your time with this crap. Worst mv i ever tried. Whoever thought it was a good idea to use the same key for an attack and to 'stick to the wall', ♥♥♥♥ U, u dont deserve to be a game dev. Not being able to aim while 'stuck to a wall' is also a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ stupid idea, again, ♥♥♥♥ U.
能想出宇宙跳跳乐，家里得请高人了

全程困难难度，打通关了（收集94.4%）。  8/10分（推荐），[b][u]但仅建议能接受【大量】【强制】【高难度】跳跳乐的人玩，仅喜欢类银的最好别买/玩[/u][/b]   原因是虽然标签有类银，但本质是一个披着类银皮的平台跳跃游戏，跳跳乐占比感觉>90%。 不过在跳跳乐方面，有不少创意，且量大/质量也不错。 尤其是宇宙地图的4个图很有新意，其中【限时+上下颠倒+冰火光暗结合】初接触时惊艳到我了，厉害！打完以后我感觉其他的图so easy了，第十神庙一会就打过了~    [hr][/hr]   [b]详细评测如下：[/b]  [b] 1）地图设计：7.5/10分[/b]  [b] 优点：[/b]  量非常大（且收集品/支线任务很多），同时各个区域内容还都有各自的特色，不是简单的换皮重复（这里说的就是你——[u]心渊梦境，类银+跳跳乐结合，但做得都很平庸的反例，回想起来纯浪费时间[/u]）。  并且死亡复活的点很多，加上一进游戏很快就能拿到死亡不掉魂的宝石，所以相当于没什么死亡惩罚。  [b] 缺点：[/b]  游玩过程中，充斥着【大量】【强制】【高难度】的跳跳乐。想纯赶路？不行，必须得强制玩一遍跳跳乐。拿完收集品想快速返回继续推图？不行，也得再挑战一遍回来，而不是快捷通道直接结束......  过程中一度血压飙升。可以喝药直接返回王座，但是限量且不同图的商人价格不一，想多快速传送+省点钱就很麻烦....   感觉没必要，优点浪费时间了。  另外整个大地图是一条直线，没什么隐藏要素和捷径可以探索的，所以说再次说明这不是类银。 还有就是这地图传送时体验不太好，按传送时自动缩小，看不清是不是要去的王座，并且上下快速切换时很不灵敏。  放大了移动比较准，但是又很慢....  [b] 2）战斗：7/10分[/b]  [b] 优点：[/b]  操作顺滑流畅，boss战各有特色，并且非主线boss很多，赞！支线任务还会触发已打过boss的多阶段，难度急剧增加，喜欢挑战自我的人有福了... 不过后期有快速回血的红宝石+治疗技能点满后，可以不断回血硬抗，用主宰之刃强攻，很快就能打死boss。  [b][u]ps：这里强推【主宰之刃】这个武器，我后期才去拿，拿到以后就没怎么用过剑和镰刀了，搭配主宰之刃增强宝石，以及快速回血或者减少鲜血消耗的宝石，刷小怪打boss爽歪歪！[/u][/b]   [b] 缺点：[/b]  因为是跳跳乐为主体，所以boss战更偏向于躲弹幕，打多了真的有点蛋疼。 小兵感觉数量很多但换皮比较多，并且很多死亡会爆炸或者超快速冲刺，导致不得不等待一会或者强制躲一下。 飞行的小兵还不好打到，收集图鉴有点蛋疼。  [b] 3）技能/宝石系统：8/10分[/b]  [b] 优点：[/b]  通关时大概40个技能点，差不多能点满2条技能树，够用了，同时可以随时在王座洗点，挺方便。 用6个宝石槽位+3种宝石限制数量，实现不同build，感觉应对不同场景也算有一定策略性和收集的乐趣。  [b] 缺点：[/b]  没啥难受的点，就是不知道为什么要限制3蓝2紫1红，其实我觉得有时候6蓝更有用。因此很多宝石我并没有用过。哦对了，技能树部分节点的说明有bug，有几个是日文和汉语拼音，不知道为啥，不过好在影响不大。  [b] 4）画面/音乐/剧情：7/10分[/b]  [b] 优点：[/b]  画面挺漂亮的，比较像空洞骑士的那种风格。 有过场动画，甚至有中文配音，厉害了！ 音乐都是钢琴曲，咋一听还不错，不过并没有特别好听  [b] 缺点：[/b]  过场动画有bug，画面和字幕对不上 有剧情但不多，世界观也不是很大
不像类银，本质2d动作游戏平台跳跃游戏，而且没有预输入。每张地图都没有重复探索的乐趣，没有捷径每次都需要重新跳一次，怪物也不能跳过。

No game has a right to be this beautiful. Both in scope, story, setting, and art.  I am blown away by the art. It literally had me grabbing people to show the new areas off.  If you like very difficult Metroidvania style games, here is your gold nugget.

Played this in Noctis mode, very fun, frustrating, but overall enjoyed the entire playthrough. Collecting items though can be hassle, hopefully Aeterna Lucis indicates if every collectible in an area is found instead of leaving it up to guesswork.

Like many of the other reviews, the game is just too big for its own good. Like the map is unnecessarily big with lots of empty space for no reason other than atmosphere. Makes the game terrible for exploration and retracing your steps.   The temple level is also aggregious with the fact that after you complete it, it doesnt simplify itself to make retravel easy and is filled with annoying invisible puzzles.  The overall gameplay is extremely simple with mostly just mashing the attack and tanking being effective for most of the bosses.  It is also painfully boring after getting halfway through the game mainly due to the annoying travel mechanics and lack of sprinting making the character move around slowly.  Enemy variety seems ok for a metroidvania, but many just goes down to elemental variants and are really annoying to clear since around 30% of them have some sort of suicide mechanic or invincibility into suicide.  The visuals of the game are kind of mediocre for some reason. Even though there are so many animations and cutscenes that seem high quality. The artstyle just seems like a highschool boys edgy art. But the cutscenes are abundant but the story seems non-sense and I personally didnt care 90% of the time.  Summary: -Map is too large making it slow and boring to explore -Gameplay is tedious with many of the platforming being super annoying -Even in hard mode bosses can be just tanked and mashed -Stupid map discovery where it doesnt fully uncover, and map hints are overly pricy for no reason (even if you have all the items in an area its still the same expensive price with no discounts per items found) -upgrade mechanic is a pain making you need to trek back to home base just to get a potential traversal upgrade (almost none of them felt fun to obtain) and for some reason there are 2 sources for double jump which seem mandatory for some of the BS platforming puzzles making you waste a gem slot.  6/10  The game doesn't seem to respect the players time, with the large map and slow movement, the large amounts of backtracks just to unlock things(Yes even for a metroidvania). The main joy of metroidvanias is unlocking new travel or unlock mechanics and finding more things to explore but this game makes it feel like a complete bore since most of the traversal upgrades are so mediocre and incremental like 1 more charge of arrows and nothing that feels new or exciting. I played 34 hours to reach the final stage and it felt like a complete drag after the 50% mark. Vertical traversal is such a complete pain to do even with a triple jump. Its like in the final stages they just wanted to pad out gameplay time by adding so much space with not much content (I mean literal space).

J'ai laché l'affaire avec ce jeu. J'aime les jeux difficiles mais celui là il est extremement mal designé ! Moche,  labyrinthique, aucun feeling dans les combats. Charactère design horrible, non mais sérieux qui à dessiné les artworks, un collégien ? Tourne très mal sur steam deack alors que le rendu graphique est daté. Allé hop à la poubelle
A great gem in the metroidvania panorama. I'm so looking forward to the sequel!

5/10 No es mal primer juego pero como metroidvania tiene demasiados fallos de diseño a mi parecer: Enemigos muy repetitivos. Dificultad artificial por momentos con hitboxes muy dudosas. Alguna que otra zona muy pesada como el cosmos donde la dificultad tiene que ver más con los controles que con el propio nivel.  La casi completa ausencia de atajos una vez te has pasado una zona de plataformeo teniendo que depender constantemente de los viales dimensionales o repetir la zona del revés para salir. El estilo visual me parece bastante sobrecargado y a veces cuesta ver lo que pasa en pantalla con tanto efecto. El movimiento cuando tienes todos los poderes no es satisfactorio del todo, encadenar movimientos no se siente bien, no es suave y siempre prefiero ir de viaje rápido en viaje rápido antes que andando.  Por otro lado la música del juego es realmente bonita y la historia me ha sorprendido, me esperaba que fuese por un lado diferente y me ha gustado la dirección que ha acabado tomando.  Dicho esto, tengo ganas de la secuela para ver cuanto se pueden mejorar los puntos malos que he mencionado antes porque realmente hay potencial aunque, para mi, este primer juego se queda cojo en muchos aspectos.
My new favourite metroidvania. lmao at guys getting skill issued and venting on reviews

This is one of the worst metroidvanias I have ever played. Every little thing this game does well, other games do better. And whenever it does something bad, it has no competitors in that regard.  The gameplay itself is okay, I suppose. Though the controls are so bad that they kinda ruin everything. The main offender is the teleport arrow. Or rather, the way they handled it (because the idea was actually really solid). Instead of aiming with the right analog stick and shooting with R2, you need to press Triangle to charge the arrow and Circle to stand still. That already feels wrong. You can at least rebind those two buttons, but then you still need to aim using the left analog stick. I can't describe how stupid it is to bind aiming and movement together. By the way, you can't stop yourself midair even if you press Circle. That causes so many problems for me that I consider the controls the main source of difficulty. Silksong was hard in terms of platforming, but the controls were so good that I didn’t get frustrated even once (well, maybe once).  Overall visual design feels bland and uninspired. The enemies look very generic: skeletons, ghosts, all kinds of slimes, bugs, some robots... bats? Really? For some reason, every other enemy explodes after dying, which adds another layer of frustration, since you either need to waste time waiting to absorb the loot or waste skill points on perks that automatically pull everything toward you.  The level design is bad, too. Some areas are just long, long corridors with occasional platforming sections. Others are death traps filled with REALLY stupid dead ends. Oh, you didn’t know you had to use a lever on the other side of this huge alien ship before coming here? Too bad! Now go all the way back and try again. Did I mention there are almost never any shortcuts, so you have to redo all the platforming sections in reverse? And then do them one more time after you finally find the item or ability needed to progress? Backtracking has never felt this painful before.  Sadly, I can’t say anything positive about it. I love this genre, but Aeterna sucks a$$. I regret wasting both money and time on it. I was hoping it would get better the more I play. Instead, it only got worse.

Franchement j'aurais adoré recommander ce jeu, il y a de très bonne base de metroidvania, mais il y a trop de défauts, le jeu n'est pas "dur" au vu du peu de punition à la mort, il est principalement frustrant: - Les phases de plate-formes sont d'une difficulté absurde, compensé souvent par un respawn 3 mètres avant. Mais du coup quand on la passe après le 23ème essai, on a même pas le sentiment de réussite vu qu'on se demande si on a pas eu juste un coup de chance. - Les boss sont très variables avec une prépondérance de pattern peu lisible et pas du tout instinctif à esquiver. (quand on essaie pas juste de les brute-force au 12ème essai) - Le marchant de carte est BEAUCOUP trop facile à rater (comparativement au GOAT dans Hollow Knight). - Parmi les ennemis de bases, on retrouve beaucoup de pattern trop dur à dodge, des re-skins pas très intéressant, etc. - La hitbox du personnage aurait simplement gagné à être réduite de 10-15%, ça aurait TOUT rendu plus acceptable. - Je trouve la DA plutôt chouette et l'histoire acceptable.  Franchement si vous voulez un bon metroidvania, Il y en a un paquet qui sont simplement de meilleure qualité (et beaucoup moins frustrant): Hollow Knight et Silksong, mais également Blasphemous 1 et 2 ou encore Nine Sols. Je regarderais avec attention les prochains jeux de ce studio car les bases restent tout de même très bonne

I got this as part of a Metroidvania bundle and it was definitely one of the last ones I gave a try and for good reason. From booting it up watching the very weak opening cutscene, the writing was lackluster and hasn't seen an editor, the controls felt clunky and when I got the dash ability it actually felt worse.  Though reading some other reviews I'm guessing the problems only get worse the deeper you get into the game. I can't imagine paying $30 for this, or even on sale.  If you enjoy this game, good on you I hope you support the devs and I hope they improve their skills but ultimately this wasn't for me.

its a good game for awhile ... then you have to use teleport arrows . alot . controls became to janky and i had to try simple areas way too many times . gotta shoot that arrow just right .... in midair then another before you hit the ground , time it ....ect . I gave up. game does go on a good sale often if you are up for an annoying challenge .
Отличная метроидвания, не без минусов (Главный из минусов - сложность), но с отличным сюжетом, геймплеем, миром и прокачкой. Короче кайф. Прошёл на все 109%

too much map, paths, platforming, backtracking, switches, etc.  the maps, the hazards at every step, the amount of backtracking, and everything else mostly felt designed with almost little or no regard for players, frustrating game to play, especially that dream palace (oracle's), where encountering any enemies inverts controls and directions, constant shooting of arrows because "illusion", all confusing, disorienting and more, that's on top of all the obnoxious platforming, timing, etc, or the Cosmos part, even though really liked the art and what was done but was rage inducing going through that, didn't finish game even though was closer to end (left was "corroding towers" from quest 'fall of light'), all this is mandatory part of main quest. custom markers also are limited, and didn't feel like they helped much anyways.   good: visuals/art, combat, male enemies I liked (elite fanatic, troll slave, fanatic private, dynastic sentinel, protector of the invictus, etc), story/lore  for anyone that wants to play, I suggest first going straight to tower of light boss (for light arrow switches, after second king's trail I think), then cradle of light boss (for teleportation arrow), then phoenix boss (for double jump), then others maybe (something like so)  edit: went back and finished game, and ending was worth it, and i liked how could continue exploring and more despite main quests ending, but all i said above still.
god

Несомненно один из лучших метро за последние 5 лет. В чем то даже лучше Холлоу Кнайт. А Холлоу я рэйчу! Сложная, огромная игра, несмотря на все каноны метроэдвании, может удивить и вызвать улыбку. Классные персонажи добавляют погружению. Атмосферная музыка и приятная озвучка. Не обошлось без духоты-поиск дракона, святилище таин, ну и космические прыжки. Некоторые боссы и полосы препятствий дадут прикурить несомненно. Так и не смог найти последнюю темную материю - либо баг, либо я невнимательный. Но рекомендую эту игру всем любителям жанра.

JUEGAZO, este es un gran metroidvania, es hermoso artisticamente, su jugabilidad es desafiante y brinda un excelente feedback al control, me diverti muchisimo completando el juego, se le puede criticar que tal vez es un metroidvania muy abrumador por la cantidad de mapa que tiene, el entorno del COSMOS es dificil de superar y el entorno del ORACULO no me parecio tan divertido como todo lo demas, en este entorno en particular las imagenes del escenario de fondo llegaron a confundirme en varias ocasiones para algun salto o dash, pero son defectos que para algunos pueden ser aciertos, otra cosa increible del juego es la musica, hermosa y a mi me transmitio esta melancolia de una historia de amor condenada a fracasar
Just don't try to 100% this without a guide for your sanity.

The challenging platforming and puzzle-style rooms are fun and hard on the thumbs. And when you get to the bosses you rage quit and uninstall. The bosses are insanely difficult and the joystick controls simply are not fast enough inputs to do things like pogo on top of enemies. The Worm boss requires you to pogo on the baby worm heads (I think, I tried twenty times, turned down difficulty to Normal and tried five times and gave up) and I couldn't count on being able to pogo. Compare to another Metroidvania like Silksong where I only used d-pad for controls and it's hard to get used to the joystick. I'm using a Xbox series x controller. Oh well it was fun for ten hours but the bosses are Dark Souls level insanely frustrating.  The art style is an amusing mix of styles that I think works well, though there are sections where the foreground covers the background where you are platforming and fighting and hoo boy that was frustrating in a few places but overall not a deciding factor. I wish I wasn't getting stopped at this point with only 25% of the game finished as there is a HUGE amount of world to explore which was a big plus.  Gave it two days and looked up that I was missing a tool that seriously helped to beat the boss. Went back, searched for the tool, found it and went back to finish off that worm ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. Have left difficulty on Normal mode and beat a couple more bosses and cleared another 10% more of the game.
量大管饱的跳跳乐，不错的打击手感，加上动听的音乐，还有独特的手绘画风，一玩就停不下来，这个画风我超喜欢，还有血量ui和地图收集品的ui，我也很喜欢，还有很多不同的武器，技能，我真的特别喜欢这个游戏，跳跳乐做的也很多，可能是跳跳乐游戏玩多了，困难难度目前玩下来很爽，没有很难的跳跳乐！21.6打折买的，体量超级大，还有独立的世界观，谁玩谁懂，夯爆了！！！

Difficile de dire si je recommande ou pas puisque j'ai fait près de 80% du jeu (dixit le jeu) en 50h... Mais que j'arrête là, le je m'a tout simplement lassé, cela faisait déjà plusieurs heures que je ne m'amusais plus... A quoi bon ?  Très bien réalisé, les contrôles répondent très bien, c'est ample, avec beaucoup d'ambition...  Mais c'est beaucoup, beaucoup trop long pour un tel niveau de challenge. Vers la fin on se rapproche davantage d'un Kaïzo Mario World que du white palace de Hollow Knight ou de son combat contre Radiance.  Ni la DA ni l'histoire ne m'ont pris aux tripes, seul le challenge me tenait au jeu, et c'est un réel plaisir de développer les skills du perso et d'arriver à les maitriser; mais au bout de 50h le jeu te tombe des mains, plus envie.  Vous voilà avertis !
Шипучка Шипы .
地图全是跳跳乐，boss战就是大型跳跳乐，连平台游戏很经典的存档点都来了，这不是平台游戏是什么？但是随便加一点boss战就说自己是类银游戏，我只能说别来蹭，平台就平台，有你自己的受众，靠标签骗玩家只能收到差评

Everything in this game is just kind of annoying. Nothing is as simple as it should be, even things like warping at checkpoints or talking to NPCs involve jumping through some hoops whether its extra steps or strangely precise positioning to talk to some NPCs. Its a lot of little things that just got to me more and more as I played.  Almost every enemy is annoying in some way, with a number of them just being invulnerable until you stand in front of them long enough for them to attack, or they'll be completely trivial but explode on death forcing you to wait because you want the drops or its on some small platform and you risk contact damage.   I ultimately decided to flush it because putting an orange boss with orange projectiles in an orange room is just awful. You can even see the boss I'm talking about in the Steam preview screens, its the 7th one after the videos,  Just ugh. Fairly sure I even almost beat it on my second try, but having to redo that long, repetitive fight just left me with a feeling of "I'd rather look at any other game."
not fun
A piano in a Metroidvania? Seriously? With zero explanation on how sharps work?Do you think every player know the music basic?, or just terrible design.

A sad attempt at a precion platfromer: the game consists of myriads of different complex platforming sections where obstacles, enemies (sometimes), and mechanics blend together to create a unique expereince of overcoming and achieving new heights... at least it could be so. But this one is just not fun or satisfying, and that is the problem. Despite its desperate throwing all kind of most convoluted "jump puzzles", all those... things are simply not that good for some simple reasons. One, the controls are just weir and not-so-great: the MC has a kind of latency between the moment you press the jump button and the jump actually regestring in game, although this thing is not that consistent (is usually happen when you respawn and... just sometimes. Sometimes you press the A button and not jump. I could not reckognise any pattern behind it but whatever). Two, the hitboxes. THe hitboxes are simply f***d up. A lot of enemies and sting-spiky obstacles have a greater hitbox that thier own model, for just a few pixels BUT THOSE PIXELS MAKE A GREAT IMPACT since you lose any sense of control. You cannot know when exactly you are going to die. Next, the hitbox of the MC is ♥♥♥♥♥♥ as well: horizontally, it's more narrow than the Char himself (and considarbly so). Vertically, on the other hand, the hitbox seems to reach just a little bit higher than the model -- than the last pixelf og the MC's hair. Again, these two factors only furthermore contribute to the lack of truly responsive controls. THe story is interesting, the music fascinating, and location as well... But the problem is, this game relies too heavily on the platforming mechanics. It seems almost it deems itself a TRUE precision platformer... The game is loaded with those platf. sections: there are LOTS, and LOTS of them. And each next is only getting worse: the more complex it gets, the more evident and outrageous all the imperfections of this game become. And that is why I would never recommend playing this. You will probably not have fun unless you like to cope and deal with unresponsive controls and... yeah, as simple as that. What could have becomen a great metroidvania that was exploring the more hardcore platforming landscape (yet explored not that well), is just a frustating, boring, really somewhat glitchy, yet highly punishing experience. Overall, I would rather nail my finger tips to the cross than play this again. If you want some so-called hardcore precision platformers that could be even more difficult but, with that, more interesting and awarding in terms of "fun", go play Celeste, Super meat boy, n++ or whatnot. But this game... pass it

Horrendous performance. Specs: 5700X 6650 XT  There are areas—for example, one with fire in the background—where the game drops to 40 FPS even on the lowest settings at 1080p.  I can’t say too much about the game yet, but so far it feels like a generic metroidvania with its own issues. However, this performance is a joke.

El juego es muy bueno en todos los aspectos, no le envidia nada a hollow knight o a otros del genero, si bien es un producto con una dificultad muy alta, no apta para qualquiera, quien busque un gran desafio aqui lo encontrara. Si bien me veo capaz de superarlo, y me habria gustado hacerlo al 100%, uno de los biomas me ha frustrado demasiado y no es que no me vea capaz de superarlo, pero hemos llegado a un punto en el que la zona en cuestion no la encuentro divertida, es demasiado tediosa, no la estoy disfrutando, y veo que es muy larga, asi que al final decido, muy a mi pesar, abandonar la travesia porque no estoy dispuesto a hacer semejante inversion de sufrimiento, solo para alcanzar la cima, y mira que me gustaria. Por si teneis curiosidad, la zona que me hace abandonar, es la del cosmos, donde saltas entre planetas y al estar en constante cambio de gravedad los controles se adaptan a ello invirtiendolos a ratos y me resulta un suplicio. Creo que la zona es original, y no veo mal que este ahi, pero la encuentro demasiado larga, y no es para mi, supongo entonces que el problema soy yo, pero en cualquier caso, decido no seguir. Aun asi, es un gran juego, muy recomendable, eso si, absteneros los que no querais un reto duro.

6/10  I keep teetering on if I want to leave a positive or negative review on this game. There are moments its great but there a few major issues that make me wanna rip into it.   The devs need to live stream themselves playing this game, start to finish so we can all see how good and fun it is. They'd never do it cuz they know they made a bs game that hides behind the "its difficult" bs.  Don't buy this game unless you're a hardcore metroidvania fan or really into overly aggressive platforming. I'm about 90% finished with this game, I've gotten pretty much all the collectibles up until this point and done all the platforming. So trust me when I say this is a waste of time. I played it on the original difficulty and its just not fun, interesting or engaging. Mainly because of how long and repetitive it is by design. It really starts to over shadow the positive aspects of the game, which is a shame. They put in an easier difficulty because EVERYONE complained about how terrible the design is, but the game was originally intended to be played on the hardest. So... here we are.  Twice, the game had issues with glitching and becoming unplayable. Both times on a boss fight, and it's really ruining my time. Especially considering that this games claim to fame is how difficult it is. Having to redo an already difficult and frustrating fight over and over because of bugs is no bueno guys.   First time it happened was in the beginning of the game when you fight the sword boss. My character kept flying to the ceiling and getting stuck. The only thing I could do was quit and restart the game, which sucks cuz I had to start from the very beginning.   The second was during the genie fight. My character would just straight up disappear from the screen. I couldn't use the attacks or heal animations to even get an idea where I was, but I could take damage. Real quality game they put together here.   Also, the button mapping for shooting arrows is just plain awful and creates a lot of needless frustration. Especially cuz later on you really rely on them to traverse the areas. They mapped aiming and movement to the same control in a precision platformer. They could have used the right joy stick which only pans the camera a bit, but they felt that was  bit silly... another example of their genus at play. WTF guys. Get it together.  Also, the ability to warp with the arrows is super inconsistent. F* it. It's pathetic.  I don't want to say this game is trash, cuz its not. But also, it kinda is. They expect you to be perfect but they get to cut corners with level design, game mechanics and control inputs and they think that's ok. They can't even get basic elements of design right. The game is terrible with contrasting important key elements of the level from the background. Everything just blends together. They even have foreground artwork that obscures your view and blocks elements and enemies from you. This is basic highschool art knowledge. Sad.  This game relies too much on "get good" / "try hard" type game play which I think would turn most players off. It also seems like a crutch they hide behind. Like its not us, its you. Our game is perfect, you just can't handle it. When in reality this game has a ton of issues.  And lets be honest here.... This is a SNES game in 2020 LMAO. Coding and Developing this game isn't anything special. Or complicated.  I'd never recommend this slop to anyone or ever buy another release from this publisher.

A metroidvania for people who've already mastered other metroidvanias. If you're the kind of people who 100%ed Hollow Knight, including superbosses and the boss rush. Or who gets bored easily by easy tutorial levels. This game jumps in the deep end and expects you to know how to swim. But if you do, you'll see some of the most engaging and interesting level design throughout the ENTIRE game. Not just in brief moments, but everywhere, every hallway, every boss fight, every branching path. It's all for experts, and it'll reward you for being so.  If you're not an expert, still struggling with tougher games and harder bosses? Well there's an easy mode that might help. But honestly you're better off playing Hollow Knight first. In the same way you wouldn't give someone black coffee with no sugar if they've never tasted it before.

Scrivo questa recensione dopo aver finito il gioco con una percentuale di completamento del 109% in modalità Noctis. Per me, come concezione, il gioco è perfetto! Non si può muovere alcuna critica alle sezioni di platforming: il gioco ti spinge a migliorare sempre di più, con una curva di apprendimento ben definita. Lo stesso non si può dire delle bossfight, perché non ha senso creare tutta una serie di pattern di attacco se poi possono essere aggirati scegliendo cosa sviluppare o no nell’albero delle abilità. Tanto vale creare bossfight più semplici nel gioco principale e riservare sfide a difficoltà crescente in un DLC o in una training zone. Questo, però, va a snaturare il gioco! È assolutamente normale avere approcci diversi alla battaglia, ma non è normale poter bypassare completamente la conoscenza dei pattern d’attacco, come invece accade in questo titolo.

Лучшая метройдвания для тех кому интересны сложные игры. Минусом в сюжетке может стать только то, что многих лейт боссов в игре можно просто зафейстанчить определенными билдами. Но эта проблема решается у Коллекционера в DLC (стоит 30 рублей на распродажах). Появляется новые режимы битвы с боссами и у всех боссов становятся доступны героический и мифический уровни сложности, которые не просто накидывают боссам ХП, а добавляют новые мувсеты, может изменяться окружение и полностью меняют динамику боя. Боссы становятся самым сложным челенджем в игре.

这是我玩过最臭最长最恶心的类银，意义不明的任务，全程任务做了也不知道有什么意义。操作难受，对比同样用弓箭传送的波斯王子，操作特别晦涩，手柄玩家体验我不知道怎么样，但是键鼠特别难瞄准，加上大量需要用到弓箭传送的跳跳乐，简直是灾难般的体验。特别是后续的太空，不错的设计，但是加上晦涩的操作手感导致体验非常糟糕。大量的跳跳乐过了以后给一些小破烂，过了以后毫无体验感。不论是任务或者跳跳乐打过一个以后都毫无成就感，只有这个游戏为什么还没有结束的疲惫感。还有那个破灵魂之镜，每次进去都得随时最好卡死闪退的准备。总的来说，游戏设计不错，但是指引在类银里也算是最差的一档了，操作难受，莫名其妙的卡顿，不推荐游玩。

首先感谢 @特伦斯塔德 赠送我本作。量大管饱的类银。  早闻本作高难度跳跳乐的大名，本着朋友送的是屎也要吃完的精神开始了这场旅程，没想到比想象中好玩的多，上头之后废寝忘食玩了好几天。如前面所说，《永恒之夜》内容量很大，庞大的地图塞入了大量收集品和跳跳乐。  美术方面，讲道理本作的美术是挺好的，很多场景都搞的不丑，但是最终呈现的效果还是有点廉价感，不过这个并不是很明显的大问题。音乐方面，本作的音乐挺用心的，还内置了一台钢琴可以让玩家自己弹。  本作毫无疑问存在大量问题，部分问题简直是不可饶恕的：有些地方能力锁设计有问题，本作在很多地方会先放一块跳跳乐，跳完跳跳乐的玩家会发现你和奖励之间还有一道能力锁，也就是说如果此时缺少能力，你前面的跳跳乐就白跳了，完全可以把能力锁放到跳跳乐前提醒玩家这里你缺能力了后面再来吧，可是本作没有这么做；部分支线设计狗屎，点名收集者的狩猎天界巨龙支线，龙会随机出现在地图上的多个位置，而你要抓到他三次，运气如果比较差需要来回跑多次地图，甚至有时候可能会刷新在上一个地点，甚至有个成就要你抓到他十次，与游戏地图的大小来说本作的传送点算偏少的，在这个前提下这个支线更狗屎了；部分地图设计狗屎，点名梦境王国和宇宙，梦境王国属于我非常讨厌的“感觉打完会漏好多东西”的地图，各个出入口的联通十分神秘，简直是一团乱麻，再加上隐形的机关和敌人，简直享受，还有宇宙更是重量级，不可否认的是宇宙这张图确实很创新，我第一次见到这种形式的银河城地图，但是体验实在太糟糕了，在宇宙里重力方向不是“下”，而是指向你所在星球的球心，如果只是方向键操作颠倒倒也还能接受，然而游戏中重要的跳跳乐能力，传送箭的瞄准也会受到重力的影响，手柄不清楚，但是鼠标瞄准的体验及其糟糕，你还需要在这样的地图进行环形的，一圈一圈从星球内部绕到外部的跳跳乐，还是后面有岩浆在步步紧逼的追逐战，真是享受啊；后期地图和boss设计敷衍，后期的几张地图实在是有点无聊，有明显赶工痕迹，至于boss的设计，有可能其实不敷衍，出招什么的可能颇有设计，然而数值的崩坏让玩家能简单地站撸速杀，导致毫无前期boss战那样紧张刺激的体验，最后两个boss我都没反应过来就弹成就了。  问题真是说了一大堆呢，毕竟相信每个全收集本作的玩家，通关过程都颇有怨气。对我来说，本作“好玩”的点主要来自于大量的收集和大量的支线带来的探索和奖励。仅仅这点就能够支撑我全收集这个“旷世神作”。  哎，果真是个混账游戏，但是如上文所述，比想象中好玩得多。推荐给喜欢跳跳乐，能接受高难度的类银爱好者。
兼具高難度戰鬥跟高難度平台跳躍的類銀河惡魔城神作，探索獎勵給的超爽，各種基於技能樹和寶石的Build也是爽的飛起。

noctis难度全收集√ 与其说是类银不如说是伪装成类银的平台跳跃游戏  本作唯一有趣的地方就是跳跳乐，宇宙：打起来很痛苦、但起码有新意（还能预防颈椎病x  地图设计极差，大部分地图都又大又空又无聊+收集品毫不隐藏，还伴随有光污染、同色系地图放同色系小怪令人眼瞎、前景遮挡地刺/小怪、每分钟全屏激光（说真的这打不到任何人我毫不理解它除了强制让人等时间防退款以外存在的意义但正常打到这里也超过退款时间了）这些仿佛恨玩家一样的设计 如果地图本身设计得好，这些“恶意”我可能会觉得是锦上添花的小巧思，但本作的地图底子就很差，这些东西只会让我烦躁  boss战大部分也毫无交互感，躲弹幕躲弹幕躲弹幕→等一两个输出窗口上去a两刀 帝皇和三见加里波第尤甚，血厚且没什么输出时机，死是死不了打也打不着，非常烦人  不推荐给想玩类银河恶魔城的玩家，只推荐给想玩平台跳跃的玩家 （顺便说在三段跳宝石焊身上的前提下，本作没有任何跳跳乐比苦痛之路难。我玩之前看到评论说本作苦痛之路只是入门or遍地苦痛之路，于是久久不敢购入，可能当时评论的大家都是dlc之前玩的或者自我挑战不带三段跳宝石吧）

Все что Вам нужно знать про эту игру: На геймпаде считываются стики, только когда стик максимально отведен от центра. Поэтому забудьте о том, чтобы у вас был мгновенный отклик - у вас будет вполне себе физическая задержка в управлении, где вы постоянно будете ошибаться в расчете расстояния при передвижении. Потому что будете пытаться передвинуться на чуть-чуть, но персонаж двигаться не будет.  Далее, если Вы нажмете прыжок, и выполните удар в прыжке, то все будет хорошо. Но если Вы не дай бог вначале нажмете удар, а и в эту секунду нажмете прыжок, то... прыжок не будет выполнен. Поэтому каждый раз, когда вам нужно ударить и прыгнуть - вы будете получать по лицу.  Про то, что у вас рывок имеет внутренний таймер в пару секунд, но визуально он никак не отображается, из-за чего вы постоянно будете падать в пропасть, слишком быстро передвигаясь по локации - про это я уже молчу. Зачем он нужен кстати я не понимаю, почему он просто не сбрасывается при касании пола, как это сделано во всех вменяемых играх - не ясно.  Я не буду писать про сюжет, хардкорность, или разнообразие противников и ситуаций, т.к. прошел только очень малую часть игры. Только скажу, что по первым часам очень скучно: бежишь непонятно куда и непонятно зачем, спотыкаясь от кривое управление и убивая достаточно простых, но назойливых противников. Отдельного упоминания заслуживают ситуации, в которых можно пройти всю локацию, дойти до закрытой двери, и только потом узнать, что тебе вообще не сюда, и ты даже получить ничего не можешь, никакого вознаграждения. И даже нет телепорта, поэтому беги, дружочек, всю локацию обратно. И с этим можно было бы смириться...  Но просто играть с таким управлением не хочется.

This is a great game i thoroughly enjoyed. There were many times where I was thinking at work damn, I just want to go home and just continue playing this game. I would recomend this game for anyone who likes Castlevania or Platforming games. But be warned this game is hard, very hard. You will die alot and have to redo parts. Luckely there is alot of Checkpoints so you don't lose to much Progress. There is an easy mode and the ability to always lower the difficulty even when you chose the hard mode but honestly, having completed it fully in hard feels rewarding.  As a Score I would give this Game a 8,5 out of 10. The Music is amazing, the Visuals are also amazing.  Now you might ask why is it only an 8,5 where did it lose the Score? I wont post any Spolers but there is one quest where you need to randomly find an NPC 3x which can be in any of the Zones... the Game is also Huge so it takes alot of time to navigate to the Locations due to the difficult platforming.The other part of the Score is reduced due to the Dungeon that has alot of puzzles. These 2 things really killed the pacing of the game, cause it takes so long to do them and figure out the solutions.  Technically the Game ran really good on a GTX 970, I only had a single graphical glich happen in the Puzzle Dungeon but I could fix it by changing the Graphics Settings. There was also one occurance where the Sound Bugged out and was playing Sound even though that sound shouldnt happen. But both of these felt acceptable. There is also Cheaters on the Time Trial Challenges, but honestly I never cared about being first getting the Platinum was challenging enough and rewarding.  I've wishlisted the Sequel lets see how this continues in 2026 :)

Uhhhhhh I agonized for a long time whether to give it a thumbs up or down, but the verdict is down.  Aeterna Noctis has SO MUCH POTENTIAL, but I feel it was squandered by trying to be too much for too long. If you know nothng about the game, imagine Hollow Knight meets Celeste but not as good as any of the inspirations. It prides itself in being a precision platformer Metroidvania, but the platforming is not as good as Celeste, and the combat is not as good as Hollow Knight. It tries to do both, and to a certain extent it succeeds, but it lacks the grace and expressiveness that each of its inspirations show in their respective subgenres.  Platforming here is not great because you don't have the same level of midair control or wall control that Madeleine has, and you frequently don't have visibility over your platforms. Screens here are huge, both vertically and horizontally, and while that's great for an exploration-based metroidvania, it's awful for one where you're suposed to land precisely on platforms that disappear after one second. So, so many of the puzzles require projectile control, which is also awful. I don't mind the idea behind the arrow and the teleport arrow, but they are not smooth to aim and to use, particularly when you have to use them repeatedly in the same platforming challenge. In Celeste, you could always see your whole screen so it was effortless to mentally map your route and execute it. Here you only see chunks of the challenge, have to pray that you'll land where you want with little mid-air control, and if you get it wrong enough times, you're sent back. Also, late game time trials are available and splinkered throughout the world, which makes extremely frustrating when you can't get a single bronze medal in any of them. Gate those things for NG+ or for late game, for Aeterna's sake, since this is not part of the main path which is already long enough as it is. Add the fact that Temple Trials are not only extremely punishing, but they are also required since many critical-path skills are locked behind them, and you have a game that's super punishing even in the easiest difficulty and even before getting to the optional content.  Platforming is so hard and punishing that I was pretty much forced to always have the gem that prevents you from losing `souls` when you die and the one that slows down time when shooting arrows, because without these two, progress is effectively impossible. Even with the `souls` gem, levelling up was incredibly, incredibly slow, gaining less than one skill point per hour usually, and with good upgrades locked behind 20 or 30 skill points. When you're forced to waste two of your gem slots just to stay alive and be able to cope with the normal platforming challenges, you're left with very little room to experiment with the remaining gems.  Then there's combat. It's not .... bad. It's just also not good. The dash doesn't truly connect, your jump usually is too short to avoid threats, the weapons you acquire throughout the game are mostly gimmicks, and your base damage after 20 hours of game is mostly the same as when you started .... it's just weird. Enemies become progressively more spongy and come in bigger packs with more tricky attack patterns, but it feels you don't become more skilled or more apt to deal with them, you just become more tanky and able to soak damage while trying to outdamage them. Hollow Knight excelled at boss fights. Boss fights here are, at best, good (never great), usually boring (basically outcompeting DPS), at worse grating like the Phoenix or the Underground Fortress AI, one of the most grating combat encounters I can remember facing on a Metroidvania.  And my last complaint is that the game refuses to end. It just can't take no for an answer. I'm 21 hours into the game, and at that point I was already on the final stretch of Hollow Knight and was already deep into Chapter 9 of Celeste. Here I'm barely over 50%, and I can clearly see there are huge areas in the map completely unexplored. Every area is bigger than it has to be, and require more backtracking than you would like. Fast travel is limited and usually forces you to keep redoing the same stretches over and over and over and over. Mapping is  a chore - if you miss the cartographer when you arrive at an area (highly likely as most areas have multiple entry points and branching paths very early on), then you're navigating blind for a good hour or so until you find him, if not more.  And everything I said is such a shame, because if the game just was a bit tighter, shorter, with faster progress through the upgrade tree, more of these platforming gauntlets placed after the credits roll or on optional late-game areas, this could potentially be one of the best metroidvanias I had every played. For my first 5 or 10 hours I thought this looked better, sounded better, was more interesting and more fun to play than Ori, for instance. The visuals are incredible, with beautiful areas, a lot of parallax, variety in the environments and enemies, and lots and lots of good visual effects. The piano track is haunting like the world we inhabit, but each area has its own energy and pace. The plot truly is interesting and go to some wild places when you less expect. It's a hauntind and beautiful world with intriguing lore.  But unfortunately, by trying to be too much for too long and failing to land its main hooks (combat and platforming), Aeterna becomes a chore that refuses to end, but also refuses to let you reach the ending through punishing platforming and unexpressive combat. Buyer beware.

I was ready to give this game a positive review. I liked the style, and the simplicity of it was honestly a little refreshing. I just can't recommend it due to the issues I saw.   First a technical bug: anytime I Alt-Tabbed out of the game I lost the use of my controller between levels unless I Alt-Tabbed again. Had to close out of the game and restart. Not a huge deal breaker, but super irritating when I just want to sit and game.  In the short time I've played I've wasted countless time spending 5-10mins going through a level only to get stuck cause I can't move forward till later in the game. That's not really a good style for this type of game. It feels like a waste of time, not "oh more things to come back to". It feels more like I got to an area I wasn't supposed to be in yet. It makes the playthrough feel unpolished.  The fighting is really less fun than it could be. It doesn't need to be the best ever, but a little more effort would make it so much better.   The game seems to drag in comparison to what they show in the videos. It just so far doesn't feel worth playing.

I've been playing on the original Noctis mode and most of the way through the game but I'm not having fun and the game feels like a chore to play that I'm dreading.  For a Metroidvania, it feels a bit soulless with enormous but samey maps that are the opposite of tight map design and at late game, are a complete nightmare to traverse - traps and platforming challenges everywhere with almost no breather. You cannot just go from A to B to get to places - you need to go through something like the White Palace just to traverse the map and often having to go back the same way with no shortcuts.  If you didn't like the red forest biome in Ender Magnolia, this game has something worse - the Dream Kingdom. Where it's not only a maze that's larger and where teleports aren't marked as to where they go, but you need to shoot truth arrows everywhere because the whole place is an illusion that obscures the real paths and obstacles. I'm currently avoiding this place because I hate it but it's important to the story and must be done.  Enemies feel like HP sponges and in some maps like The Cradle, there's very little contrast between the enemy and background making them hard to see. Bosses are also HP sponges. With contact damage and no dodge/parry to generate iFrames, some fights end up really frustrating and just feel unfair. You get a shield that can act like this but the recharge is 25s and needs you to invest gems that act like traits to make it far more usable which means you need to choose it over utility for your very limited slots. You need to waste precious damage slots on gems for utility that other games will give you as free upgrades to abilities that can be toggled.  There's also poor visual clarity for some platforms where it's hard to see where it's safe to stand on or because the background is too detailed and cluttered making it difficult to see (crystal mushroom rooms).  With how precise you need to be with the platforming, it's frustrating when it seems like inputs regularly don't seem to register, like jumps and teleport to arrow, or misfire like direction + dash.  The systems are worse than general Soulslikes where potions will reset at save points and seemingly designed to waste your time. If you used potions during a boss fight but don't win, you need to either farm more or backtrack to find the merchant to buy them so you may as well not use them. If you want to respec for a boss fight, you'll need to backtrack through platforming hell in a giant map to a throne/large save point and then go all the way back to the boss. Teleporting back to a save point is its own potion that you need to buy charges for to refill which seems pointless when most other games give you this as a free unlockable ability.  I end up with a headache and feeling exhausted after a session with little payoff. Story is very mid and QA for English script isn't great and there's a lot of bad grammar. Unless you enjoy platforming challenges for the sake of it, it's hard to recommend this compared to other Metroidvanias like Silksong, Nine Sols or Mio which are difficult but it feel rewarding when you overcome challenges. This game feels difficult for the sake of it but without the fun or reward, feels very padded and has a lot of mechanics that seem intended to waste your time.

This is probably one of the more tightly designed metroidvanias I've played. Compared to other popular titles like Nine Sols, I had complained there that the builds weren't relevant enough. Except the final boss, everything else could be handled with the basic setup. With Aeterna Noctis, switching out gems for different builds is actually useful and can make a significant difference.  For example, against the slime boss with constant adds, switching to gems that promote blood and convert blood to healing allow you to switch tactics from constantly dodging to just going wild hacking the boss to bits.  For the ghost knight, getting caught by his constant switchups is tricky, and, if you're too far for a teleport, you can easily get caught it the screen wide attacks. Switching your build to gems that promote quick shield recovery can give you an additional layer of insurance in case you get caught off guard.   The game is challenging. Bosses require practice to learn proper counters and optimal gem setup, and the puzzle platforming mixing air dashes, jumps and teleports can also take time pull off.   Overall, though, I do think its great. A tough, but fair challenge.

The platforming in this game is incredible, intricate, and difficult, and the controls are intuitive and responsive enough to make that consistently enjoyable. Music is gorgeous, art direction is beautiful but there's definitely more than a few locations that have so much going on visually that it's difficult to make out what's happening sometimes. The story is good and characters are enjoyable; the game never lets you forget that you're playing a game, and I think that's to its own benefit. Overall, this has been one of the best Metroidvania/precision platformer experiences I've had.   8.5/10, because the platforming and overall atmosphere is genuinely so good it makes the flaws feel irrelevant.

第五十一款通关的类银，体量很大，打完如释重负…要比喻一下的话，可以说是类银中的臭豆腐，爱的人很爱，讨厌的人估计开局直接劝退。本作其实类银浓度不是太高，而更像在蔚蓝里加了类银元素，因此，强烈推荐跳跳乐爱好者尝试一下。另外，本作通关难度中等，全收集难度较高，全成就难度极高，且强制多周目。建议选择noctis难度，大部分成就普通难度做不了。（都玩跳跳乐类银了真的不打算挑战一下吗）  在侃侃而谈之前，先说明一下本作有几个问题点，跳跳乐难度高暂且不论，这汉化有点瑕疵，有拼音先不说，连日文都出来了，然后操作方面，本作纯键盘无法通关，必须加鼠标；里面的怪物攻击只要起手式出来，即使你通过闪避或者跳跃穿到背后，也会秒转身打到你，顺便一提，刚开始的闪避不能穿怪，需要装备很后期的红宝石才能穿怪，cd4s约等于没用；然后本作闪避是不会取消攻击动作的，或者说你按攻击的时候按不出闪避？而当你的闪避奏效的时候，会一边挥剑一边向前冲，直接撞到怪物怀里；本作键位较多，改按键以后说明一栏的按键是不会改的，会造成较小影响；最后是本作bug不少，会有人物卡死情况，前期的墓地图会比较卡顿，这一点可以把帧率拉高解决（没想明白为什么高帧率会比低帧率还顺畅？）  好了，当你接受以上的问题，那么欢迎来到aeterna的世界！本作美术音乐世界观都是上乘，体量宏大，人物对话幽默，关键剧情甚至加了配音（虽然听起来怪怪的）而且探图方面，只要购买了地图，那就是实时更新，探到暂时拿不了的道具还会自动帮你标记，怕没探的地方漏收集也不用担心，还有情报地图可以助你一臂之力（不过有点贵，建议就龙岛，宇宙，梦境，天空岛这种散图买一下）传送点不多但有回城药剂比较方便，存档点很多，基本跳跳乐之前都会有，大可不必担心365里路。担心死亡惩罚过重？别怕，本作很前期就会给你一个死亡不掉魂的宝石，装上以后随便紫砂（顺便一提，死亡惩罚和空洞一样掉魂，关闭额外能量上限，但即使重新死在别的地方也不会刷新魂位置，只能自己回去捡或者招魂，但招魂扣经验还有不少钱）担心跳跳乐难度过高？也不用怕，本作自带的dlc诅咒深坑里有三段跳宝石，拿到光箭以后就能获取，加上不久之后能获得射箭时缓，难度大大降低（也许是刚出难度过高差评如潮所以加了三段跳吧，毕竟我实在很难想象没有三段跳通关的难度有多高）什么？你说不擅长战斗？也不用怕，中间天赋树点上去加上回能宝石约等于乌恩再世。最重要的一点是，当你完成本作全收集，大概也没几个类银有能难得住你的跳跳乐了。  地图设计一般般，整体还是比较线性，而且大部分跳跳乐到收集品终点是没有捷径回来，要硬跳回来（当然也可以紫砂传回来）更过分的是某些图甚至强制要多跑几趟。跳跳乐难度以及喜好各有说法，我就单独说说自己游玩感受吧，从开局到宇宙都是很棒的，能力一个个获取，性能一点点变强，地图也不停加入新机制，探索欲爆棚，即使是最反人类的宇宙，也算是很有创意了。但宇宙之后的天空岛和光明神殿就很一般了，中规中矩的没什么新意，和宇宙比起来就像奖励关，但比较无聊，合并一下都行，而支线帝皇封印4（应该是游戏最难的一段限时跳跳乐）借用朋友的话说就是某些没有打磨，没有设计，没有新意，为难而难，又臭又长，还有bug的跳跳乐设计。打完没什么成就感，只能说如释重负。还有一件事就是任务引导有些谜语人，压根不知道去哪，全靠群友口口相传了属于是。  然后战斗方面，大体上手感还不错，除了上面提到过闪避不取消攻击预输入，前面大部分boss还算可以，招招都有应对法，且攻略流程不一，但后期重量级boss有点粪了，点名帝皇，不仅跳跳乐狗屎，boss也粪的一批，基本没有输出空间还全是大屏攻击，频率还不低，此外，光明女王，史莱姆女皇，解脱束缚加波利弟全是个中高手。  最后，以上提到的难点粪点也许高手看来也还好，那恭喜，本作专为高手设计了一大堆成就还有挑战，正常打完游戏获得的成就还不到一半，剩下严苛的挑战专给m和高手。  总体而言，我玩的很开心，也希望热爱跳跳乐的你能享受这款四大名著之一。

“通关完恒夜就可以成为类银大手子！”实际上是跳跳乐大手子。。。 （全程困难通关，96.4%完成度，全收集过于繁琐故跳过） 这是一款优缺点及其明显的类银（甚至以至于有些偏离类银范畴），前期（完成度60%以前）的跑图、收集和战斗方面，跳跳乐难度适中，boss设计不算出众但不单调，说真的体验极好，让我回到了几年前玩空洞时的那种快感。 1.谈优点必须提到恒夜的美术设计，地图美术区域区分高，中世纪手绘风特别吸睛，并且敌人的动画流畅，看着确实舒服。世界观极为庞大，构筑的很精细，看得出来制作组是有野心的。并且主角会说话这一点也让rpg方面的代入感更强了，然而剧情故事方面比较短板了，还算有点狗血吧，但也没烂尾，给下一部留了叙事空间。 2.战斗手感较为上乘，技能多样但是有些冗杂，实际上战斗基本只会用到盾反平a和箭的组合，而且大部分boss掉的蓝很少，回血都不够更别提放技能，技能效果和伤害也比较鸡肋。然而宝石（护符）bulid还算中规中矩，前期基本就没有搭配空间，后期战斗就靠回血流硬抗就过去了，很轮椅。本体的体量也是量大管饱，接近全收集36h左右，全收集可能也才40h+； 3.地图设计是灾难级别的，探图80%都是毫无新意的跳刺玩法，并且大部分还要求你跳三遍及以上（初见两次，补收集一次）前期还好都是基础的跑酷。探图方面也没出现堆怪之类的恶意，存档点堆得确实多，并且大前期就能拿死亡不掉魂的宝石（基本就是没死亡惩罚），个人认为前中期的体验是最佳的。然后，从光塔开始，就全是制作者的恶意了。 关于地图的吐槽（带情绪，慎看）： 首先，它设计隐藏很恶趣味，它不会提示你这里你暂时过不过得去，也就是软锁（你没有必须能力也能过，但会更难），但是它会在终点加硬锁就让人很无语（“我tm费半天劲跳进来了你让我无功而返还要跳回去？！”） 然后是设计上的纯恶意：光塔的全屏激光，满满的光污染，毫无意义的设计。跳跳乐落点极限，并且限时，还在跳跳乐落点或者空中加一些随机移动的小怪，这点是普遍的毒点（深坑，深渊等等）。 然后是最大粪的地图，宇宙。角色参考系翻转，但是你的视角不反转，单纯的反直觉反操作设计，但他还要求你在逆天反转情况下快速用弓箭瞄准定点。你甚至需要跑四个星系，要在超级长，恶心并且在反复扭转你的视角的情况下限时完成（我不明白这种逆天挑战为什么塞主线）。 跳跳乐要难很简单，你设计得难（很考验反应，很考验抓时机和对于角色机制的理解），但不代表你设计的有趣，很多干扰项完全就是负反馈，没有存在得意义，更别提你的设计真的平庸，都是用降低容错（落点少，存档少）来限制玩家。 我只觉得这是设计者过于    傲慢    ，所以才会自信地把这样平庸而充满恶意的跳跳乐塞满地图。 4.最后是总评，我对恒夜的评价是：“被傲慢地图设计者毁掉的神级类银”，明明美术剧情还有战斗设计都很优秀（哪怕新意很少），但是遍地的跳跳乐和宇宙的反人类机制毁掉了明明可以和空洞奥日等拼一拼的机会。 这是一款优秀的游戏，但说是类银过于牵强了。 个人体验评分：7.2/10（扣分点在后期大量堆料的跳跳乐和无意义的探索） 推荐指数：2/10 虽然我确实被这所游戏深层的魅力所吸引，但恒夜的电波属性太强，所以我不会向其他人随意推荐这款游戏，因为它设计出来不是为了让玩家探索得开心，跳得开心，而是为了满足设计者的恶趣味和服从性测试。

5/10  The first half of the game is quite decent and resembles a budget version of Hollow Knight. It's fun to explore locations, and the platforming isn't too annoying. In short, it's okay.  But in the second half, the game becomes too twisted with excessively annoying difficulty, unnecessarily monstrous locations with endless enemies and inadequate platforming. Even conceptually cool ideas (such as Cosmos) only cause frustration. And the game breaks one of the basics of game design - difficulty should be followed by reward. But in the second half, there are no rewards for your sweat, everything is left in the first half. All that remains is suffering  Maybe it was just my problem, but I also constantly confused what was the background and what were the platforms you could stand on, what were the platforms that caused damage, and with all this environment the enemies also blend in. Very poor work with the color contrasts between backgrounds, foregrounds, and other entities.  Although I have to admit that from time to time I enjoyed the game, I can't recommend it to regular players. Only if you are a fan of the genre with a penchant for sadomasochism.

[i]Aeterna Noctis[/i] presents an intriguing story that gradually reveals what happened to its fallen kingdom and broken world. The narrative is engaging enough to keep you curious and motivated to move forward.  In terms of gameplay, this is easily one of the [b]hardest platforming experiences[/b] I’ve encountered in the metroidvania genre. The game demands extremely precise movement and mastery of your abilities. Some of these abilities can be quite difficult to control, especially when playing with a controller, which adds another layer of challenge.  As for boss fights, most of them feel fair and well-designed, offering a satisfying level of difficulty. However, there is one particular boss—who feels like Escanor from [i]The Seven Deadly Sins[/i]—that caused extreme frustration and took an unusually long time to overcome.  [b]Summary:[/b] I strongly recommend [i]Aeterna Noctis[/i] to fans of metroidvania and hardcore platforming games. However, if you don’t enjoy difficult platforming, this game may not be suitable for you. Overall, I give this metroidvania a solid [b]8.5 out of 10[/b].

Irgendwie bin ich aktuell - nach nur ca. 9 Std. (ich weiß, dass es durchaus lange gehen soll) -etwas unentschlossen. Ich sehe viele gute, teils sehr gute Ansätze, die aber oft nicht gut weitergedacht oder umgesetzt werden. Das kann sich noch ändern. Aber ich stelle schon eine klare Tendenz fest. Zunächst ist die Geschichte nicht unbedingt originell (und wird es auch nicht nach dem ersten kleinen Twist), motiviert aber grundlegend, die Dialoge sind ebenfalls ganz witzig. Die Spielwelt ist in ihrer Weitläufigkeit und relativen Offenheit interessant angelegt, man bekommt nichts einfach vorgesetzt, die Quests laden ebenfalls zur weiteren Exploration ein, da viele nicht auf die Schnelle zu lösen sind. Dieser Eindruck von spielerisch erfahrbarer Größe weiß zu beeindrucken, gleichzeitig weiß die Welt mit ihrer Größe nicht immer etwas anzufangen: Einige Abschnitte sind zu lang, zu repetitiv. Platforming-Passagen wirken auch teilweise gestreckt, wenig fokussiert. Die verlangte Präzision wird, v. a. durch den unnötig schwer zu cancelnden Dash, auch nicht immer ganz gut unterstützt, auch wenn das Platforming prinzipiell funktioniert. Die Belohnungen sind meist befriedigend, wenn es nicht gerade die x-te Truhe mit 500-1.500 Gold ist. Ich sehe schon, was man sich da für gehörige Summen zusammensuchen und sparen soll (u. a. Geheimwissen über Areale für mehrere 10.000 Gold), das hat also schon eine klare Funktion. Weniger, dafür größere Schätze wären aber reizvoller gewesen, zumal die Gegner auch Geld hinterlassen. Und Schätze, die am Ende nur Farming, also Lebenszeit, ersparen, sind eben mäßig befriedigend. Ich weiß auch noch gar nicht so recht, was einige der gefundenen Objekte bedeuten - nach über 8 Std.! Ich denke aber, hier ruht eine der Stärken des Spiels, das zunehmend dazu motiviert, nützliche Items zu finden, um die durchaus knackigen Herausforderungen (ich ahne schon, wohin es gehen wird) besser bewältigen zu können. Dass ich z. B. den Doppelsprung quasi nebenbei gefunden habe, noch dazu als ablegbares Ausrüstungsteil, nicht als substanzielle Fähigkeit, ist bezeichnend; und das finde ich nicht schlecht, hat mich vom Konzept her überrascht (und das schaffen Spiele, zumal Metriodvanias so häufig nicht). Das Kampfsystem ist ganz solide, aber Kontaktschaden ist für mich schon mal ein Minus, zumal das Spiel in keiner Hinsicht an die Eleganz eines Hollow Knight herankommt. Allein dass ich meine Spielfigur nach einem Treffer kurz nicht sehen kann, ist irritierend. Einige Hitboxen sind auch nicht gut gemacht. Die Reichweite der bisherigen Waffen lässt etwas zu wünschen übrig, aber das Arsenal wird sich noch erweitern. Die Bosse sind bislang ziemlich öde. Reine Gimmicks a la Triff-den-Schwachpunkt mit Ausweich-Passagen dazwischen (die beim ersten Boss auch noch unausgeglichen sind), der zweite Boss ruft dann noch regelmäßig ein Dutzend Adds, also alles, was man so antrifft, wenn Entwicklern nichts Interessantes einfällt. Sehr schade, vielleicht wird das noch besser. Dass keine Thron (also Checkpoints zum Anpassen der Ausrüstung) in direkter Nähe der Bosse war, ist dann noch so eine störende Kleinigkeit. Auch ästhetisch ist das Spiel ok, kann aber mit den Genregrößen - Hollow Knight, Ori, Nine Sols - nicht mithalten. Dafür ist die Optik zu generisch, der Sound zu wenig besonders, obwohl die Atmosphäre durchaus unterstützt wird. Vor- und Hintergründe sind auch nicht immer klar unterscheidbar: Stellenweise sehe ich Stacheln oder herumfliegende Partikel von Gegnern vor den zu stark beleuchteten, zu wenig kontrastierten Hintergründen nicht.   Momentan bin ich bei einer knappen Empfehlung: 5,5 / 10

I'm about 21 hrs in and my progression is at 60 % as of writing this review, i wanted to wait till i finish the game completely but seeing as i have unlocked all movement abilities and grasped the core gameplay i can for certain say that this is one of the most unique metrodvanias i have ever played, Artstyle is gorgeous, i don't know why devs chose to display such ♥♥♥♥♥♥ screenshots from the games, because game feels and looks absolutely amazing, each area sounds ambient, you can really feel the polish of ambience and Art style as soon as you drop in first zone of the game, there is a lot to love in cutscenes, art and music this game has to offer, This game is MASSIVE there aren't ridiculous amounts of zones like in comparison to silksong for instance, but each zone is huge, and when you think that you are done exploring them there are always more to find and look after for when you are backtracking them once you unlock abilities to access previously unreachable zones, or get sent back for certain quests, i think story is fine you might find it bit cliche at the start but characters you interact with and the main character as well have a lot of personality to them and you will grow to like them as you progress though the game, despite having flashed out combat with a lot of interchangeability this game feels more like a platformer though, and when i say that i absolutely MEAN it, i play on noctis difficulty from the start and platforming is super tight, i'm not sure if platforming becomes easier on lower difficulty but game always gives you an option to lower you difficulty anytime you want and vice versa, this is not a casual game, you will DIE ALOT mainly from falling when jumping from one place to another only to miss a platform by a small margin fall to your demise and get hit by projectile or contact damage on the way down and lose 2 heart containers, luckily this game has one of THE best respawn mechanics in a form of torches, basically there are alot of torches if you have played elden ring think of them as stakes of marika but instead of appearing only next to boss fights they are spread across every room in the game alot of times there are multiple of them in the same room, i think this is necessary for a game with insanely hard platforming as this and its not nearly as frustrating to die in this game as it is in any other metrodvania, this being said i do think platforming is insanely punishing, even for me its little bit too much, if you are not into platforming in general you will absolutely HATE this game, boos fights and combat are snappy, animations are clean and game gives you a lot of room to wiggle around in terms of customizing your character through trait tree (which is absolutely free to respec anytime you want) and gems also act like charms in hollow knight, all of the bosses i have encountered as of now look and were amazing, i have also killed some of them earlier than intended and game rewarded me in a form of steam achievements, speaking of which there are more than hundred of them, and if you want to 100 % the game with all the achievements, well good luck cause some of them are genuinely pain in the ass to do.  to close of the review I only have minor complaints to say at least, game is super polished but at time it just really isn't and its in the most weird places you would expect, like when you zone out from one room to another the invisible wall which transports your character from room to room sometimes is too close so your character gets stuck mid motion before the screen goes black, and other times its complete opposite the invisible wall is way to deep it so you have to keep running off screen to reach it, and i also think that arrow aiming is kinda trash, sometimes it doesn't aim where i want it to go and even on the mouse and keyboard its not as responsive as i would like it to be, i cannot imagine how can you play this game on controller.  With that being said i think if you are metrodvania fan its a must play, if you are a bit on the casual side you might want to sit this one out but for everybody else i wholeheartedly recommend this game, needless to say i'm excited to see whats to come and i most definitely looking forward to see more of world of Aeterna, and eventually to 100 % it in the end!

The world is amazing, some biomes look amazing and the story is amazing. But I just cannot recommend it. The first half was some off the most fun I had, but this game is brutal, and this comes as someone who finished the C sites from celeste so I had parkouring experience. The combat and exploring is "normal" difficulty but the parkouring feels frustrating. You will not even unlock shortcuts to skip tedious parkour sections so traversal feels slow, even late game. The game even focuses on these demanding platforming sections. The game is furthermore riddled with small little problems that should not be in there. This all adds up to a frustrating experience where playing the game is not really encouraging. I cannot recommend this, only to hardcore platformer enthusiasts. I think the developers were a bit too ambitious with this game. (I stopped playing at the planets since the reversed controls were too frustrating for me).

A sprawling Metroidvania with fantastic platforming and diverse, original biomes, Aeterna Noctis had me hooked for from the jump. My only gripe was the endgame felt a bit meandering, I assume due to the bloat that the post-release DLC added to the game. I simply tracked down a guide and focused on the main path to see this one through.

永恒之夜正式通关了，这游戏感觉还是应该分到跳跳乐游戏里，整个游戏充斥着各种难度不低的跳跳乐。我来几句话总结一下优缺点。  优点：体量是真的大，50h通关还未全收集。美术风格还不错，不同地图的场景都各具特色，特征色彩都不尽相同，彼此间很分明。从暗灰死青的阴森墓地，到金光闪耀的黄金高塔，还有蓝色的光明摇篮，紫色的结晶洞穴，黑暗血红的深渊。这其中又尤以宇宙这张地图的美术最为惊艳出色，配合由天体铺排而成的关卡，颇有身临其境之感。除此之外，作为跳跳乐为大头的游戏，跳跳乐的关卡设计我觉得还是比较优秀的，而且种类繁多，有着解谜向、追逐战、较简单的风景段等等关卡。宇宙这整个关卡的设计理念也足以让人眼前一亮。 最后战斗方面，角色的数值很高，真的很高，配装合理的情况下后期boss都能换血打死——你的性能有些一般，但是你无脑的数值又弥补了这一点——我觉得这还蛮好的。而且还可以无偿洗点，扩展玩法。  缺点：操作手感不好，手柄按键分配不好，以及地图陷阱的碰撞箱有点蜜汁判定的味道——有的时候撞刺上有种被吸上去的感觉——这几个缺点导致有些比较难的位置跳起来体验会有点差。 引导巨jb差，这游戏的跳跳乐难度除了宇宙的几段和帝皇封印4，其实都没这么难，感觉上手困难其实主要是这b游戏的关卡引导做的像shit（应该说是完全没有），每次上手新能力都没有教学关，手感和能力特性全靠玩家自己悟，另外难度曲线又不平滑，体验会好就怪了。而且这游戏还把一些极大影响游戏体验和跳跳乐手感的东西拆分到装备护符里，装不装角色性能都是两个水平。 大世界大地图，但是地图给的信息非常有限，而且除去跳跳乐的关卡，你会发现这个地图很空，有很多地方缺乏填充。 宝石系统缺乏深度，很多宝石只提供了点数值上的强化，收集了半天最后发现好用的宝石就那么几个，导致我后期都没什么收集欲望。 Boss设计并不算很好，在玩家数值膨胀的后期，boss也是不要命一般的狂加弹幕和全屏攻击，看的我眼花缭乱，完全没有拆招的兴趣，真差不多得了。 最后还要吐槽一下剧情，男主的人设和性格我还挺喜欢的，但是这个主线到最后我还是没搞懂是在干什么，游戏里还有好多神棍一样的npc，故弄玄虚般叭叭了一大堆，只是让我感觉更迷惑。总之，开局搭建的世界观让我挺感兴趣，但是世界观下的具体故事写得像一拖四，结局打完我一句“就这？”。有点虎头蛇尾了。  总评：8/10，难度不低的瑕疵之作，但是瑕不掩瑜，而且量大管饱，打折这价格更是要啥自行车，喜欢跳跳乐的无脑入。

I can't even right now... I spent 3 hours with then Eye of the Beholder trying to get a no hit run.  I then give up on that, remove the 1 hit kill gem, then immediately beat the boss without getting hit.  .................... happy ending to that I guess.     This game is big for a metroidvania.   I enjoyed running around, but at times felt some areas seemed too large.  I'd rather have them be like this though.  Enjoy the vast world.  Got across the first part of the map and realized I wasn't even halfway.    The platforming: Very satisfying.  You will lose a lot, but you just get better and better with the instant retries.  Most of the time playing this game will be jumping from one spot to another without getting hit.   Glitch? After getting stuck in the catacombs and not able to get the soul back with one more blood heal left, I decided to go to the soul guy to buy the soul back.  I had 96% EXP (about 45,000 EXP at the time) in the soul, but when I sold/bought it back, it was empty.  Why?  Then later I purposely lost all of the health twice to have the soul disappear after losing too much.  But the soul stayed in the same spot and it had all of the EXP still there.  So why bother have the Soul Guy to buy/sell the soul?!  Worst character in the game.    Story: It's ok.  My holiday break has been playing this game and beating all of the bosses with no hit runs.  I have 4 bosses left.  I feel unmotivated to beat them with no hits.  This game is easy if I allow them to hit me.  Weird how easy it is to get good at these kinds of games when practicing on the harder difficulties.   Also, do not start with normal mode.  Go to Noctis.

tldr: If you enjoyed Hollow Knight (especially the white palace) and Celeste chances are you'll like this game a bit. I'd say it's the second most fun I've had with a metroidvania. Really just give it a try, pick it up on sale if you're not confident.  I and I'm sure many others have heard a multitude of things going into this game. The majority of which are about the difficulty so I'd like to speak on that first.   If I were to guess I think many people (and reviewers) played the game on noctis mode; I played on the normal difficulty mode. The normal difficulty if I were to ballpark it falls somewhere around the base game of Celeste and Hollow Knight, though I'd say it's easier than both (depending on build and playstyle). This was no problem for me, while I wish the platforming was more difficult on normal mode it was still extremely engaging and I enjoyed every moment of it. As for combat; against regular enemies it is not all too engaging (bar some gauntlets and late game) and I'd say the bosses range from okay to great.   From what I have seen and heard on Noctis difficulty that is a different story; the game is quite a bit more difficult.  I don't have as much time for games as I'd like to and with that I would say normal difficulty absolutely respected my time. I rarely found myself irritated or annoyed playing through the game even when I was dying. In that sense it was a great level of difficulty. If I were to nitpick I do wish there was a difficulty option that used the noctis platforming difficulty and retained the normal combat difficulty, but I absolutely understand why that is not a feature. I just found the platforming to be the strongest element of this game.  I've seen others complain about a particular ability you get about halfway through the game. Yes mechanics are to each's own but I absolutely loved it. The flow state you can get into with the ability is amazing and mastery of it is extremely rewarding in traversal, platforming, and combat.  Anywho onto more general stuff:   The story is fine. It was interesting enough to keep me going but not so interesting as to consume me or anything of the sort.  The art is pretty great. All hand drawn style and there are some absolutely gorgeous locations that I could just sit at.  The music / soundtrack is fantastic and creates a great atmosphere.  The map is absolutely massive and one of my favorite video game worlds / maps I've come across. I've not felt myself compelled to explore a map to this extent since I played Hollow Knight for the first time.  There is a fair bit of build variety.  Bosses are varied and unique, no two really feel the same. Some are mechanically intense fights and others are gimmicks, both are usually fun. There is also a chase sequence or two that are fantastic.  There are ui elements that keep track of a lot of important stuff that makes playing and keeping track things easy. Stuff like x number of collectable or the quest log and much more.  Speaking of quests they were mostly pretty great. I did not end up finishing every one of them, but I did do most and none of them felt like a chore.  It really does feel like Aeterna Noctis tried to innovate in quite a few ways. Whether it be some of the upgrades it offers the players. My favorite implementation of backtracking in any metroidvania. The build variety. The emphasis on platforming and the mechanics that come with it. Some of the gimmick bosses. The checkpoint system. Those are just some of the ways I feel like the game tried to push things forward, but it also retains much of that great metroidvania core and elements from past titles that people loved.  Honestly most of my gripes with the game are small things. It does lack polish in some places. That UI I mentioned could be more clear. Feedback upon hitting things could be much better. Visual clarity in certain spots could be improved. The map guy somehow making it everywhere in this world feels very very wrong from a immersion perspective. Most of it is small stuff like that.  When it comes down to it though I look back on my playthrough with 90% positive thoughts. I enjoyed my playthrough more than I had expected.

Really good game, one I would call a "flawed masterpiece." Inspired by Hollow Knight, it distinguishes itself with unique systems and level designs, and an attention to detail regarding QOL. I especially appreciate frequent area checkpoints and the ability to promptly retry boss fights. You earn both currency and XP from enemies, but only XP is lost on death (unless you have a certain item equipped that eliminates corpse runs entirely).  Platforming and combat feel great, though some enemies can be frustrating (e.g. ones that explode after you kill them). Bosses are [i]mostly[/i] great, though some exhibit rather suffocating attack patterns that may feel unfair as you struggle to figure out a strategy. Most areas are enjoyable to navigate and explore, though a couple areas are better in concept than in execution (e.g. the mind-melting yet tedious [spoiler]Dream Kingdom[/spoiler]).  It's definitely a tough game but there are lots of abilities, items and upgrades that make every challenge more approachable. I think this is a bit of an underrated classic of the metroidvania genre and I'm greatly looking forward to the sequel next year.

Обзор Aeterna Noctis: сюжет, геймплей и особенности Сюжетная завязка  В центре повествования — Король Тьмы, втянутый в бесконечный цикл сражений с Королевой Света. Их противостояние — не просто битва добра со злом: оба героя устали от вечной войны, периодически пытаясь найти путь к миру. Однако судьба неизменно возвращает их на поле боя.  История затрагивает темы:  изнуряющего повторения одних и тех же событий; тщетных попыток вырваться из предопределённого цикла; внутреннего конфликта персонажей, осознающих бессмысленность борьбы. В финале игрок узнаёт, удастся ли Королю Тьмы разорвать этот круг страданий. В будущем ожидается сиквел — Aeterna Lucis (название отсылает к противостоянию света и тьмы: noctis — ночь, lucis — свет).  Геймплей: ключевые механики  Платформинг и ловушки Игра насыщена сложными прыжковыми секциями с шипами, циркулярными пилами, опасными жидкостями и другими препятствиями. Многие зоны требуют точного тайминга и запоминания паттернов. Система контрольных точек В отличие от многих хардкорных метроидваний (например, Hollow Knight), здесь: автоматические чекпойнты (фонари) расположены часто; после смерти персонаж возрождается в 10–15 метрах от места гибели; нет необходимости преодолевать длинные дистанции заново. Боссы Каждый требует изучения паттернов атак (напоминает босса‑дерево из Dark Souls III). После поражения игрок появляется прямо перед противником. Некоторые битвы сочетают платформенные элементы и тактические решения. Прокачка и билды Амулеты дают уникальные способности: тройной прыжок, замедление времени, телепортацию через стрелы, сохранение опыта при смерти. Древо навыков включает полезные умения (около половины веток), позволяющие создавать специализации: мечник (агрессивный ближний бой); лучник (дистанционные атаки); танк (выживание и защита). Система опыта напоминает Sekiro: при смерти теряется накопленный прогресс, который нужно вернуть. Исследование мира  Масштабная карта с 16 разнообразными регионами: от фэнтезийных ландшафтов и склепов до подводного корабля и инверсированного космоса с гравитационными аномалиями. Уникальные механики передвижения: телепортация через стрелы (ключевой элемент платформинга во второй половине игры); система тронов для быстрого перемещения и прокачки. Награды за исследование: амулеты и снаряжение за сбор осколков (каждые 5–7 штук); деньги за нахождение скрытых объектов (например, 12 компьютеров); музыкальные ноты для игры на фортепиано в склепе. Визуал и атмосфера  Ручная рисовка создаёт эффект «ожившего аниме» в стиле Castlevania от Netflix. Стилистическое разнообразие: кат‑сцены в технике мозаики; контрастные локации (мрачные дождевые пейзажи, яркие фрески дворцов, неоновые подводные станции). Музыкальное сопровождение — меланхоличные, гнетущие мелодии, отражающие усталость главного героя. Особая фишка — режим импровизации на фортепиано с 7 тональностями и диезами. Сильные стороны игры  Баланс сложности: низкая цена ошибки благодаря чекпойнтам; постепенное освоение механик (от базовых прыжков до сложных телепортаций). Глубина прокачки: гибкие билды через амулеты и древо навыков; возможность комбинировать стили (например, лучник/танк). Нарратив: эмоциональная история о вечной борьбе; саркастичные диалоги героя, подчёркивающие абсурдность цикла. Детализация мира: уникальные локации с собственной эстетикой; скрытые квесты и коллекционные предметы. Потенциальные сложности  Высокая требовательность к точности в платформенных секциях. Необходимость многократных попыток для освоения сложных зон. Объём контента — игра рассчитана на десятки часов прохождения. Итог  Aeterna Noctis — это:  продуманная метроидвания с гигантским миром и вариативной прокачкой; история о вечном противостоянии, поданная через призму усталости героев; сочетание хардкорного платформинга с удобными механиками (чекпойнты, быстрое возрождение). Игра рекомендуется фанатам жанра, готовым к сложным, но справедливым испытаниям. Демоверсия доступна в Steam, а в будущем ожидается продолжение — Aeterna Lucis.
Карта сводит сума, игра по ощущениям слишком затянута. Платформинг и музыка хороши кучи отсылок на популярную культуру от лавкрафта до масс эфекта и вархаммера.

Some executions were very clumsy. The list includes:  1) The camera doesn't follow you in some straightforward platforming sections. 2) The combat against enemies in biomes is not the best. 3) The art style choices can legitimately be hard on the eyes. The audio balancing can also hurt your eardrums for no reason. 4)The quests are annoying because you can encounter the room where the quest objective is. However, if you haven't activated the quest beforehand, the room will be empty. 5)The rebind button screen is hard to read and annoying. It would have been easier if your cursor were more visible.   Despite the above complaints and nitpicks, I had a lot of fun exploring the areas because the varied items and progression system prevent the gameplay loop from becoming stale. The varied weapons are fun to use, and the boss fights are mostly great. The biomes are of a high quality, with some standouts that have unique mechanics.   If you love old-school 2D platformers, you won't be disappointed trying out the original Noctis mode.

Aeterna Noctis is a non-linear Metroidvania with good combat mechanics, challenging platforming, and RPG-like leveling and skill assignment. It runs on the Steam Deck, but due to performance limitations, I would play it on a more robust system with an input device with good response times.

Aeterna Noctis - игра, которая умудряется одновременно быть длинной, скучной и раздражающей. Это тот редкий случай, когда игра превращается в пытку сразу после запуска. Такое впечатление, что разработчики задумали сделать метроидванию мечты но что-то пошло не так  В итоге перед нами - бесконечная фабрика боли, уныния и прыжков по платформам, которые повторяются тысячу раз  Минусы: 1. Игра, которая давит. Всегда. Везде. По всем линиям фронта.  Aeterna Noctis - это когда ты заходишь развлечься, а выходишь с ощущением, будто разгрузил два вагона цемента. Усталость не приятная, как после сложной Souls-игры, а тупая, выматывающая. Ты не проходишь игру - ты отрабатываешь смену.  2. Геймплей однообразный до состояния комы  Прыжок. Удар. Прыжок. Ловушка. Всё. И так 30+ часов. Разработчики, похоже, решили, что если менять фон и текстуры, то игрок не заметит, что он весь этот путь делает одно и то же. Но замечаешь. И очень быстро.  3. Сюжет уровня “мама сказала, что красиво”  Сюжет? Сюжет в игре - это набор штампов, слепленных как попало. Такой текст можно написать, если дать ребёнку задание: “Придумай тёмное фэнтези, только быстро”. Пытались в ДС, аля никаких подсказок ищите сами, в итоге только добавили духоты(  4. Управление мстит тебе лично  Геймпад исправен, руки на месте - а игра живёт по своим законам физики. Нажал вовремя? Молодец. Но сработает ли? Кто знает! Лотерея. А в платформере это равносильно издевательсту.  5. Марафон по пустоте  Мир огромный… и мёртвый. Как будто его делали, чтобы потянуть время и заставить тебя бегать ради беготни. Это не исследование мира - это экскурсия по пустым локациям длиной в п*здалион.  6. Невидимость платформ как фича  Иногда передний и задний план так сливаются, что прыгаешь вслепую. Ты просто не понимаешь что происходит на экране.  7. Финальный босс - контрольный выстрел  Финальный босс - это уже не испытание, а издевательство. Три часа ковыряния, после которых меня хватило лишь на одно: включить трейнер, прикончить босса, закрыть эту страницу жизни и больше никогда туда не возвращаться. Забавно, что с трейнером я получил в первый раз удовольствие от игры. Без него - только ярость, ненависть и неприязнь.  Плюсы (да, они есть): 1. Боссы действительно неплохие… пока не начинают раздражать  Идеи интересные, бои местами стильные. Но всё это тонет в общем болоте однообразия. Радость короткая, как отпуск.  2. Иногда встречаются нормальные платформенные моменты  Из разряда “ого, это было круто”. Правда, такие моменты встречаются так редко, что их можно перепутать с галлюцинацией от усталости.  Итог:  Aeterna Noctis - это красивая упаковка, внутри которой лежит длинное, душное, однообразное разочарование. Если бы игра была чуть короче, чуть разнообразнее и хотя бы немного дружелюбнее — был бы шанс. Но сейчас это путешествие, которое проще забыть, чем кому-то советовать.  Оценка: 3.5/10 - советую только тем кто любит побаловать себя фикалиями, для других игра не предназначена.
