Infelizmente um dos piores metroidvanias que já joguei, combate muito fraco, com chefes exageradamente dificeis, onde os mesmo para simular uma dificuldade spaman ataques pela tela inteira, sem criatividade nenhuma, o plataforma é legal, porém as hitboxes são um lixo, o personagem é muito longo para este estilo de jogo, a arte é bonita, mas a direção de arte junto com o game design falharam muito, pois os inimigos em sua maioria são da mesma cor do cenário de fundo, a trilha é boa, em fim um jogo muito frustante e repetitivo, vou zerar só por zerar mesmo, mas já deixei de me divertir com ele a muito tempo.

[h1] 于是，我把这个混账游戏全收集了 [/h1]  玩永恒之夜的动机其实是我把锈苔dlc打完了，然后自认为没啥银河城的跳跳乐能让我破防了，正好群友又一直在给我推，于是决定挑战一下。 结果是这游戏就成了我玩的银河城里最难最混账之一了。  省流：量大管饱，跳跳乐水平优秀，但是跳跳乐极多，还有逆天地图，非跳跳乐高手不建议玩困难模式。  说它难，倒也不算太难，个人感觉它所有关卡的难度其实都还没红丝绒高，更何况它有个三段跳和传送箭时缓的宝石，第一次带时缓宝石去跳跳乐的时候我一直在笑，然后感慨以前过的什么苦日子啊。 但它还是很难，如果一般的银河城玩家想不开来玩的话，真的建议不要在乎成就直接开普通模式（毕竟恒夜有每个boss的无伤成就，有竞速成就，有付费dlc成就，正常来说困难模式通关也拿不到一半的成就），因为打简单模式都有可能会破防。  这个游戏前期的跳跳乐虽然也不少，但在有跳跳乐基础的情况下算不上很难，而当你拿到传送箭离开西墙后，才是出新手村了。  中期的每一张地图都是重量寄。以下会包含一部分的关卡剧透，但我觉得这玩意剧透了也没什么。  最简单的梦境，核心设计是隐形墙，你看到的墙和地刺不一定是真的墙和地刺，而你要想分辨出路在哪只能用传送箭，传送箭会有一个小范围揭示真假的效果。并且梦境的房间的入口和出口并非在地图上是联通的，你进一个房间右下角的门可能会从左上角出来，所以每个房间的联通关系只能自己试出来，那我为什么说这个地方简单呢，因为它本身的设计更接近于解谜，所以没有过于逆天的跳跳乐，和后面几个图比起来算是纯良的。 然后是海底要塞和深渊，海底要塞简单一点，抛开跳跳乐不谈，它最大的特点是有岔路口，其中一条路会给你能推进主线的道具，而另一条路只有漫长的跳跳乐和尽头的收集品，以及一个你第一次来大概率拿不到的主线道具，因为你要先拿前面那个主线道具然后开启机关后才能回来拿这个主线道具，而且这段的跳跳乐比较长，这就导致了如果你想全收集至少得跳两边这里。 深渊要更难一点，而在深渊你可以拿到最后一个能力，又称黑暗国王在黑暗中会被限制能力，具体说来就是没这个能力你碰到黑暗区域会受伤，有这个能力你可以进黑暗区域了，但是在里面没法使用传送箭。显然，这种限制就是为后面更难的跳跳乐所用的。 接下来是宇宙，这个图是整个游戏里最恶心的一张图，难倒不难，但是有个混账的反重力设计。一般的游戏设计重力反转，要么是你视角转操作不转，要么是视角不转操作转，只有你恒夜，是视角不转操作也不转。这也就意味着当你站在星球上时，角色的上下左右和操作的上下左右是不一样的，这就导致了宇宙跑起来及其反直觉，而且四元素的收集得一遍跳跳乐一遍克服它的反直觉，甚至最恶心的地方要你绕整整两圈，还限制传送箭（哈哈，这就是前面深渊给的能力啊），在你推完这张图的主线后，恭喜你，还有场逃脱战等着你呢。我的两位群友都是在这个b图破防退坑的。 大后期的两张图没什么好讲的，纯毕业考试，在前面的基础上虽然没有过于逆天设计，但是综合性更强，难度更高。  关于它银河城方面的地图设计，以我个人的感受来说算不上是很好，虽然它有很多的收集品，但它的地图更大，这导致了在没有跳跳乐的地方就显得很空，但是它跳跳乐多啊，九成的地图都是跳跳乐，尽管剩下一成会显得又大又空，但是绝大多数时间玩家还是能够激情跳跳乐的。  当然，这个b游戏我还是想推荐的，一是本着苦不能只有我吃的伟大精神，我还是希望更多人来玩然后破防；二是这个游戏跳跳乐质量是真的好还量大管饱，除了宇宙以外我几乎没什么怨言了，绝大多数时候跳跳乐都是又痛苦又爽的。

[h1][b]Uma Jornada Entre Luz e Sombra Onde Somente os Determinados Avançam[/b][/h1]  Há mundos que se abrem diante do jogador como histórias a serem lidas; [b]Aeterna Noctis[/b] se ergue como uma epopeia destinada a ser vivida. Em um universo onde Luz e Trevas travam uma guerra eterna, você encarna o [b]Rei das Trevas[/b], um soberano amaldiçoado a vagar por eras infinitas sem descanso, sem paz e sem vitória. Sua única missão? Quebrar o ciclo. Reconquistar seu trono. E restaurar o equilíbrio em um mundo que pulsa entre o esplendor e o caos. [b]Aeterna Noctis[/b] não é apenas um metroidvania. É um [b]poema sombrio[/b], um mundo esculpido com amor artesanal, um desafio forjado na paciência, na habilidade e na persistência. [hr][/hr] [h3][b]Um Reino Que Chama por Seu Senhor, Mesmo Que Queira Sua Morte[/b][/h3] Logo nos primeiros minutos, o jogo deixa claro: não haverá piedade. As terras de Aeterna são vastas, perigosas e cheias de mistérios. Cidades caídas, templos esquecidos, florestas vivas e cavernas ancestrais — todos os cenários são pintados com uma arte 2D deslumbrante, rica em personalidade e atmosfera. Cada área é um pedaço do mundo que conta sua própria história, repleta de personagens melancólicos, seres corrompidos e criaturas majestosas que tanto guiam quanto testam você. Não há caminho seguro. Cada sala é uma prova. Cada plataforma, uma ameaça. Cada inimigo, um lembrete do peso da coroa que você carrega. [hr][/hr] [h3][b]Ação, Precisão e A Morte Como Companhia Constante[/b][/h3] O coração de [b]Aeterna Noctis[/b] pulsa no combate — ágil, técnico e veloz. Espadas, habilidades especiais, esquivas e ataques mágicos formam um ritmo que exige reflexos e domínio. Não basta apertar botões: é preciso [b]dançar[/b] com o perigo. Mas o verdadeiro charme — e tormento — do jogo está nas plataformas. Saltos milimétricos, armadilhas cruéis, obstáculos que exigem absoluto controle mecânico… e é aí que o diferencial brilha: [hr][/hr] [h3][b]Dois Modos, Duas Experiências – Uma Mesma Jornada Épica[/b][/h3] O jogo oferece dois modos completamente distintos, moldando a aventura ao gosto (ou coragem) do jogador: 🔹 [b]Modo Aeterna – O Equilíbrio da Ação e Exploração[/b]Este é o modo tradicional, uma experiência metroidvania clássica: mapa amplo, inimigos desafiadores, puzzles inteligentes e plataformas que exigem habilidade, mas com uma curva de dificuldade equilibrada. É a jornada heroica ideal para jogadores que querem explorar tudo sem quebrar o controle ao meio. 🔹 [b]Modo Noctis – Onde Apenas os Mais Bravos Sobrevivem[/b]A cereja amarga do bolo.  O [b]modo Noctis[/b] transforma o jogo em um inferno meticuloso e fascinante de plataformas de precisão — algo que o gênero metroidvania raramente ousou tocar.  Aqui, [b]cada salto é uma sentença[/b], cada armadilha é fatal, e cada vitória é conquistada com suor e orgulho. Um modo feito para aqueles que amam [i]dominar[/i] o impossível, que buscam desafios onde poucos sequer tentariam entrar. Se Hollow Knight testa sua paciência, se Celeste exige precisão… Noctis faz você unir as duas coisas ao extremo. [hr][/hr] [h3][b]Exploração Que Recompensa os Curiosos e Humilha os Impacientes[/b][/h3] O mapa é gigantesco, interconectado e cheio de segredos. Passagens escondidas, atalhos engenhosos, upgrades espalhados por recantos sombrios — [b]Aeterna Noctis é um convite constante à curiosidade[/b]. Foi construído para ser explorado com cuidado, com atenção e com amor aos detalhes. Nada está ali por acaso. Nada é entregue de graça. [hr][/hr] [h3][b]Uma Trilha Sonora que Respira Junto com o Mundo[/b][/h3] A música desempenha um papel quase ritualístico no jogo. Melodias épicas, outras tristes, algumas misteriosas… todas se encaixam perfeitamente nas regiões que você percorre. Algumas trilhas são tão imersivas que parecem ecoar a própria luta interna do Rei das Trevas — preso entre seu passado, sua maldição e o fardo do destino que carrega. [hr][/hr] [h3][b]Um Metroidvania Artesanal e Impiedoso[/b][/h3] Cada pixel, cada golpe, cada armadilha…  Tudo em [b]Aeterna Noctis[/b] foi criado com uma precisão tão apaixonada que é impossível não sentir o peso artístico e emocional que os desenvolvedores colocaram ali. Não é um jogo feito para agradar “todo mundo”.  É feito para [b]marcar[/b] quem se aventura em sua escuridão. [hr][/hr]

I don’t care for the game mainly for two reasons: its world-building and its writing. The world feels like a collection of loosely connected themed areas rather than a cohesive whole. After playing games like Nine Sols or Hollow Knight, the difference is night and day.  The writing, on the other hand, is downright dreadful—it feels like something penned by a teenager. Take the Goblin King side quest, for instance: it’s utterly ridiculous. The main character tries to act tough and dismissive, telling the King off for his request, yet still ends up doing it anyway, coming back with childish excuses—it’s just absurd. I can’t stomach it.  I’ve seen Aeterna Noctis praised for its brutal, high-skill platforming, and it certainly demands precision and patience. But I’ve never been one for pure platforming—without a strong game built around it, there’s little reason to push through.

I opt to pick `No` since this game is not for me, but it still might be for you, below are the reason why i pick no  This game started off fine for me and actually feels good after i got dash (very early on), double jump, and wall jump. But then after i got the arrow abilities i notice that we can do 360 degree ranged attack and when i research it turns out the game will go heavily on this 360 degree mechanic later in the game and as someone who only use keyboard for metroidvanias (i suck at using controllers and using mouse on 2D platformer hurt my wrist pretty quickly) i resign to the fact that i might not finish this game, but that's fine i could just try to push as far as i could and stop when the 360 degree mechanic become too much.  But that's NOT why i give it a `No`.  In Metroidvania usually there 3 kind of challenges (Gauntlet, Platforming, Boss), this game have those. The Gauntlet in this game is fine but the Platforming (like specific platforming challenges) and the Bosses most of the time dictates your moves, you have to react to the challenge specifically with little to no room for variation, i thought it will be a one off but each boss i fought have been like that and given how spongy the bosses can be fighting them is kind of tedious. There is a boss that i thought is more lenient but when i try to hit him i got damaged despite no change in visual apart from their pose, but their hitbox feels like its expanding i couldn't even get near at all without getting contact damage.  That's it, i guess it's on me expecting this game to be combat focus despite it being platforming focus. This game is Platforming Focus, so keep that in mind.

[h3] The positives [/h3] Nice graphics (even if the character designs are kinda ugly), the main character is charming (he is sassy), great music and the controls are responsive for most part (even if some pixel perfect sections can be frustrating, but you can change the difficulty on the fly), very decent story and world building for a metroidvania, the map of Aeterna looks like a proper “place” (the areas are connected in a feasible way, it’s not like other metroidvanias that links a forest with a volcano out of nowhere)  [h3] The negatives [/h3] Some sections struggles a lot with visual clarity, just too much clutter, similar colors, etc.. The combat is pretty basic and shallow (this game is all about platforming), a couple of side quests are obnoxious with their requirements (like the celestial dragon ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥), the teleports (save points) remains too spaced out for such a huge map, the gibberish dialogues not always sound charismatic (the protagonist sounds great tho, so they got it right the one that need it the most). No “loadouts”, so you have to rework the skill tree multiple times, I think any game that uses the skill tree gimmick (or even the “charms” gimmick of Paper Mario that got popularized by Hollow Knight), you have to add loadouts as QoL. And for metroidvanias in general, the “map printscreen” of Prince of Persia Lost Crown should become the standard, the map navigation in Aeterna Noctis is decent enough, but more QoL is always welcomed   [h3] Conclusion [/h3]  Great game, a shame it overstays its welcome a little bit, also lack of polish at release. Finishing the latest 3.1 version, you can cheese a lot with the (almost) infinite heals and double (then triple) jumps early, also change the platforming difficulty on the fly, so I guess most people can finish it nowadays without too much of a hassle. For platforming fans, this game is a must-have, it fuses the precision platforming formula with actual form and purpose (the metroidvania elements). Hope more people will give it a chance to the upcoming Aeterna Lucis, this series deserves more love. Overall, 7.5 out of 10. Niched "precision platformer" metroidvania, well-made, I think every metroidvania fan should give it a chance, try something different with a unique personality

I finished the main game at 100% on Noctis mode, with the only thing left to do being the DLC content.  Aeterna Noctis is platforming focused. And in that area it is a 10/10. You have to enjoy hard platforming to get the most out of the game. You could play on Aeterna mode, but the game's brilliance is in its hard, sometimes bonkers hard (mind boggling even) platforming. Immensely satisfying and difficult. One area in particular is amazing for just how challenging it is, and beautiful also.   Gameplay felt smooth and responsive. I died a lot, but it always felt like my fault. When you start unlocking all your movement abilities, it's just great. Same with combat related abilities. I will say at first the combat and movement felt a bit underwhelming, but again, skills, abilities, it comes together nicely.  Bosses look great, with lots of variation and great design. I'm not a huge fan of boss fights in general, but these were fun and well made. What I liked most about them is that they're not long, drawn out battles of tedium. They might seem hard at first but there always seemed to be an optimal build and strategy that makes them extremely manageable (and fun). What a lot of people say is the hardest boss in the game, I got on my third try, solely because of the right build and strategy. Best part is no tedious runbacks to the boss, something I really appreciated after playing Silksong.  Builds go from ranged, to health and tanking, to melee and crits, to a mixture of all three, and then all the gems on top of that. You can have builds that help with platforming, builds that are great for exploration and combat. Respec is completely free anytime you're at a throne, so no issues playing around.   The game looked good to me. As an older person with poor eyesight, I never felt that the design got in the way of gameplay. It looked beautiful, with lots of variation in areas. I hardly ever had an issue with clutter or distinguishing elements, no more than literally any other game i've played. It was a non-issue.  Other things I liked  Music is great, no problems.  The corpse runback is here, but it's not punishing or annoying. You don't lose your money, just your ability to earn XP until you get your corpse back. And you get as many tries as you want to do that, or you can buy it back.  Backtracking isn't terrible. There's plenty of teleportation options, even a vial that lets you teleport back whenever, and they can be bought at a cheap price. Some areas are quite large, but you can leave and do other things to break it up a bit.  Some cons  Performance was mostly fine on my 3070 ti. There was really only one area that I experienced drops in FPS and that was towards the beginning of the game. Capping the FPS at 60 helped to keep things smoother and I left it like for the rest of the game.  I would liked to have seen more NPC's, more lore, and a better fleshed out story. Just better integration of the areas in general and reasons for their existence. Having said that, the mystique doesn't really hurt the game either. I'd just like to know more about the world and see more people living in it, other than enemies.  Conclusion  For me, this game is my favourite Metroidvania, above Hollow Knight, Silksong, Blasphemous, and Nine Sols. For the amount of fun I had in this game, the satisfaction in completing it, the intense and sometimes epic gameplay, and for its lack of tedious and frustrating boss fights, it's Aeterna Noctis for me. Great experience that I didn't want to end.

I really wanted to like this game so bad... I love metroidvanias and I love platforming and difficult bosses, so on paper this game should be richt up my alley. But sadly this game is not for me.   I loved the story, even though I didn't get far so it may even be better - or worse - later on. And I guess that's basically it for the positive things I have to say about it.   There were so many things that bothered me, even though I've only played ~2 hours. The game starts incredibly slow with not much to hook my attention.  And while I loved that it is platforming heavy, the controls are not fluid enough imho. The game sometimes ate my jump when trying to run and jump off a platform, even though I jumped way before the end of the platform.  That the controls are not fluid was also really noticable in combat. There's a delay in the dash input which meant I often dashed into the enemy instead of away.   I played it on steamdeck, and while the game ran great, the size of the text and sometimes the spacing was difficult to read and gave me a headache. I also found the map not really clear, even after I bought the map from the Merchant. I don't think that had to do with the steamdeck, but I can't really compare it obviously.  And last but not least, after I stopped playing I wondered why I hadn't really gotten any achievements. I don't mind if a game is sparse with achievements, but I was just wondering if something was bugged.  So I looked up the list of achievements, and it seems there are a lot of achievements you can only get if you play it on the hard mode. I may be mistaken, because I didn't get far enough in the game to have encountered the things you'd get these achievements for, but it seemed like the achievements were some "normal gameplay" things like doing certain bossfights etc.  And like I'm all for tying harder achievements to the in-game difficulty, but if you can't get achievements for gameplay related things on an easier mode, like completing certain platforming challenges or defeating a boss, I find that ♥♥♥♥♥♥ to your audience.  By all means tie achievements to hard (additional) platforming parts, for not getting damage on bosses, for defeating the game on hard mode or for completing it without dying, just don't tie so many achievements to difficulty.   I've heard a lot of people talking about how much they love it so who knows, maybe it's a skill and/or a me-issue. But all in all I can't really recommend this game, sadly

三年前打了二十几个小时，因为太难退坑了，看到明年要出第二部，就想着捡起来重新玩玩。历经42小时，除了一个实在找不到的黑暗材料和一个宝箱（地图上的问号明明清完了啊），也算是全收集了。  玩下来最大的感受，真有必要做这么难吗？人家都是战斗之余加点跳跳乐，这游戏是跳跳乐之余加点战斗。而且即使是主线必经的跳跳乐，都有相当的难度，更别说第七神庙，帝皇第四门，此箭载于我心，还有个别灵魂之镜的逆天难度。有一说一嗷，传送箭本身设计的确实不错，有些正常的跳跳乐跳过去之后成就感十足，但是也有很多跳跳乐跳过去之后只觉得恶心，很明显制作组对于难度的把控是有问题的，毕竟如果真会把控也不会把所有地图都做成无穷无尽的跳跳乐了。  可惜啊，可惜，我个人觉得，这游戏的综合素质真挺高的，t1水平其实是有的，庞大的内容量，有意思的世界观，上乘的音乐，出色的演出，有点意思的Boss设计，但都被逆天的难度给掩盖了。按照这游戏纯纯路边一条的口碑和销量，估计也不可能大赚特赚，这样还有第二部看来制作组应该是不缺钱的，只能说希望下一部，制作组还是应该考虑一下普通玩家，不要只沉浸在自我满足的“艺术”世界里了。

Ótimo metroidvania, inspirado em Castlevania SOTN, Hollow Kninght e Ori (ele traz o melhor dos três em um só pacote), mas com seu jeito próprio. Um pouco preguiçoso na questão dos inimigos (poucos tipos, repetitivos), mas o mapa é imenso e o jogo é longo, realmente longo. A história bem legal, tipo nota dez. Não me arrependo da compra, mas recomendo esperar a promoção, pois nem todos terão paciência para a dificuldade, pois o jogo é extremamente difícil em algumas partes. Digo, INSANAMENTE difícil. Pulos, dashes e teleportações acrobáticas que irão corroer o seu juízo centenas de vezes para tentar te induzir a um "rage quit"... Não é para amadores. Mas com persistência e INTELIGÊNCIA você facilita a sua própria vida e acha um jeito. Quase sempre haverá algum jeito mais fácil de driblar os desafios. Bem, algumas partes exigirão habilidade, inevitavelmente. Mas minha impressão do jogo, já estando perto dos 70%, é que ele é o MELHOR metrodvania disponível no momento. É uma OBRA PRIMA.

This game suffer from bad visual designs, the first areas are just too cluttered, the effect in the virtual world where the platform morph is cool looking but quite bad for platforming clarity. The camera by default zoom out way too far to see your character detail so you really can't see any animation on him, I'm also using a 27 inch monitor btw.  The dash has a cooldown which is super weird because there is no visual indications when the cooldown is back, but it still replenished after you pogo so it feel janky at times  You cannot unbind the left stick to movement and the menu also forces you to use the left stick which is quite frustrating since I like to play 2D games with Dpad instead  Overall, so far the game is fine, just plagued with visual clutters and bad visual design in general. Coming from Silksong, this game is definitely very rough around the edges. Will give it some more times since people seems to recommend this game a lot.

A fantastic, challenging metroidvania, with very difficult platforming, excellent bosses, unforgettable music and beautiful art. It's a shame that it got so many negative reviews just because it's difficult, and it even has an easier difficulty mode. It deserves much better reviews.  Feedback for the devs:  The art looks incredible, but sometimes, it is a bit too detailed and makes it a bit hard to see certain projectiles and enemies. This was almost never an issue, but it might be worth looking into for future projects. Slightly more contrast between the foreground/background layers and the enemies would go a long way.  The UI, at first, is very overwhelming, and takes some time getting used to. A menu showcasing questitems and keys would've helped, and cleaner, less overloaded menus in general. It's only a problem for the first few hours.

困难难度通关这个游戏以后，只能用“可惜”来评价。明明有着优秀的世界观、音乐、美术，最后却变成了一个被无尽的跳跳乐和索然无味的战斗填满的游戏。  本身是一个相当吸引人的反传统剧情，但是整个游戏流程却和这个剧情叙事极度割裂，你只是在疯狂地跳跳乐和打怪，然后过段时间给你播片讲一点剧情，连一个令人印象深刻的NPC都没能做出来，最后还挖了一个大坑未完待续。  严格来说这并不是一个传统的银河城游戏，而是充满了大量跳跳乐的蔚蓝式游戏。其实我不能否认传送箭是一个挺巧妙的设计，但是正是这个设计成为了万恶之源，因为他导致了操作上限的过度拔高，而整个游戏地图也必须因此做得大而空，人形boss更是必须攻击大范围aoe带瞬移，从而造成了偏离了传统银河城地图精巧，重探索和战斗演出的特点，而变成了一个考验解谜和精准操作的高难度游戏。  所以，如果你没有一定的银河城或者蔚蓝的游玩经验，那么我认为整个游戏是过难的（当然，制作组分了难度，但是却不允许解成就，等于一个软限制）。但是如果你有同类游戏的经验，通过丰富的能力以及合理利用制作组设计了很多有利于跳跳乐的道具，整体的难度其实并不算很出格，制作组刚好把难度卡在了一个适中的位置，只要反复尝试就能摸索出规律，没有需要非常极限的操作（除了帝皇四门跳了我5个小时），而且部分的关卡设计也能看出制作组的匠心（比如梦境的四铃铛谜题非常巧妙）。但是问题在于在此之前你必须度过一个非常难熬的前期，而我想大部分玩家其实并不能忍受这个不知所云还引导稀烂的高难跳跳乐游戏。  至于战斗部分就比较无趣了，可能是制作组的怜悯，给了主角很高的数值和轮椅武器还有很长的霸体的时间，小怪全都是路边，很多boss也可以直接拼血过而不必上窜下跳地躲，但是整体演出也没有什么亮眼的地方，也没有哪个boss做了什么比较好地塑造，只是跳跳乐完了奖励个boss，然后继续跳跳乐而已。  最后，我也有一个有趣的角度，或许玩家其实就是黑暗之王本人，制作组就是创世神混沌，我们一路对抗着神的意志，经过千万次重生来到终点，回首却有万般言语不知从何说起，只剩感慨——“艰难困苦，不足道也”。

プレイ中、面倒くさいステージに遭遇したので、気持ちを落ち着けるために〇ッドセルズをやり始めたら、こっちのゲームに戻ってくることが出来なくなってしまったので、物語途中ですが、レビューを残したいと思います。  進捗度70％でのレビューになります。  このゲームは自分にとってはプレイしていて、すごく好き！な部分と〇ソゲーか！？と思う部分と両方の部分がある珍しいメトロイドヴァニアでした。 ちなみに私はoriはメトロイドヴァニアっていうよりパズルゲーだろ…という感じになって途中で挫折しています。 このゲームはoriよりはメトロイドヴァニアに寄っていると自分は思いました。  〇好き嫌い織り交ざった要素：ワープする矢 ステージ中にへんな白いふわふわで邪魔されてとれないアイテムや通路がいっぱいあります。 これらはワープの矢をとると通れるようになるのですが、かなーり手元の操作が煩雑です！  まず矢を撃つ（エイムは完全に手動）→撃った矢がふわふわを通り抜けたところでワープボタンを押してワープ、ワープすると上下左右をふわふわに囲まれた部分にでてきて、そのままではダメをもらって戻されてしまうので、すぐに再び矢を撃って、また矢がふわふわを抜けたところでワープボタン。 というのがバンバン出てきます。  矢の速度はふっつーの他アクションゲームでみる矢と同じような速度で飛んでいきます。 ので猶予は短いです。  ふわふわのあたり判定も大きいので、ちょっと角度が悪かったりすると、ワープに失敗してダメージをもらったうえで、ギミックの最初からやり直しです。  矢を撃つときは多少ゲーム時間がゆっくりになるのが救いですが、それでもそんなにゆっくりはしてられません。  しかもこのふわふわを超えたところに結構重要なアイテムがあったりするのでやっぱりメトロイドヴァニア的にはいかないとと思い、矢を撃っては戻され、撃っては戻され…をなんども繰り返しました。 その時は「なんだこの〇ソゲー！」と思ったのですが、このワープの矢をふわふわを超えるためじゃなくて道中で使い始めると感覚が180度回転します。  取った後のステージにおあつらえ向きに、ワープの矢を使って自由に移動しようぜ！というステージが来ます。 これがめちゃくちゃ面白いんですよ！ ふわふわで鍛えられて手元がスムーズになってくると、二段ジャンプとか空中ダッシュよりも移動速度が早いし、ワープ距離も長いし、スパイダーマンがビルの間を颯爽と駆け抜けるように移動ができるようになるんですね。 じゃまなふわふわもないので戻されたりしないし、多少エイムをミスっても次の矢で全然挽回できます。  その時は「ワープの矢たっのしーーーー！！ほかのメトロイドヴァニアでも採用すべきでは！？」って気持ちになりました。 このゲームならではの体験でした。制作者の方ありがとうございます！  〇ちょっと気になったところ。 ・このゲームはアスレチックメトロイドヴァニアなので、ジャンプやダッシュの自由度が高いが、その分とっつきづらさもある。 ジャンプボタンはボタンの離し具合で小ジャンになるし、ダッシュもレバー入れ込みで自分の好きなタイミングで止められるんですね。 まぁそれができるってことは、道中でもガンガンそれをやらされるわけで… 着地できる足場が全体的に小さいのも相まって、最初のうちは、ほんとに何気ない穴でもバンバン落ちます。 ここで嫌になる人は多そう。  しかも、ほかのメトロイドヴァニアでははしごとかを使って登らせるような場所でも、階段のように配置された足場をたどっていくようにして登らないといけないようなステージ構成が多いんですね。 普通にめんどいです。 まぁワープの矢を取ればガン無視でごり押しできますのでヤッホーです。  ・ボスがギミックボス。 せっかくoriよりも攻撃力アップ要素とかが多くてメトロイドヴァニア感が強いのに、ボスは攻撃力とか関係なくギミックを解けるかどうか。というボスが多かったと思います。（ちょっとレビューを書くまでに時間を置いてしまったので曖昧） ギミックをつかいつつも相手のHPを削って倒すボス。とかのほうが個人的には好みなのでちょっと残念ポイントかな。  〇許容できない点 ・マップがマップ屋を見つけてそのステージのマップを買わない限り、ほんとーーーーにうっすら自分が通ったところくらいしか見えない。そして案の定マップ屋もわかりやすい位置にはいない。  これホローナイトとか有名な作品でも採用されてますが、私的にはイライラポイントです。 ホローナイトが成功したからそこからインスパイアされてるのかな？ 本当にやめてほしい！ せめて通った道くらいは見せてくれてもいーんでないかい…？  もう途中からは全部のマップを公開している方がいらっしゃって、その方のマップを見ながら攻略してました。 マップ制作者の方、本当にありがとうございます。  ・ワープするステージと惑星。 上記二つのステージは本当に〇ソだと思いました。特に惑星。 惑星が中断した直接の原因です。 まぁもうこれは制作者さんがやりたいことと、私が求めていることのずれなのでしょうがないですね。 多分このゲームを再起動して、再挑戦することはないと思います。  〇その他 ・闇の王なのに、部下からため口をきかれたり、コツコツお願いされたサブクエストをこなしているのはなんだか笑えた。  ・ストーリーはソウルライクヴァニアと違い、しっかりしていたので、最後まで見たかったな。  ・最初はチュートリアルが丁寧だなぁと最初は思ったが、いきなりぷつっと丁寧感はなくなり、自分で説明書をみる感じになる。 最後まで丁寧なチュートリアルにするか、最初からスキルをとったら自分で説明書よんでね！のどちらかに寄せた方が混乱しない気がした。  ・完全に自分のせいですが、最初はシステムがあんまりわかってなくて、図書館的なところのカギ？かな？、を取った後に図書館に戻るのを忘れていて、壁つかまりジャンプを取り忘れ、この先どう進むんだ！となってしまった。 カギを見つけたら、ステージ攻略中でも戻るようにしたほうがいいかも。  ・敵を倒した後に出てくるお金などが消滅するのが早いのがちょっと気になった。  こんな感じでした。  対戦ありがとうございました！

Aeterna Noctis, developed and published by Aeternum Game Studios, stands out as a compelling entry in the Metroidvania genre, blending challenging gameplay with a richly detailed and interconnected world. Players step into the role of the King of Darkness, embarking on a quest to regain lost powers and restore balance in a realm shrouded in mystery and ancient lore. From the outset, the game sets a tone that is both atmospheric and foreboding, inviting exploration and demanding mastery of its intricate systems.  The world of Aeterna Noctis is divided into sixteen distinct regions, each with its own unique visual identity and gameplay challenges. This expansive map is designed to encourage players to explore every nook and cranny, rewarding curiosity with hidden secrets, lore fragments, and alternative paths. The game’s non-linear structure allows for a personalized approach to progression, which keeps the experience fresh and engaging as players tackle different zones in their own order. The hand-drawn art style is striking, featuring fluid frame-by-frame animations that breathe life into the characters and environments, evoking a timeless fantasy aesthetic that complements the dark narrative.  Combat in Aeterna Noctis is precise and fast-paced, requiring players to carefully learn enemy patterns and utilize a variety of abilities and upgrades to overcome increasingly difficult encounters. The controls are responsive, and the combat mechanics encourage strategic thinking rather than button-mashing, rewarding skill and timing. The game offers two distinct modes: the standard Aeterna Mode for a classic Metroidvania experience, and the more punishing Noctis Mode for those seeking an intense challenge. This variety adds significant replay value, catering both to newcomers and veterans of the genre who crave difficulty and depth.  Narratively, the game weaves its story through environmental storytelling and beautifully crafted voice-acted cutscenes, supported by eight language options that broaden its accessibility. While the plot may take a backseat to gameplay for some, it enriches the world and provides emotional context to the King of Darkness’s journey. The atmospheric soundtrack further elevates the experience, with haunting melodies that underscore the game’s themes of loss, redemption, and power.  Despite its many strengths, Aeterna Noctis is not without flaws. Some players have encountered technical hiccups, such as glitches and crashes, which can disrupt immersion, especially on certain platforms like the Nintendo Switch. Additionally, the game’s length and difficulty curve might feel overwhelming or repetitive to some, as the constant challenge demands patience and persistence. However, for those who enjoy methodical exploration combined with tough but fair combat, these issues are minor compared to the overall depth and polish of the experience.  Ultimately, Aeterna Noctis is a well-crafted and ambitious Metroidvania that successfully marries challenging gameplay with rich visuals and storytelling. It invites players into a dark, beautifully realized world where every corner holds a secret and every battle tests skill and resolve. For fans of the genre, it offers a rewarding and memorable adventure, proving that even indie studios can deliver an epic experience with heart and style.  Rating: 7/10

Esse jogo é uma verdadeira joia da atualidade. Eu comecei a jogar sem compromisso e só parei depois de completar todas as quests e coletar todos os recursos disponíveis.  De início fui observando aquelas cutscenes modestas, história com trama envolvente, trilha sonora épica, pensa em um jogo onde os desenvolvedores investiram a alma, tudo construído com muito carinho, pura arte e magia!  Mas o que mais me intrigou foi a trama além de uma história de amor... Sim, foi o ódio que algumas situações de exploração e chefes conseguem transmitir. O combate é impecável e ao mesmo tempo implacável. Seus nervos irão torcer, suas mãos vão suar, um deslize = morte, botão errado = morte, olhou para o lado = morte, você está tremendo porque não sabe se vai conseguir? = morte. Aqui é a hora que você pensa em parar, porque já morreu mais de dez vezes. Sensacional, pois a mecânica do jogo apesar de ser o 'padrão' onde você precisa morrer para aprender, contudo você acaba se divertindo com a maneira que o jogo extrai o máximo do seu potencial e reflexos como jogador.  É só questão de tempo para pegar o jeito, eu fiquei meio bom depois de 50 horas :D

Игра имеет свои недостатки, но она точно останется в моем сердечке как одна из лучших. Шикарный платформинг с отзывчивым управлением, отличная рисовка, прекрасный саундрек, неплохая боевая система хоть и есть нюансы которые "решаются" билдом. Платформинг испытания на время - душно, ради хорошего времени самый главный скилл - это скорость нажатия рывка.  Очень жду Aeterna Lucis, надеюсь доработают боевку, увеличат бестиарий, испытания подсмотрят из своей же игры Eden Genesis которую я тоже рекомендую.  Немного обидно что рейтинг занизили любители соевого Hollow Knight со словами "СЛОЖНЫЙ ПЛАТФОРМЕР- ФУ" (считаю такое неадекватным - игра просто не для вас), игра заслуживает намного большего.

本来一开始，还觉得游戏是不错的，但是玩到后面，越来越想给差评。因为这个游戏实在有些太故意的恶意了。 先说优点：体量够大，BOSS设计的不错，有几个印象很深刻的BOSS，比如[spoiler] 火凤凰[/spoiler]，还有[spoiler] 海底城主脑 [/spoiler]，设计的都挺喜欢的，不会太难，有需要有一定的分析，见招拆招。还有跳跳乐的玩法，难度算适中。存档点数量比较合理，通常不需要走很远的距离就有一个存档点，死亡惩罚相对不那么高。 但是缺点挺多的，主要在于：这游戏可以说是集各种恶意与一身了。 首先，堆怪，而且是那种恶心人的怪，例如在[spoiler] 海底城 [/spoiler]，有的地方同时有大量的地面快速攻击的怪物，还有打死放亡语的怪，同时还堆几个空中瞄准激光加放导弹的怪，再加一些高频放可反射子弹的怪，堆在一起，不是说没办法逐个击破，但是实在是恶心人。 然后，一个更严重的问题：有的岔路，在你缺某种能力时候是走不通的，是死胡同。但问题是，游戏在这种死胡同的入口不去通过某种手段限制玩家不让进入，而是设计了一长串高难度跳跳乐，让你走的过去。当你终于走过去，觉得能拿宝物了或者该到下一个剧情点的时候，不，你发现这是死！胡！同！死胡同就算了，最要命的是，你想回去？对不起，除非你拥有某种游戏消耗品，否则你必须给我老老实实原！路！返！回，同样高难度跳跳乐给我反！着！走！回！去！不想反着走？对不起，已经存档了哦，你想回档到岔路口也不行哦。而且这样的情况，不止一处，整个游戏过程中，经常因为这个问题走冤枉路，白白浪费时间，后面还要再走一次这条路才行。就算是恶意，我从没见过哪个游戏会说让你走错路了，还强制你走回去，而没有能返回的办法的。黑魂还知道给你返回篝火呢，大不了损失点魂。 还有就是在个别地方甚至给你在跳跳乐的路上，半空中放点飞行怪，还是攻击不好躲开那种，虽然这玩意可以用于下批续跳跃和空中冲刺，但是拿一个怪放那还是不太合适吧。不过相比上面是小问题了。 然后是地图标记就3种，还得买，还限制个数。这个搞不懂有什么意义，纯属恶意为难玩家了，别的类银河城起码几乎没见过在标记上这么扣扣嗖嗖的，不过相比上面提的，这个也算小问题了，无所谓。 总体来讲，如果你喜欢跳跳乐，喜欢打BOSS战，并且接受上面说的恶意的话，这游戏还是挺适合的，其他情况，我认为不太建议去玩的。

I can't believe I am seeing any negative reviews for this game.  It might be my favorite metroidvania of all time.  If you exclude roguelike/roguelite this is probably in my number 1 spot.  Best in class gameplay and mechanics.  Tons of versatility in builds and ways to play.  Incredible platforming.  Fast travel is great.  It leans on some ideas of other games and improves on them.    The only downsides I can see in the game is that its long and challenging.  But that is what I wanted so it's not a problem for me.  It was so gratifying to beat each boss and clear each area.  And it being long was awesome.  I didn't want it to end.  I look forward to the seqeul.    Personally I didn't love the art style but it didn't impact the game at all for me.  The story wasn't great either.  The combat mechanics in themselves are very very basic but combined with the incredible movement abilities like teleport arrows and it is something unique and special.  This game doesn't deserve a single negative review. It might not be for you but it is a great game regardless.

大家好！我是手残，打完这个游戏已经成为跳跳乐糕手了，你也来试试吧！总体推荐，量是真的大，变态的跳跳乐也是真的多。 建议没钱直接ce，地图商人的地图和道具显示要买，一定要买，后面被为难的地方多着呢，不要在这里难为自己，有地图没地图两个体验，我们在大量跳跳乐里面发现了少量的路，所以跑图非常难受，一次能搜刮完就一次搜完。 怪能打就打，不能直接技能树中间点满开启轮椅人生。 前期记得去古老神殿拿二段跳宝石，拿了跳跳乐都会红温，不拿怕不是直接卸载游戏。  —————————————————————————————— 以下是碎碎念 —————————————————————————————— 50h简单难度通关游戏，进度103%，基本全收集，就差dlc，好玩爱玩。 —————————————————————————————— 42h打到了85%，熟悉使用传送箭后太好玩啦！后续并没有解锁降低难度的能力，但这个跳跳乐就是可以反复试错，心态一直在 是人跳的？ 和 我真牛逼！ 之间反复横跳。 —————————————————————————————— 简单难度用轮椅（技能树中间线点满）23h进度打到了51%，感觉值回票价了，后续跳不跳得过都无所谓了，所以改回推荐，在能玩的前提下希望别人也来受苦。 —————————————————————————————— 不死心，重新点技能把苟活流点满了，是真能苟啊，简直就是轮椅，硬换血打过boss了，现在17h打到了36%进度，仍然不推荐，因为我又见到了背景花得要死的跳跳乐，看不清，真的看不清，跳失败的原因是因为视力不好真的难受。 —————————————————————————————— 现在进度是14h，凤凰没打过，这个破背景什么也看不清，感觉每个招式都能躲过去但重来一百次归来还是看不清，算了我是真的菜，就打到这里吧。 —————————————————————————————— 现在进度是10h打了20%，而且大概率是游戏最简单的那20%的进度，后面要打多久不敢想。 刚开始玩我还奇怪，这么阳间的设置为什么评分这么低，难道就因为我选的简单难度？开局就能开传送，到处都是检查点，没走几步就能拿到死亡不掉魂的宝石，装上后可以说毫无死亡惩罚。 直到我打上了塔，超级，超级，超级多的跳跳乐，问题不大难度不高，然后我开了大剑boss，死去活来一小时，然后去熔炉见识到什么叫超绝容错全靠检查点复活的极限跳跳乐，我xx地终于知道为啥给这么多检查点了。更破防的是想搜攻略把二段跳先拿了，看到底下说前期这些跳跳乐都算简单，后面还有更变态的，这评分应得的TuT

It's the most controversial game for me to review. I beat this game on Noctis (the "intended" difficulty) in 55 hours by 109% (I assume that 1% is beating all boss-rush dlc challenges, which I don't care about). At first I was very pleased with the game and couldn't understand, what people don't like about it... now I know. The main reason is - the game stops to be fun and starts to be annoying (most of the time).  Platforming stops to be fun when you obtain crystal arrows, because devs think that precise shooting arrow in 1 inch high corridor full of spikes is fun smh. The most fun jump puzzles are the ones w/out this ability, and sadly there are not as many of them as I wanted to. Exploring and fighting stops to be fun in the 2 locations, because there is enormous amount of enemies which to kill you have to hit them 4-7 times each. And on top of that, the locations themselves are just platfroms above platfroms above platforms... Bosses were never fun for me, but towards the end you can just face-roll many of them (even the last one), which was good for me, but that's not a very good thing overall. Metroidvania aspect is somewhat competent, I'd say. But don't expect any revelations here, the world connection itself is pretty poor. Backtracking is not fun at all, because most of the platforming in this game is tough, and everytime, when you need to go somewhere, you have to pass through same exact jump puzzles over and over again, always to be concentrated, and that's just awful. Take GRIME, Hollow Knight for example - in these games during backtracking you are in your head, you build plans on what are you gonna do next, relaxing, etc. But here... no fun allowed, focus is your eternal state, because you gotta say hi to mister 1 inch high corridor full of spikes or else it will obliterate you (it will anyway lol). Plot is dissapointing. The music is just piano, so no memorable tracks for you, because they all sound alike.  Well, at least I can praise art-design, but unfortunately that's the only unambiguous pro. To wrap this up, if you want tooth-crushing platforming on every inch of the game with almost no sense of progression by damage - this game is for you.

This game is probably the best game i ever played. For context, I loved Hollow Knight and Ori, where we have a challenging platforming and also story. Aeterna Noctis, felt like Hollow Knight (very challenging) but not mental as the "Path of Pain" (Hollow Knight part where you will want to kill yourself). The saving locations and teleport are quite balanced. One thing that really annoys me in some platform games (i.e., Hollow Knight) is how unforgiving dying can be (re-spawn far away) or lose all your stuff forever. The story is awesome and complex (can be confusing at times), so is the music and map size. Some platforming at different gravity points are really challenging (imagine a small world pulling you to the middle while you platform) It's been a while that i don't have that much fun in a game :) Thank you devs for this gem. Looking forward for the Aeterna Lucis later this year!

I do like this game, but just a heads up, it constantly forgets to be fun. Challenging platforming is great, but doing those same challenges in reverse is a waste of time - because there are no shortcuts back to where the player starts (with a few noticeable exceptions where it was impossible to make the player slog back through the entire section). Backtracking is a pillar of a Metroidvania, but so much of my backtracking was because the game doesn't allow the player to buy enough markers to indicate why a path was left unexplored.   Aside from those annoyances, this game really does have some unique and beautifully rendered areas that I will never forget. Movement feels good, mobility upgrades are given at a good pace, and while the story wasn't good, it never interfered with the game play (looking at you, After Image).

只能说除非你十分想玩类银河恶魔城游戏而所有热门的都玩过，不然别玩这个。   有太多跳跳乐，跳跃冲刺攀附墙壁的手感都很好，唯独里面有个技能叫传送箭，就是射出一根箭然后传送到箭的位置，然而，射箭的方向非常难操控（我是键鼠不知道手柄怎么样），尤其是后面有一片以星球为主的地方（类似于愤怒的小鸟太空版），处于星球的下半部分方向操纵相反，总之非常折磨。   支线任务较多，但是引导几乎没有，一条支线甚至要在两个区域分别完成，而且不找NPC接任务，你连任务的相关元素都看不见（比如有个解密任务，需要找六个字符，而这六个字符散布在这个任务区域里，你在打完这个区域boss后才能接任务，才知道要找字符，相当于你要探两遍地图）。   支线任务不能一次性领完，比如NPC要你去找一个东西，说找完给你奖励，结果找完了去领奖励，又告诉你还需要另一个东西，等你找到了，又需要找下一个，设计这种任务机制的真是神人了，这种任务还不止一个，我真想给制作人一拳。   强行拖长游戏时间，可传送点给的太少，传送道具获取困难，并且从一个传送点出去的路大部分都是死路，也就是说你探索完支路后只能原路返回而不是去下一个传送点传送，更逆天的是有些收集物在你跳过来拿到之后，还要再带着它原路返回，真是极品设计。   boss招式比较匮乏，一个boss可能就会三招，然后反反复复用，而boss血量普遍很高（困难模式，其他模式不知道），玩起来有点乏味，对于我这种普攻党真的是坐牢   空洞骑士的既视感太强（一共16个区域），有两三个区域简直一模一样，但是这个游戏里的魂或者说能量（就是空洞里的白槽）不能靠普通攻击攒，只能靠打败小怪后，由小怪掉落，这导致打boss战及其蛋疼，技能也根本用不了，只能留着回血，真的逆天。 总之这个游戏只能说量大管饱，但真正惊艳的设计非常少，只能说可以一玩吧。我反正被那几个破支线弄烦了，每次满心欢喜地回去交任务结果告诉你：逗你玩的还有下一步，最后给你一个垃圾护符，真希望每个游戏都能有开发者通关视屏，让这些SB狠狠吃自己做出来的shit.PS要玩就选最简单的难度，因为我确定你不会想打全成就的

https://ggapp.io/null/reviews/aeterna-noctis/pumNYYiU  This is a bit of a difficult game for me to review.  Even though I finished the game and generally had a good time, I still had quite a bit of mixed feelings about the game.  Aeterna is a metroidvania and mostly follows the rules of the genre.  You start off relatively weak and then as you progress and get more powers, you start to become a powerhouse and get to steamroll most everything.  As a general rule of thumb, metroidvanias tend to fall into two camps, the combat camp, and the platforming camp.  There's also the general exploration camp as well, but generally the gameplay mechanics only really encourage either making combat difficult, or the platforming difficult.    Aeterna defintiely falls into the category of making it more of a platforming challenge than a combat challenge.  Almost all of the difficulty in the game comes from the precise platforming and its pretty much a fact that during certain sections of this game you're going to have very sweaty palms.  I played in Aeterna (normal or easy) difficulty and while 95% of the platforming is fine, there were defintily some portions that gave me the Path of Pain PTSD flashbacks.  The combat itself is also half decent, giving you a good selection of weapons and powers to choose from, although by and large I used the default sword you start with 99% of the time.  The weapons you get are never given a proper introduction and their use cases are almost always situational.  There'll be times when I remember to use the other weapons, but I'd say for the most part you're fine ignoring them.  There's also a decent skill tree, focusing on ranged, health, and melee.  You get a bit of options, but some nodes are just clearly better than others.  5% less cost on skills or heal faster?  I'm not sure anyone would pick the 5% less cost on skills. =)  The story isn't anything to write home about either, there's a story for sure, and the main character is quite chatty in both exposition of story as well as telling you how to use each tool "press b for etc".  There's some mystery as to what exactly is happening as the story in the game doesn't really tell you, but its better than the "your princess is in another castle" storyline at least.  Artwork and exploration is also quite well done in the game.  It takes a bit of time for the art to grow on you but it evantually does.  There's quite a lot of variance in the game from underground dungeons to overworld exploration.  The game starts off with somewhat of a unified world (albeit very very straight line), and then devolves into individual worlds.  Some of it is semi-connected but by-and-large its separated dungeons for much of the end-game.    Music is mostly piano and for some that might be appealing but for me it was ok.  I played about 1/4 of the game without music and didn't notice the lack of it to be honest.    So why is this a hard game for me to review?  Mostly because of how hard the platforming in the game is.  For those who love celeste and its ilk, it must feel like some sort of god's gift where your dreams come true.  A metroidvania that has celeste'esque platforming??  But for me, metroidvania is mostly about exploration and the growing power fantasy and in this game the exploration mostly takes a back seat to the extreme platforming.  Even in the normal/easy difficulty there's quite a bit of precision and while the difficulty is dialed down, if you're not good at platforming, you might as well not even bother to start the game.  Boss fights also involve some platforming where if you don't hit the right platforms a the right time you're going to die/get hit.    Overall, I give this a solid 4 stars.  If you're into platforming, then 5 stars easily, but compared to the 6 stars that I give Hollow Knight (out of 5 stars), that's kinda where Aeterna Noctis lands for me.  I really want to recommend it, but the extreme emphasis on platforming makes it hard for me to push it any more than what I give it.

Fazia muito tempo que eu procurava por um Metroidvania assim! Esse game é um exemplo muito claro do que significa Metroidvania, Adorei!   Os gráficos são muito bem feitos, muito detalhe, os efeitos de luzes e transparência, é muito lindo!  O design das fases é muito bem pensado, muito bem estruturado. Eu só achei que alguns trechos têm uma dificuldade desnecessária, são poucos pedacinhos de fases que fiquei até um pouco incomodado. Um ponto aqui também, é que em alguns momentos o game deixa o jogador meio sem ter certeza do próximo passo, vagando pelas fases procurando qualquer coisa que continue a história. Achei que faltou um tipo de "log" das conversas e uma descrição melhor das missões.  O design dos personagens e inimigos também é muito bom. Na medida para que não sejam banais, nem difíceis demais.  O som também é ótimo. As músicas são composições lindas, muito focadas no piano e orquestra. Cada fase tem o seu tema, e os efeitos sonoros são muito bem colocados.  A história é bem interessante, tem uma pitada de humor sarcástico e um personagem que conta histórias com referências da cultura popular como se fossem outros mundos! Aqui eu coloco também a questão da progressão, que achei muito boa em relação à dificuldade e aquisição de poderes, mas não em relação a progressão da história. Há momentos em que poderia ter um direcionamento para as opções disponíveis, pra evitar deixar o jogador vagando pelo cenário durante horas tentando descobrir o próximo passo!  A jogabilidade é muito boa com apenas 2 pontos de atenção: há alguns movimentos que poderiam ter as combinações de controles melhor pensados. Como as flechas, porque temos que usar o mesmo stick de movimento para mirar, causando alguns movimentos meio atrapalhados.  Em suma, sem dar spoilers, é um jogo maravilhoso! Eu terminei com 99% e talvez até volte pra completar aquele 1% que faltou!! Valeu cada centavo!

I play a ton of Metroidvanias, and I really do not like this one.  I find it overly frustrating for essentially mediocre game play, and overall just not worth the time.  I have too many other games I really enjoy to waste time with this kind of jank.  If the game were really special and had good controls or unique combat it might be worth it but it doesn't... it's just mid in all respects, but tuned to be difficult and very punishing on death for no good reason.  Don't get me wrong... there's nothing wrong with difficulty in general, but it needs to be balanced by quality of game play.  That's the issue here... lots of difficulty, harsh punishments for any missteps... but only mediocre, run of the mill game play with very little interesting design to make it worth the time investment.  I also play on Steam Deck, so game time above is not correct.  And by the way.. this game has NO right to be steam deck verified.  The text in this game is microscopic, and the controls remap function is unusable on the small steam deck screen due to the text scaling.  Beware of the false "Verified" rating this game has.  Everything else works ok, but it should be "Playable" at best.  The combat is ok... the movement is good.  Range can be a bit of an issue, but in general the flow of movement is fine.  The graphics and sound design are very good.  I don't like how the bounce works.. you have to jump and press down and attack, which is fine... but it is very tightly timed, so if you are off at all you just take damage and fail the bounce.  And that is generally true in this game, everything you do is very tightly timed.  But this game has every single one of the "bad" tropes that plague the genre.  It's designed to be very punishing.  You can only respawn at lanterns once, otherwise you respawn back at the start of the zone.  That's even if you manage to collect all of your lost souls from a death, which is another issue.  You lose all of your darkness every time you die until you reclaim it. None of these are super unique... they are bad designs, but they are also common to some of the games in this genre for some reason.  The issue with this game is they combine in a very harsh and frustrating manner with no actual payoff from quality or skill of combat.  It's just punishment with no reward.  All of this just makes the game more punishing than it deserves to be, and the game is just very frustrating to play instead of fun.  The other issue the game has is that controls are really awkward on game pad by default.  They do offer a menu to change those but it doesn't actually work, because there are functions in the world that will still all show the original keys.  Additionally, if you remap the B button for example, then you can't use any of the in world thrones or chests... even if you remapped a different key to B's default function.  It just won't work.  So you are forced to restore the default, which puts dash on RT, which is very awkward.  Again this just adds frustration to an already frustrating game. This isn't a souls-like... you don't get a great sense of skill and reward for defeating bosses or enemies.  The combat flow is simple... it's just tuned to be brutal.  And that doesn't really work because without some sense of skill, everything just feels unnecessary and the game isn't fun enough to offset the bad respawn and death chest mechanics.  There are tons of really great, really rewarding Metroidvania's with great graphics and gameplay on the market.  I see no reason to waste time on this one, given the combination of how uncomfortable and punishing it is for really no returned value.

这游戏我觉得比空洞骑士还要神，作为一个恶魔城忠实玩家，玩过的恶魔城游戏数不胜数，但这个游戏不火我觉得真的没道理，我看好多差评都是因为跳跳乐，但作为我来说，我是比较喜欢跳跳乐的，因为感觉操作起来会很酷，我也玩了蔚蓝，这个跳跳乐比蔚蓝难度可低太多了，boss战难度比空洞骑士低太多了，再加上死了不需要跑图，惩罚很少，我觉得已经算大幅度降低难度了，我下来要说的是优点，游戏音乐满分，钢琴曲简直无敌，游戏关卡设计满分，比如宫殿的解密，宇宙上面的玩法，每一次都会给我带来极大的震撼，玩法算是很新颖了，再加上游戏的风景简直太美了，这点可以和奥日媲美了，这游戏说难的话我觉得并没有很难，因为他已经能做到把死亡惩罚去掉外加附近就有篝火可以存档，这个一下子就把游戏难度拉低很多了，不用死了再继续跑图了，整个游戏现在还没通关，还差一点，但完成度已经到70多了，我真的很希望这个游戏可以火起来，要比我玩过的渎神，空洞，奥日，暗影火炬城，血污，盐与避难所，莉莉要好的多的多，总之玩法上的创新已经地图设计真的是一大特色了，整个游戏没有卡住过，最难的貌似也只有暗影王子打了半个小时，其他的所有地方没有卡超过10分钟，我希望这个游戏公司可以出续作，这样我再也不用等丝织歌了，我心中的神作

109%noctis全收集通关 事实上我并不推荐朋友们玩这个游戏，它的难度不适合大部分人，评论区的差评也主要针对它的难度。 不过我玩的挺尽兴的，通关后很爽，给个好评支持一下制作组。 二代也快要出了，期待一下。 ------------------------------------------------------------------- 这游戏的优缺点也值得说一说，总体是优点多，但是细节差： 看得出来制作组是想模仿空洞骑士的，空洞里面有的系统这游戏全有，还额外加上了我喜欢的加点系统（而且还不是那种后期全能点齐，毫无抉择的那种），还创新的加入了射箭的技能，给跳跳乐带来了更多变化（也更难了）。  这游戏开场动画就给我整不会了，字幕和旁白语言对不上去，混沌（chaos）念成考斯，明明旁白讲了一堆，字幕就简单概况了一下。混沌的念法让我感觉有点AI的味道，但是整体听着还行。 在看成就，（中文），大部分都没问题。但是大部分成就描述后面都是（Noctis），只有成就与世长辞是在诺克提斯模式中，和之前没有统一。看看DLC  Virtuoso,描述写着Aeterna Noctis: Virtuoso 是一种 Boss 冲刺模式。boss rush在中文语境里也算常用，所以这不是一眼机翻吗？这游戏的中文整体感觉是机翻+精修的味道，大部分地方语句都挺通顺的，但是很多细节的地方都有问题。一个合理的猜测是（虽然我不是首发玩的），上面2个翻译的问题应该都是后续更新的内容，这种明显的前后不一致的问题说明初版（本体）内容是精翻的，而后续内容制作组图省事，更偏向机翻。 剧情上也是，明明每个字都认识，实际上我没看懂这剧情（虽然也没认真看就是了。。。），不过剧情真的很怪。 很多地方字体大小都不一样，简体参杂着繁体。。。  跳跳乐充斥着这个游戏，毫不夸张的说这游戏除了打怪（堆怪）就是跳跳乐。制作组很贴心的几步一个路灯（存档点+复活点），装上死亡不掉灵魂的宝石（护符）就是随便死，残血找到路灯直接送死就能满血。不过没有这个路灯这游戏是夸张般的难。跳跳乐的设计问题挺多的， 1跳跳乐拿完奖励，还要反着跳一遍才能回来。制作组明明会设计回型结构，跳跳乐跳完刚好开个捷径回到起始位置，或者一个传送门回去，但他偏不是每个跳跳乐都这么设计的，不想反着跳只能用返回骨片回传送点了。但是那种前不着村的位置就很恶心，要好好考量从有限的传送点跳到起始位置的难度和反着跳一遍的难度，哪个更容易接受。 2跳跳乐很多是从尖刺和电锯中穿越过去的，即有很多考验跳跃时机考验判断力的跳跳乐，而不是蔚蓝（大部分）那种考验操作精准的，所以这种跳跳乐在掌握时机前真的是一直一头撞上电锯撞死。 3跳跳乐喜欢塞怪，字面意思，放个空中弹幕怪纯恶心人，尤其是空中只有限时平台，还特别小，被弹幕打下来平台也没了，找不到新的立足点就直接摔死了。 4跳跳乐引入了射箭（尤其是传送箭），所以难度很高，要同时具备跳跃，冲刺，下劈，射箭，传送，切换箭的熟练使用，非常难。用射箭减速宝石（时间蓝宝石）时感觉有bug，有时候会忽快忽慢，而且如果连续射箭传送再射箭的话，可能第二次射箭没有减速，速度会一下子变快，很恶心。在平地先射箭后跳跃的话，因为减速宝石的减速，跳跃很可能跳不起来。高难跳跳乐肯定是要装备时间蓝宝石的，但是会忽快忽慢真的很恶心人，被坑过好多次。 5射箭本身有点问题，射箭的瞄准手感特别垃圾，远不如玩过的其他平台游戏（比如波斯王子失落的王冠，漫游奇境，grime），鼠标和手柄都要适应好久。而且手柄是射箭瞄准用左摇杆，默认和移动共用一个，经常会出现向前射箭，射完没有即时复位摇杆，人也向前移动，直接撞刺上了，很恶心。 6宇宙跳跳乐简直不是人玩的，第一次玩的头晕想吐，恶心。不信自己去试试，角色在星球上360度转，在背面操作都要反着来，很容易产生认知偏差。 游戏里个人感觉最难的跳跳乐是皇陵的最后一个，获得黑暗箭绕星球2圈，和冰火星球  地图特别大，玩的也过瘾，但是全收集跑图要跳跳乐也恶心，地图大，但是传送点很少，一张图1-2个。传送骨片经常用还要找离传送点近的地图商人买，全是浪费时间的多余操作。全图找贤者的支线设计就是纯垃圾，地图那么大，随机位置出现，要一个一个位置去找（可能的位置有10个），有些离传送点远的位置是真tmd不想去。幸亏不做成就找几次就行了。 皇家阶梯（天界之路）纯粪图，开放的地图，根本没有边界，地图又大，里面的内容又重复，经常是几个平台几个怪，玩得烦躁。  主角有6种武器，类比就是空洞的剑技（蓄力斩），法术（下砸）。6种武器除了鲜血手里剑外早期就能获得，但是问题很多，先不说好不好用强不强的问题。 1都不认识这些武器叫什么名字，怎么释放。展示收集的位置在从地图切换到进步（进还是繁体字，而且怎么看也应该是进度吧），只能看有什么，但不能移动选择框到上面详细看。而怎么用在按esc的菜单里面的怎么玩，里面写的还是（斧头，镰刀，长矛，手里剑，武士刀，佯攻）。在对话和宝石里这些可是叫（狂野之刃，镰刀，循环之矛，鲜血手里剑，主宰之刃，佯攻是主宰之刃的升级），鉴于有机翻的嫌疑，我一直在纠结狂野之刃和主宰之刃是不是一个东西。明明可以合并偏要分开，而且名字也不统一一下。 2这游戏模仿空洞的灵魂系统，出了个类似的鲜血系统，消耗鲜血可以释放上面的武器。但是灵魂是打怪就给，这鲜血是打死怪后才会掉落，还要人去捡，实用性差太多。而且尤其是打boss的时候，就几个boss打他会掉鲜血，而大部分都不掉，等于说除非打之前鲜血攒满了，不然整个boss战上面6把武器都因为没鲜血用不了。明明空洞是战斗和法术并用，这游戏根本就不行。 有宝石天照之魂的主宰之刃很强，2-3下一个怪，攻击距离长，而且能隔墙打怪。  宝石系统很丰富，蓝色宝石有很多实用的，比如跳跃次数+1，死亡不掉灵魂，射箭减速，自动恢复鲜血，自瞄自动追击，可以说蓝色宝石的使用贯穿这个游戏，相比紫色和红色宝石，尤其是紫色。紫色宝石主要是强化前面6把武器的，每个武器对应2个宝石，本意是挺好的，奈何紫色宝石要中后期才能获得，和获得武器的前中期不匹配，容易造成武器没有宝石强化不好用，武器不好用所以都懒得试宝石强度。紫色宝石一开始获得的时候都是无法使用的，要做完铁匠的主线才能用黑暗素材去升级，md条件太多更难用了。红色宝石强，但是好多是长支线的最后奖励，获得的很晚。  小怪很恶心，这游戏有无数种史莱姆，而且全是死后自爆，非常恶心，普通攻击的近战根本打不了。而且从中期，拿着喷火器的哥布林，拿着链球的怪都是近战难打的，还有一群空中弹幕怪，追踪瞬移怪，反着拿普攻推图绝对恶心。推荐射箭和主宰之刃推图，不过经常要用传送箭位移，个人用不习惯切换，不喜欢用箭推图，而且用箭推图刚需宝石和加点（蓝宝石和其他宝石抢位置），主宰之刃只要一个紫宝石就行了。玩完就感觉空洞那些只会撞人的小怪是多么的亲切，空洞里恶心的水晶山峰的狙神，这游戏遍地都是。。。  boss战就不提了，纯恶心人，弹幕类，瞬移类的boss巨多无比，尤其是一些演出好的boss，弹幕完美融入演出环境里了。点名批评史莱姆王，它有一招是空中滴水的弹幕攻击，我一直以为是背景的洞窟在滴水，心想这怎么打到我了。整体上boss的招式都很难躲，不过我是换一套全输出的build，硬莽过了大部分boss，可能也算简单？  游戏支线有个叫委托的位置记录关键信息，只不过记录的很大概，用起来比较糙。  经济系统挺糟糕的，这游戏大多数时候都感觉穷的要死，只有终盘才够把所有东西买全。开个宝箱给几千，后面图的小怪给100-200，但是商店卖的东西都是几万的，最贵的乐谱10w。只有终盘做完长支线或者打过后面几个boss给的钱才多，一次好几万。 ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 这游戏优点是体量大，内容丰富，有动画有语音，彩绘玻璃真的好看。缺点真的是细节太粗糙了，改进的地方有很多。希望制作组越做越好。个人玩起来也是痛并快乐着！

На самом деле довольно скучная игра, местами тупорылая и дисбалансная, весь хардкор заключается в платформинге, карты огромные, но по сути своей пустые в плане исследования. В отличие от других игр подобного жанра, рывок не даёт уклонения, есть тупорылый щит который можно усилить самоцветом, чтобы ускорить его перезардку. В общем для любителей постоянно долбить кнопку прыжка с периодическим выплёвываением стрелы с телепортацией может и зайдёт. По ощущениям в игре, персонаж просто каменный, неповоротливый кусок Г, по прокачке тоже нудятина редкостная.

It's a shame this game isn't more well regarded than it is. From Symphony of the Night to Hollow Knight I've always felt underwhelmed by those adventure/metroidvania games as a whole while everyone else seemed to hold them in such high regards. To me they always felt kind of drab, empty, and underwhelming because of the dull level of difficulty. Even HK, which is supposed to be a harder game, feels like baby's first metroidvania. Aeterna Noctis actually aims to add some much needed challenge into the genre and the challenge that is there feels spot on. There is nothing here that anyone with a little bit of practice shouldn't be able to overcome especially considering I completed it on a mouse and keyboard and don't consider myself especially great at video games compared to others.  Sadly, the skill level of average gamers in 2025 is probably near where most arcade and NES bred gamers were at when they were 8. This fact seems to have resulted in this game getting a lot of undeserved negative reviews. It's unfortunate that rather than respecting Aeterna Noctis for what it is or trying to raise their skill level to meet the demands of the rare harder than average game, these people rather cry and moan to developers and expect everyone else to be brought down to their elementary level of skill.  The only true negative thing about this game is that it apes Hollow Night's style so hard and so unashamedly. Even very small quirks like having small cutscenes for the blacksmith upgrades and the way checkpoints, death, and gems work are lifted directly from HK. The creators of Aeterna Noctis were clearly very talented and could have created something much more unique. Luckily they did not hold back with the visuals. This game is very colorful with lots going on in the environments. When I think back to HK all I can really remember is gloomy brown, black, and grey enviornments. Aeterna can not be more different in that respect.  This is a must play for any serious Metroidvania fan and IMO leagues above other highly praised games in the same genre. There is a ton of content here, far more than other comparable games. They did announce a proper sequel to this game, Aterna Lucis, so hopefully that game gets the respect this series deserves.

It's an overall lower budget Hollow Knight, with much better mechanics and progression.  It's harder than Silksong, but it's difficulty progresses much more naturally than in Silksong. It starts out potato easy, and ends up being stupidly hard, whereas Silksong starts out hard, increases slightly in difficulty, then stagnates.  But as I said, it's lower budget and you can tell by it's low production quality. Game looks nice and all, but it's not really consistent (like backgrounds don't really have any story implications, like they do in HK, animations are kinda janky, enemy designs are uninspired, and the music is incredibly forgetable).  It also suffers in the boss design area, most bosses aren't really that good.  But the good parts of this game are the platforming, the progression, the exploration, the platforming (have to mention it twice, it's THAT good!), and basically everything in between bosses (which is something that's kind of lacking in both HK and Silksong).

Excellent and lengthy metroidvania. If you're a fairly fresh/casual metroidvania player, Noctis mode is probably too rough for you. However, if you're an avid player of metroidvanias, it's not particularly hard or challenging for the most part. I'm currently 2/3 done with the game and haven't had a single moment where I felt frustrated - longest I spent on a section was maybe 5min. Luckily, respawning and reattempting is quite simply, and no section is SO long that it feels unfair. Regardless of any videos you might watch there is NOTHING as difficult as the Path of Pain in Hollow Knight imo. Yes, the average section is significantly harder, but the highest difficulty is well below the lengthy and difficult sections of the Path of Pain. In fact, most sections have a lot of "give" to them with your skillset (like, you need 2/3 of your teleport arrows, so you still have a 3rd to make up the gap). It's only when you finally get into the DLC that you are required to use ALL available resources, and even then it's not that tight to navigate.  Next we have the game itself - standard metroidvania affair done as good as any top tier game (Hollow Knight, for example), but having the "weapons" act as unique interactions is VERY much appreciated (the scythe acts as your secondary weapon and gives more "blood", the spear as your downward thrusting attack, the shuriken as your ranged, etc). Never will you not have your trusty sword, but supplementing the weapons instead of replacing them feels great!  Lastly - some performance issues. There are sections where the FPS drops (namely when reflections are involved), and it's a bit disappointing in a game that my 3080 Ti should run just fine, but it's not common enough that it's a major issue.

What an insane game! Insanely hard. Insanely good. And insanely polished.  I am not a masochist enough to get no-hit achievements, and I already died 676 times, but I very, very, very much enjoyed the challenges this game issued to me. Maybe not the bosses, because some of them were harder than souls-likes and made me mad, but platforming was brilliant! Even the most ridiculous ones like in [spoiler]Cosmos (HOLY ♥♥♥♥ what a map!).[/spoiler] Precision and timing needed for some platforming was... insane. But I totally enjoyed overcoming everything game placed before me, with some puzzles stopping me for as long as 40 minutes.  Definitely the most difficult Metroidvania I have played. One of the most beautiful – definitely top 5. And, for me, one of the best – I would go as far as top 4 right behind Ori Will of the Wisps, Prince of Persia: the Lost Crown and Hollow Knight.   Fake languages of all NPCs really bothered me at first, but after a few hours not only I didn't mind them, but even started to like them.  If you need to get gray hair from stress fast, I totally recommend this game!

I don't like to bash indie game studios as I'd like more indie games but this game feels like a flop. It's like the design team played Hollow Knight and then said, "I know how to make this worse" and then proceeded to make this game. It doesn't stay true to the feel of a Metroidvania but liberally uses the tag. The controls are clunky, the game play is punishingly difficult to control but not in a way that feels like it's fun to learn. Celeste's difficulty doesn't come because it's clunky but because it's tight, well-designed platforming. This is difficult because it's poorly designed by choice. The design team didn't know how to design the maps around the abilities so instead locked them all at the beginning of the map and then makes you search for keys to take back to the beginning for new upgrades.   The bosses aren't fun and the combat is clunky with weird spacing on weapon attacks, platforms and enemy movement.   I was drawn in by the art but don't really want to stick around because of the game play itself, which I feel a game should do.   Play it for free but you aren't in for a fun ride. Should have refunded but wanted to give it a chance.   2/5 Combat 2/5 Platforming 3.5/5 Art design (-.5 on the buggy animations) 2/5 Metroidvania feel 1.5/5 on Dialogue and world building  Overall 2.2/5

I was gifted this game as a part of my group's Game Bookclub Exchanges. My buddy had wanted me to give this one a go and purchased it for me since he had it on PS5 and I'm on PC.  Overall, it was a pleasant surprise to play as I hadn't gone into this game expecting what it ultimately delivered. Playing in Noctis mode gave me a nice platforming challenge and the abilities you end up gaining for mobility made traversing and exploring the world of Aeterna Noctis really fun.  For combat, I'm a fan of how many options you're given for building your arsenal. Whether it be the skill tree and its three main paths, the gems you collect for unique effects or the weapons you collect, you will no doubt find something for you.  Enemies for the most part weren't anything special in my opinion, some of them show up multiple times with a different gimmick. I'm also not big of a fan of having a good chunk of enemies being able to teleport so close to you, it felt especially annoying when I simply wanted to traverse an area to reach a goal.  The bosses, however, showcased some of the highest highs and lowest lows of the game for me. It both represented the ideal image the developers wanted to paint of a fast-paced battle where you dash and dodge incoming attacks, but also showcased some shortcomings with the game's design, performance and bugs.  For the most part, the game ran well for me, but some areas did suffer inexcusable lag (the Mirror Room in particular) and some enemies and bosses would teleport into walls, which made me vulnerable to their attacks and I couldn't hit them back. Despite being a fan of the game's platforming sections, the occasional stutter really makes a difference for the areas that depend on fast and precise action.  All of this is to say that, if you are willing to look pass some shortcomings, this is a hidden gem to keep an eye on and play.

The game has tons of bugs. But the biggest and most annoying bug is the map loss. When I finished the game at 5%, I lost the map once. After playing for 11 hours, and completing 28% of the game, I lost the map again. This is really annoying, because RPG games rely a lot on maps. I asked for the game back, and a refund, but unfortunately it was not granted. I had a lot of trust in this game developer after playing their game Afterimage, but now I am so disappointed. In the future, when buying STEAM games, and especially from this game developer, I will consider carefully.

Noctisモードで100%以上達成しましたので、個人的な感想を含めてレビューしていきます。  正直このゲームはかなりの高難易度なので、人にはお勧めはしづらいです、お勧めしたら友人を失う可能性もあります。 ただゲームとしては面白く、特に中盤辺りではスキルも増え、広大なマップを色々探索出来るようになり、アスレチックも慣れてくるので、ゲームとしては一番楽しい時期ですね。 バトルも決して簡単ではないですが、敵もボスもよく観察しながら戦うと、相手の行動がわかり避けられるようになり、ボスによってはごり押しも出来るので、バランスはそこまで悪くない方だと思います。   それでもこのゲームが何故お勧めしづらいのか、特に気になったところを記載していきます。   【一部エリアが見づらい箇所がある】 床が同色で見づらかったり、敵が背景に隠れていたりと見づらい箇所がありました。 一部光が強い場所もあり、あまり目に優しくない印象を受けました。   【近接武器がいまいち】 武器は弓が強く、剣は範囲が狭いので、正直使いづらいです。 個人的に使える武器は刃(紫宝石(天照の魂)で範囲拡大)と弓(青宝石で3way(トリケファルス)＋ホーミング(ヒートセンサー))が強いです。 ただ弓は宝石枠が取られ、装備出来る宝石の制限が足枷になってるので、スロットを解放するまでは使いづらいのが玉に傷。  終盤になると浮いてる敵が多く、近づくと逃げるので剣は当てづらく、ボスは瞬間移動を多用するのでかなり大変です。 それに近づくとバリアを張る敵もいたりと近接は何かと不遇なので、一部ボスのごり押しくらいしか用途がないのが残念。   【バトルよりアスレチックの方が難しい】 これはバトルよりも明らかに難しく感じました。 アスレチックに関しては、絶妙な操作が必要かつ、中盤辺りからは2つ3つの動作が必要になり、それが出来ないとまず突破できないので、かなり難易度は高めになっています。 正直アスレチックの難易度を下げて、バトルの難易度に少し手を加えた方がまだ良い結果にはなったかもしれません。    【アスレチックがあまりにも多くだれる】 この大ボリュームに対し、全体的に最初から最後までトゲやダメージゾーンがあるアスレチックがくどいほど多く、正直難しさより面倒さが勝ってきました。 しかもミスるとスタートor途中まで戻されるので、この戻し作業に萎えてしまう人もいると思います。  それにただの鬼畜アスレチックならまだしも、Cosmosという宇宙空間でのエリアが極悪で、円を360度回りながら、いつものトゲ地獄アスレチックを行うので、脳がバグって操作がおかしくなってきます。 しかもこの状況でoriのような時間制限付きミッションが発生するので、さすがにここの開発陣はやばいな・・・って思いましたよ(笑)  ただこの極悪アスレチックも、攻略がわかり慣れてくると不思議と簡単に突破できるようになり、プレイヤースキルが上がってくるのが実感できるので、修行の成果が出ていると感じる人もいるでしょう。(高難易度ではないと満足できない病に侵される可能性も・・・)   【その他】 ・ファストトラベルが遠くボス前にないところもある ・ファストトラベルの演出が長い(特に列車) ・地図を販売している商人が入口近くにいない ・一部ボスの再戦が苦痛 ・アスレチック突破後、スタート地点に戻るワープがない場所がある ・玉座に座らないと宝石装備が変更できないのが不便(スキルリセットはまだ許容範囲) ・とあるNPC探しが数箇所にしかもランダムでいるので、探すのがあまりに面倒   高難易度と謳ってトゲを至る所に配置し当たると戻される仕様･･･  その戻し作業が単純に面白くないので、そろそろ変更を加えた方がいい気がしますね。

Aeterna Noctis is a flawed masterpiece. The art and platforming rival the greats of the genre - it feels fantastic to play. The exploration lacks some features (not knowing 100% of all the platforms/rooms, even with the map; not knowing if you've collected all items in an area, and even being able to pay for an item locator if you have all of the items), and the localization is subpar.   However, even with some detractors, it's a joy to play and I loved it.

[b]Pros:[/b] [list] [*][b]Excellent visuals and character art:[/b] The backgrounds and character animations are highlights, with wildly varied and interesting environments ranging from gothic crypts to eerie flesh tunnels to sci-fi facilities. It creates a cool world. [*][b]Excellent Platforming: [/b] Movement feels tight and responsive. The platforming challenges are tough but fair, with death usually being the player’s fault. Later movement upgrades like the teleport arrow make navigating deathtraps thrilling (but also very difficult). [*][b]Satisfying Progression:[/b] Upgrades feel meaningful — new movement abilities, extra gem slots, and damage boosts have noticeable impact and make your character feel stronger and more capable over time.  [*][b]Massive World:[/b] This game is [i]huge[/i] (Can also be seen as a [i]negative[/i]). If you love getting lost in sprawling, interconnected maps filled with secrets and side areas, Aeterna Noctis delivers that in spades.  [*][b]Boss Fights:[/b] While regular enemies are mostly uninspired and awful to look at, most bosses are decently designed, providing satisfying, challenging encounters that test your skills.  [/list] [b]Cons:[/b] [list] [*][b]Awful Enemy Design:[/b] Slimes, bats, elemental circles — most enemies feel like lazy NES-era placeholders designed by a different team. Many areas reuse reskinned versions of the same uninspired enemies, killing variety and excitement in combat.  [*][b]Boring, Wasted Combat:[/b] The combat system is mechanically solid, with multi-directional attacks and good responsiveness, but enemy design and encounter variety are so poor it’s barely engaging.  [*][b]Terrible Dialogue & Localization:[/b] The writing is verbose, awkward, and poorly localized. Every interaction is padded with unnecessary, badly written lines. Pop culture references (Star Wars, Indiana Jones) feel out of place and painfully unfunny.  [*][b]Excessive Dialogue Interruptions:[/b] Inanimate objects and minor moments are bogged down by walls of pointless text when a simple message would do. The story itself is uninteresting and skippable — and you’ll want to skip it.  [*][b]Dream Kingdom Is Abysmal:[/b] This area is so badly designed, with flipped controls, invisible platforms, and pure memorization puzzles, that I feel it ruins the entire experience. It’s genuinely one of the worst sections in any game I’ve played.  [*][b]Optional Simulations Are a Grind:[/b] These time trial challenges require near-perfect routing and memorization for decent rewards, turning what could’ve been fun platforming gauntlets into frustrating, robotic exercises.  [*][b]Relentless Backtracking:[/b] The massive world and punishing platforming make backtracking tedious, especially when you realize you missed an upgrade behind a movement ability you didn’t have hours ago.  [*][b]Pacing Problems:[/b] The game’s size and difficulty will outlast most players’ patience. It demands a lot, both in skill and tolerance for its clumsy narrative and repetitive combat.  [/list] [b]Verdict:[/b] [i]Aeterna Noctis[/i] is a visually interesting, precision-platforming Metroidvania buried under bad writing, ugly enemies, and questionable design choices. If you’re here for [i]tough platforming[/i] and [i]huge world exploration,[/i] you’ll probably find something to love. But if you’re looking for engaging combat, coherent storytelling, or balanced pacing — this isn’t it.

Steam Achievement: "Successfully complete the first stage in the Temple of the Kings (Noctis)" - 48.4% of players have this achievement.  Less than half of all players stuck around long enough to hit one of the very first markers in the game. That's a bad sign.   The dialogue's an overwrought imitation of the anime conventions it apes. Painfully long-winded at times — that first interaction with the map seller is excruciating! And the result is an bizarrely guided Castlevania-likes where nearly everything from the story to the mechanics is told rather than shown. Brief interludes of exploration interrupt a near-constant flow of lore and instruction that, by the story's own premise, our PC should already know. HE SAYS THIS HIMSELF. CONSTANTLY. WHAT IS THE POINT.  Pair this slog with quirky controls and multiple minor visual bugs in just one hour's play and I'm just not willing to stick it out. There are so many other metroidvanias I can, have, and will play.

If you really like hollow knight this game would probably be for you. If you like precision platforming then this game would be for you. If you're expecting a typical metroidvania even at normal mode then you'll probably have a bad time. There's a huge focus on precision platforming and it can get tiring very quickly. From what I've seen the difference isnt wildly off from noctis mode so if you're expecting to avoid irritation on normal you're still in for a ride.  The game is a labor of love. That much becomes very apparent because it is quite a long game packed with content.. long enough I got exasperated when I got to a certain point and put it down for a year to pick it up again to go "oh right, that's why I put this down." The big plus about that is the "how the play" option when you press start because I think they expected people to be on and off for the game.. From what I'm aware this was an early access game that released in segments so that would make sense.  I mean I'm glad I got through it in the end and honestly I enjoy the story but I don't see myself playing noctis mode anytime soon.

优美的画风，量大管饱，打折后的价格绝对是值得入手的一款类银！ 虽然我玩得很坐牢，但是我仍然推荐游玩。 单纯通关游戏应该用不了太久，但是游戏地图数量很足，流程够长，玩下来到后面还是觉得很值的（坐牢坐晕了，确信） 总体游戏跳跳乐的量实在是有点大，说实话我不理解这样塞满地图的跳跳乐跑酷，跳过一次之后终点什么也没有的话，大部分是也不开捷径的，你需要自己跳回去，实在是阴间。弓箭的鼠标瞄准我也不是很理解，游戏内实际上没有特别需要精确瞄准的地方，为什么不支持用键盘瞄准，非要用个鼠标？键位值得吐槽的点也很多 ，宁按键解释没中文就算了，我可以靠经验自己理解翻译，宁这个改了按键之后的按键提示不改是几个意思？打到后面我根本不知道有些键哪个是哪个，可恶啊真的是，还好重要的更多是跑图技能。 boss普遍不难，但是小怪是真的恶心。 地图就不得不提一句，宇宙这个左右键顺时针逆时针的跑真的要把脑细胞跑没了，玩得我开始拧脖子看屏幕了。 一言蔽之，跑图实在是阴间，不过瑕不掩瑜，确实是值得玩的好游戏。

I gave it a good try, played for 20 hours. It made a fine first impression, my first hours were enjoyable. But, it gets increasingly un-fun after a while, as it becomes obvious that the main goal of the developers was to make things hard. Whether it's actually fun to play was apparently not a big topic. Platforming gets to a point that is just mentally exhausting and too complicated and unintuitive control-wise.  Boss fights leave no room to breathe and your character gets stunned too long after a hit. Half of the bosses attacks I could not figure out how to avoid the damage, even on the 50th attempt. It just seemed unavoidable and random.  Your own attacks are completely nullified whenever you get a hit and they have a weird delay and cannot be canceled by yourself. Half of the time I got hit in a fight was because I pressed a button but nothing happened because I was still in the animation or some delay of the previous action. Too clunky for a MV especially with hard bosses. Aside from these negatives in combat, exploration is also extremely unrewarding for a MV. 90% of what you find are treasure chests with money, and that money is completely useless after the first few hours, because you cannot buy anything that helps you in any way. After I accumulated 100k money, I spent it on a very expensive special item on a shop, and you know what that does? Nothing. I just wasted all the money I collected throughout my playthrough. Well, it was useless anyway. I found half of the gems in the game (equippable items that give you passive buffs), and they are all totally underwhelming and have almost no impact in combat. After the few first hours of my playtime, I didn't get any more powerful in any way. No better weapons or attacks, no new abilities, just nothing except for more health. Actually I'm already one step away from max health. But my save file now states that I have completed 50% of the game, with almost no player progression in terms of combat skills or abilities? Sorry, but this fails as a MV for me. If you want a challenging platformer, just play Celeste or super meat boy or something, there's no need to wrap that around a half-baked MV.

Disclaimer: Played on Noctis mode, didn't even try Aeterna mode.  It does a lot of things really well, but does other things inexplicably badly. I don't think the majority of players will even be able to complete Noctis mode, once you get the teleport arrow pretty much every platforming section after that is hard as nails. If you're one of the people who finished Hollow Knight's Path of Pain and thought "Yeah, I want more of this." This game is for you, because basically every section after getting the teleport arrow is like that.  +Platforming sections are very creative and require almost pixel-perfect use of teleport arrows and wall hanging to get through. +Map is absolutely gargantuan. +Tons of secrets, almost every time you go off the intended path the game rewards you for it. -The environmental hazards are weird, like I was thinking "Huh, I've never seen amorphous blobs used as hazards in a platforming game", and now I know why, you have no idea whether you will will clear an obstacle or clip the edge of the blob and be sent back to the last checkpoint. -Visually very noisy, in some areas the foreground clutter obscures the game area. Sometimes you can't even see your character because there's foreground objects obscuring him. The crystal caves was probably the worst offender, at some points I couldn't tell if a crystal that would kill me was an onscreen obstacle or something in the foreground. The Phoenix fight is particularly bad, the developers asked for feedback and everyone told them you can't differentiate between the damaging flaming feathers it drops and the lava background. they kept it in the finished product anyway. -Kind of ties into the first point, the King's hitbox seems slightly too large to get by the amorphous obstacles consistently. -I don't know if some areas are even possible without the gem that slows down time when aiming the teleport arrow, I kept it equipped for the entire game the moment I found it because I didn't see how it would even be possible to pass some sections without it. Should probably be a default ability rather than using one of your gem slots, really. The ability to hang on a wall and not move unless you input a direction should also be an innate ability instead of requiring a gem slot. -Sometimes I couldn't even see the area ahead and had to commit suicide to see the next path. Should probably let you  hold R1 or some other button and move the D-pad or analog stick, something to let you take a look at the area first. -There are a couple of sort of anime videos in it. They're pretty ropey, there was an attempt, they clearly did not have the budget for it.

Someone thought mashing Hollow Knight and Celeste together was a good idea. Or Hollow Knight but the whole game is Path of Pain but twice if not three times as hard. The main character you play as is the King of Darkness but he might as well be called King of Platforming because that'll be majority of the game. The game have flaws. And I mean a lot of flaws. But I had fun 7/10 of the time with it so it was fine. After you get the teleport arrow, it is just all precision platforming and the difficulty ramps up immensely. I played through the game with a controller/Steam Deck, which makes the game harder because aiming the arrow with a joystick is like threading a needle. Backtracking is just doing the platforming in reverse, just as annoying and frustrating since there are no shortcuts. Please don't EVER implement a mechanic where I have to retrieve my souls in a game where you'll die a lot x10 more frequently because of precision platforming. It is not fun and forced me to use a Gem to circumvent this mechanic. Even when you're max level, you still need your soul to use more than 1 of your Blood Charges (or basically same thing as Hollow Knight's Soul charges mechanic that you use to heal/cast magic). I enjoyed the game and it was very challenging. But my god just when I thought to myself "I hate this place the most," I'm wrong and presented with another area that felt worse. But thankfully, it is only 3 specific areas I personally dislike. Dream Palace, Cosmos, and the Undersea Fortress. Is the platforming possible? Yes. Is it fair? Most of the time, no. There is a Gem later in the game that'll give you an extra jump, which makes the platforming easier but not enough to trivialize the platforming. Alright, I think I said enough negatives about the game. Despite it feeling stressful, it was genuinely fun. It is just Hollow Knight every level is Path of Pain x2. The game is REALLY long. The LONGEST metroidvania game I've ever played. The map is REALLY big with a few interesting areas. Even though ruined by the platforming and its gimmick. I'm looking at you, Cosmos and Dream Palace! The movements are smooth and the OSTs are really good. The combat is generic. Overall, I give the game a 7.5/10  Edited: After finishing the game 109%, there is gem/skill tree build that makes the game easy mode and might as well make you unkillable when fighting the Free DLC and base game final boss. So I just cheesed the last two bosses because I got tired of learning the remaining bosses due to the mentally exhausting platforming. If you're struggling and are tired with the bosses, just cheese them or come back later after getting more gems and skill points.

А теперь к минусам: 1) Боссы - [spoiler] В "лучших" традициях bullet hell, слишком сложно все атаки доджить.
