TL;DR:  Really hard precision platformer, that gets incredibly frustrating at times, but highly satisfying (also, there's an easy mode apparently, which makes the game much more manageable).   Great music, nice story, bad menus, annoying quick travel system, decent combat (sometimes annoying)  Full review:  I finished this game on Noctis difficulty and I can say it is one of the hardest games I've ever played (and I'm a pretty good Kaizo Mario Maker player.....not great, but pretty good). This is almost exclusively down to the extreme precision required on some of the platforming, which at times gets incredibly frustrating. Finishing every platforming challenge in the game is akin to being part of a masochistic cult, but every time you complete one of these, you feel like you've reached Nirvana and are being showered with champagne spray from your fans while your parents nod in approval at the time you've wasted.....they finally get it.  I highly recommend the developers to be a little forgiving on the hitboxes in the sequel since it is impossible to tell how you're even hitting spikes in some puzzles.  The combat can be a little repetitive, since most of the enemies are pretty easy to defeat once you know how. Boss fights can be challenging and sometimes unfair, but also incredibly satisfying once you beat them, leading you to yell triumphant obscenities at the dead sprites on your TV whilst you contemplate whether it's time to go to bed. [spoiler] Incidentally, I got the Vampire's Soul gem before properly undertaking Garibaldi III, the Emperor and the final boss. You can pretty much tank them with that gem, Leech and 2-point heal, which was great cos I couldn't be bothered to learn the fights after so many challenging platform puzzles. [/spoiler]  The slowdown arrow should just be an innate ability. I played this game with a controller since it's usually better for platforming, but aiming with analogue sticks often feels like trying to thread the eye of a needle with a sausage. After using the extra slowdown (which I had not activated for the many many hours I had it), platforming became much easier, albeit it still harder than a titanium mountain on viagra.   The menu is really not great. I won't go into too many details since many people have already reviewed this, but it just seems like a very weird system and really frustrating when resepeccing your skill points (you should be able to backtrack and not have to reset everything each time).  The teleport thrones are also way too few, which leads to a lot of really frustrating backtracking, especially if you want to alter your gem sockets or your skill loadout.  Then there's the map thing. The developers should just take a leaf out of every Metroidvania book and let the map be fully visible as you discover it. The fact that you can faintly see it and only properly unlock the ability for it to be visible (not complete by the way) after you randomly find the NPC and spend an increasing amount of money per region is crazy. (Also from a story perspective, how the hell is he getting to all the places you are getting to? This game is harder than a coffin nail and you're meant to be an immortal god, but this dude just wanders everywhere without any issue.....[spoiler] including the Abyss where your nightmares live and space, where he apparently seems to just breathe without a problem? [/spoiler] Why doesn't he finish the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game?)  The music is great, which is important, because you will hear it a lot while dying thousands of times to the same spikes. Seriously though, they made a big effort here and it feels like the developers cared a lot about the music.  Art style is also nice, but is a little too much on the dark colours, making it hard to see things unlike that Rorschach test where you saw your mum having sex with the postman and the milkman, which you can never unsee.  There is some of the most creative level design I've ever seen in a Metroidvania, which can also be a little frustrating to get used to, but very well executed nonetheless.  This game is the closest I have ever come to smashing my controller to pieces, hurling the broken pieces at my TV, realising they wouldn't do the damage and then going to a hardware store to buy a sledgehammer (and safety goggles), sit through a construction/destruction safety seminar and then wailing on my TV until it is in as many pieces as what's left of my dignity.....I highly recommend it if you like very very hard precision platformers.

The level design and exploration is great. Platforming is tough, but fair. Combat is enjoyable. Music is awesome. However, there are so many bad things, that I cannot recommend buying this game at full price. If it were a $15 game, it would make sense to forgive such problems, but selling itself at top tier indie price isn't justified.  The art style is all over the place. As if they took something from each metroidvania and just piled it all together in one game. You get crystal caverns and enemy style from Hollow Knight, and then you got most of the game in Castlevania/Bloodstained style. All with a little pinch of Steamworld Dig and who knows which games. It's so different, feels like two different teams made the game without communicating.  Now, I could even go over that, but then you have a copy of game mechanics as well without understanding why they work in other games. You need to buy map markers for 5000? Which is like a 20-30 minute grind. For ONE map marker. With branching paths, areas restricted with powers, quests, etc. it's so easy to forget where are the things you need to do. Even 16 markers are not enough. I spent all to buy them and I still run out. Which leads to endless backtracking just trying to find where the thing was. Now, you do get more powerful, so early enemies are easy, but to backtrack you also need to go through difficult platforming seconds again.  Another problem is the visual clutter. Often you get hurt or hit without seeing the threat before hand, and in some boss fights I struggled to see where's my character on the screen. It's like someone said "we need more detail" and they just copy/pasted stuff all over the place.   Now, I was able to put up with all that because I really liked the exploration and combat. The thing that broke me to give the thumbs down is the arrow aiming nonsense. You cannot stand still while aiming the arrow shot. The aim with controller is very finicky, which wouldn't be a problem if the number of arrows wasn't limited and you had time to aim. But no, you get one second to aim, and your character is still moving in the direction you push your joystick. I have no idea why they though to use the same stick for both and then make them work at the same time. This is a major design flaw that breaks the game.  I will probably keep playing, hoping that the arrow shooting is minimal and only required for optional quests.  Get it at huge discount. Not polished enough to justify the full price.

If they put as much effort into making the game fun as they did making it hard, this would probably be a good game. As it stands, though, it's a pretty miserable experience. Timing for a lot of the platforming is frankly ridiculous. Combat is mind-numbing at best, and frustrating at worst. The game ranges from looking gorgeous to being ugly or interfering with gameplay. There's a lot of challenge in the game, but it's not the fun kind of hard.  As an example, the point where I quit because I wanted to actually play a game I enjoyed was the Graveyard Master boss fight. He has three attacks. His first is a side sword slash. This one sucks because if you're cornered, it's guaranteed damage. Theoretically, the dash has i-frames according to the tutorial text, but I literally never got it to work against a single attack in the entire game. Either it's broken or the timing on it is so nonsensical as to render it unusable. You also can't dash through enemies because that would make the dash, you know, good. If you're not in the corner, you can dodge away, but he'll teleport before you can punish and start another attack.   Second attack is a plunging attack with shockwave on both sides. This attack is fine, it's readable, provides an opportunity to land a few hits when you avoid it, The only problem with this attack is that he hardly ever seems to use it.  Third attack has him go to the side, and then drop spikes from the ceiling, then raise them from the floor, or vice versa. He leaves a single safe spot for you to stand in. If he feels like it, he can put the safe spot in a place you can hit him. This happened maybe 10% of the time for me.  Do you see the problem here? There's almost no reliable opportunities to damage the boss, so you spend ages just waiting for him to finally feel like doing something that leaves you an opening. And fighting most of the enemies in the game feels like this. Sword and shield skeletons? Can't break their guard, can't dash past them, if you jump over them, they turn to face you instantly and block attacks from above. All you can do is ignore them or wait for them to do their attack. It's boring. Exploding enemies (of which there are dozens)? Sure hope you didn't want their loot promptly, instead you get to wait until they finish exploding.  Additionally, the game lifts a lot of mechanics from Hollow Knight, but just does them worse. Downstabbing your sword to bounce? Sure, you can do that. On specific things, and the bounce is super low. Attacking enemies to get resource for attacks or healing? Sure, it has that, but instead of getting blood on hit, you get it on kill, and have to run around and collect it. Less useful, and more tedious than soul from HK.  I could go on, but I think I've made my point. I'd call the game Hollow Knight at home, but honestly, it's not good enough to even receive that moniker, and this is from someone who has pretty mixed feelings on Hollow Knight. If you like stupidly precise platforming and can deal with the overall mediocre gameplay and miserable combat, you might find something to like here, but I honestly can't recommend it.
Great metroidvania with amazing visuals, explorations, movement, combat and abilities. Platforming is very difficult but never felt too punishing, so it was enjoyable to play to the very end.

Este juego es una maravilla. Diria que el mejor metroidvania que he jugado hasta la fecha.  Es dificil como él solo, y largo pero bien largo. El plataformeo es duro pero muy gratificante y cuando dominas las mecánicas es un gusto desplazarte por el mapa. Eso si, hay zonas tipo puzle que requieren mucha habilidad y precisión que para superar una primera vez están bien, pero dificulta mucho el backtracking.   Empecé a jugar en dificultad noctis y me estaba flipando, me parecía la dosis justa de dificultad que me hacía querer reintentar las zonas y superarme, así como los enfrentamientos contra bosses (recuerdo lo mucho que disfrute el primer enfrentamiento contra garibaldi, un baile de esquivas y de buscar el momento justo para colarle algún ataque entre medias), pero antes de comenzar el segundo tercio de la aventura se me hizo muy cuesta arriba tanto el plataformeo como los bosses, y ya la guinda fue el templo del oráculo, que zona más enrevesada por dios, al final tuve que volver a dificultad normal y a punto estuve de plantearme abandonar el juego por ese dichoso templo, pero entonces apareció mi Salvador Javucho, el rey de la oscuridad (literalmente, ya que es quien pone voz al protagonista en esa lengua inventada) y siguiendo las pautas de su tutorial que tiene en youtube pude superar este y otros desafíos venideros, y menos mal porque poco después me terminó de hacer click el plataformeo y encontré una build que me hizo sentirme cómodo de nuevo y le volví a subir la dificultad, y dios que juegazo hasta el final.  Volviendo un poco a lo de Javucho, me encanta el mimo que ha puesto al elaborar su guía al 100% y se hacía súper entretenido verle jugar por la maestría con la que domina los controles así como por todas las píldoritas de lore que va contando (lo del cronista es un detalle y si no fuera por el casi ni me habría percatado, ya que cada vez que habla es una referencia a cualquier cosa, one punch man, metroid, star wars, los gremlins, etc.) Además que cuando hay diálogo pone su voz de doblador y mola mucho. Hasta llegué a comentar en uno de sus vídeos y no veas la ilusión que me hizo recibir su respuesta. Un grande.  En cuanto a la build, he ido probando un poco de todo y entre el árbol de habilidades y las gemas hay muchísimas formas de personalizar nuestra forma de jugar por lo que solo es cuestión de tiempo y probar hasta dar con la que más cómodo te sientas o para ir adaptandote a cada situación. En mi caso fui con la rama central que es supervivencia, con temas de triple salto (dios cuando la pruebas no puedes vivir sin ella, que necesaria se vuelve muchas veces y la de quebraderos de cabeza de los que me he librado con el plataformeo en algunas zonas del final), y luego gemas de regeneración automática de sangre y la que provoca que los enemigos suelten más sangre, y yo iba atacando a guadaña que ya de por sí hace que suelten más sangre. Me volví prácticamente un vampiro y era prácticamente inmortal, ya que a poco que ataque la sangre no se acaba y si toca curar me curo 2 puntos de golpe, que sumado a la habilidad de renacer y a la que aumenta la invulnerabilidad a 4sg tras recibir daño, prácticamente me podía pasear por ahí como si nada y cuantos más enemigos más fácil y mejor.  El arte es sublime, en general me ha encantado. En los rostros, dibujo de los personajes en algunos frames y determinadas animaciones hacen ver el arte de aeterna noctis bastante amateur, pero en el diseño de los escenarios ahí sí que no hay queja ninguna. Quitando algunas zonas que no es que sean feas sino que son menos llamativas, como la zona inicial, ciudad famelica y alguna que otra más, las demas todas ofrecen estampas bellísimas. Por decir algunas, me encanta la explanada verde y con flores que hay antes de llegar a la torre de la luz, donde esta el viejito que te da la misión del shuriken de sangre, la propia torre de la luz cuando estalla el rayo, el cosmos que está bastante chulo... el fin del mundo que es muy muy bonito... el inframundo, decadente y con unas figuras bastante perturbadoras (una de mis favoritas), el templo del oráculo que a pesar de lo que me ha hecho rabiar es precioso (aunque parte de ese mérito es del tema musical), y el que es mi favorito, la zona donde encontramos por primera vez al robot del tranvía, que deja ver cómo un lago de fondo y un cielo precioso.  La historia me ha enganchado, tiene una narrativa bastante cuidada y cada escena animada hace merecer la pena el calvario por el que hemos tenido que pasar hasta llegar a ellas. Esa escena introductoria en la que te presentan el universo, narrada con la voz de Galadriel del señor de los anillos... aún la tengo clavada en mi memoria.  Ahora bien, me he dejado la musica para el final y es que no creo que las palabras que pueda decir aquí hagan justicia al gran trabajo que se ha hecho con esta banda sonora. Prácticamente TODO está a piano, es excelsa casi en su totalidad y me encantaría poder aprender más de un tema. No son pocas las veces que me he quedado ensimismado en el propio menú principal, dejar el mando y cerrar los ojos y escuchar esa melodia que de lejos es mi favorita de todo el juego, seguida por la que suena en las fases del templo de los reyes (lo que disfruté con ese plataformeo, es GOD), y diría que en tercer lugar está la musica que suena cuando escapamos de Caos al completar el Cosmos, con ese guitarreo y la batería, es sublime.   Dentro del juego uno de los coleccionables son las partituras que cada vez que nos hagamos con una podemos hacer que el propio rey de la oscuridad las interprete cuando las llevamos al órgano, y cuando descubrí que esas melodías (que son una version reducida y sin acompañamiento)  están disponibles para descargar tanto en formato MIDI como PDF como uno de los DLC me hizo mucha ilusión saber que voy a tener la oportunidad de aprenderlas una vez me anime a retomar el piano. Es un gran detalle por parte del estudio permitir que dispongamos de esas piezas musicales de esta forma.  El juego no es perfecto, lo sé. Esa dificultad que casi me hace abandonar estoy seguro que ha podido con muchos jugadores, y puedo ver que tiene muchos defectos, pero es que no puedo poner otra nota que no sea un 10, por que los ratos que he pasado con este juego y lo que me ha hecho sentir, soló puedo saberlo yo. Una pena haberme quedado en la estacada y no haber podido alcanzar el 100%, pero casi. Me ha hecho muchísima ilusión saber que la secuela Aeterna Lucis, donde llevaremos en esta ocasión a la reina de la luz tras ser derrotada al final de este mismo juego, esta muy cerca de salir y posiblemente acabe cayendo de salida. A partir de ahora seguiré muy de cerca el trabajo de Aeternum Game Studio y todo lo que tenga que ver con el universo de Aeterna.  Actualizo: Completado finalmente en un 107% en unas 55h más o menos, del 109% que es el máximo tras la adición del DLC el foso de los condenados.

As a preface, if you enjoy platforming above all else then you might develop a drastically different opinion of this game than me. That said, let's begin with the review of everything that went wrong with this game. Breaking it down into sections, let's start with the obvious major issue that plagues every other aspect of the game by osmosis: Art.  Art (and perhaps more importantly Art direction) in this game is really noticeably amateurish, and not just in a charming indie way. More in a "Why would they have a foreground that can literally obscure the player, enemies, and projectiles?" way. Why would the lava slimes be the exact same color as the lava in the background, and when they die they shoot lava that's visually cluttered by the explosion of loot upon death? This might sound like an isolated issue, but over half the enemies in the game you'll encounter (maybe more?) are variants of exploding slime enemies. All of them are color coded to blend into the background of where they are and often they have explosion effects that make me wonder if I'm old enough to need reading glasses. These mistakes are fundamental issues that someone in the team should've noticed just in the course of seeing that purple enemies on purple surfaces on purple backgrounds that shoot purple attacks maybe isn't a great idea. Good difficulty comes from overcoming *clear* hazards and *visible* attacks. There are spikes in the foreground, spikes in the background, and spikes that actually kill you. Why are all these present on the same screen with the same art style? Why do many enemy attacks have much bigger hitboxes than the actual animated attack? This is really basic stuff, so I'm hoping the devs already learned their lesson and didn't repeat this kind of mistake in their next game(s).  Similarly to the overall art direction, the menu design might be the worst I've ever seen in a game. I'm not even sure where to begin on how bad it is so I'm not going to go on about it. Suffice to say, this is literally the most basic thing a game should have. The map clarity resembles your recollection of a night you were blackout drunk, even when you buy the maps from the guy who is only there to sell maps because the devs played hollow knight and just decided to copy things without considering whether it actually made to sense to add them to their game. On that note, the death penalty has no reason to be in the game at all. More on that later.  And before the inevitable refrain arrives, yes I beat the game on Hard so this isn't a "get good" issue. On that topic, the difficulty of the game is all over the place. Combat in the game is, frankly, extremely bad. Moreover, enemies are designed more to waste your time than be a genuine threat. And even if they were, the checkpoint system completely removes difficulty from combat outside of boss fights. You're actually better off killing yourself if your health is particularly low because you're probably 5 seconds from a checkpoint. This makes the death "penalty" a meaningless souls/hollow knight reference that doesn't actually affect your gameplay at all. What's really humorous is they must have thought it was some kind of huge deal since the payment to get your soul back if you don't want to go get it in the rare occasion you didn't get it back immediately is losing ALL of your exp. This feels needlessly punitive to people who aren't good enough at platforming or combat that this mechanic actually affects them, while meaningless to anyone just cruising through and killing themselves at opportune times near checkpoints.  Anyway, most enemies are either the aforementioned exploding slimes who are designed to literally do nothing (they don't attack) but waste your time upon their death explosion that will force you to jump around or shield, or enemies that pose no real threat to you until the enemies in the very last area of the game. More importantly than the enemies themselves though, the actual feedback of combat is abysmal. There's no real hit feedback or hitstop (except when you yourself are hit for some reason), when you block something with the shield there's no indication you blocked (which can cause situations where you're not sure if you actually even blocked a hit), there's no castlevania backdash cancel, no jump cancel except out of dash (which can't be cancelled into), and basically no fighting game or castlevania-esque cancels you can perform that make combat smoother and more responsive. This makes the game very extremely basic in combat despite the wide array of weapons and tools. Ultimately they're a series of bandaids over a rotten core. Give me a whip and 6 different ways to move cleanly over 6 different weapons and clunky movement with no cancels. This leads me to my next points...  Bosses. Because this game is always platforming-focused above all else, especially in the boss fights, this might be an area where I can admit the game doesn't have fundamental game design flaws but rather just isn't to my personal taste. If you want boss fights to be platforming extravaganzas then I think you could really enjoy the fights here. Unfortunately for me, once I'd seen 100 teleport arrows puzzles in a row, I wasn't exactly thrilled that the boss fight(s) were also a teleport arrow puzzle. In theory, a game like this could break up all the platforming with boss fights being more focused on the combat system. However, since the combat is so barebones it can only really be a platforming exercise with occasional hitting the boss with your weapons of choice. The final boss especially felt like some kind of self-parody of the game, wherein the answer to every boss mechanic was "Just teleport arrow, bro." and the answer was correct.  If you've read this far, I'm sorry you've had to suffer through this much of my prose. However, I must include one final problem that plagues the game. Backtracking. So, the fast travel system is very poorly realized to begin with (not to mention how annoying it is to to have to go through the imprecise selection process on the map) but in addition there's no way to get back to the fast travel points but by traversing or by using a consumable you will probably only have one of at a time for most of the game and costs money to buy. What this means is, in conjunction with the devs never thinking of the concept of a "shortcut after puzzle completion," that whenever you do the latest teleport arrow platforming puzzle, you gotta do the same puzzle backwards just to get back to where you started. It'll often be basically the same thing the way back, so it's not "new" or "exciting" gameplay to have to go back the way you came. It's just boring and made me want to uninstall due to how annoying it was every time. People say this game has a lot of content, but frankly I think if you actually had convenient fast travel and didn't have to do every puzzle on the way in and on the way out, I could've cut like 10 hours off my finished playthrough on hard.   Finally, I'm genuinely happy to be done with this game and I hope reading this has been as cathartic for you as writing it was for me. Good luck to the devs on their future projects and I hope they learn from their mistakes.

Yes, I have played too many challenging games, precision platformers, and metroidvanias, but this game's difficulty feels just right even on the hard difficulty. I'm in the endgame now and I've never been stuck for more than a few minutes on any boss or section, other than a boss that apparently I beat earlier than you're expected to. And it's been a really fun game the entire time! My only complaint is not knowing where the map seller is in each area and so you can end up wandering around blind for too long if you miss him. I'd recommend looking up a map online for each section solely to find the map seller, then go back to exploring. Otherwise, it's been a flawless experience.

玩了12个小时解锁成就：[h1] 不朽是一种折磨 [/h1]（死亡一千次）楞是给我死红温了    上次给我玩破防的类银河战士恶魔城还是刚发售那会的英灵士魂，我只能这游戏和那个不相上下，跳跳乐实在太多了，就算能力拿齐了很多地方还是费劲，普通难度BOSS倒还好，但是困难难度BOSS血量多了1倍都不止，第一个BOSS打着感觉就不对劲，直到蠕虫BOSS果断换了普通难度整个人都舒畅了。 我的建议是没怎么玩过类银河恶战士魔城游戏的玩家最好别玩，同类型的好游戏太多了真没必要来受着罪。  类银河恶魔城爱好者熟手玩普通难度就行了，困难难度还是留给大佬和受苦达人来吧

Noctis难度全支线全收集，先说结论 [h3] 如果一款质量尚佳的类银河城作品，受束于所谓“难度门槛”而遭到冷遇 那不是这款游戏的憾事，而是无缘认识它的类银玩家的憾事 [/h3]  [h3] 尽管我会给相对较高的评价，但实则缺点和优点一样多，很多设计给人一种“用了心但不得其要”的感觉 [/h3]  讲讲优点 内容丰富。公认的东西不赘述，值得称道的是每个区域都做出了自己的特色。小怪种类繁多+流程漫长，但通关补收集时见怪便知当初相遇的大体位置，足见设计成效 探索感强。内容多、隐藏多、且收集品和探索难度的关联性强。经历一场挑战之后，你总是能得到期待中的丰厚奖励 手感舒适。尽管跳跳乐判定严格，但操作精度完全跟得上自己的微调。复杂关卡撞刺后，首先思考的都是自身的操作优化 曲线平滑。流程从头到尾都贯彻循序渐进的难度升级，挑战跟着熟练度走。后期十钥四门这些关卡其实卡不了玩家太久，便是得益于操作惯性的纯熟 剧本尚可。讲了个基本没有借鉴痕迹的故事。尽管内容算不上精良，但不乏创意，且文案诙谐风趣 BOSS设计。不出色，但交互节奏强。只要学习成本到位，打法就很容易掌握。这方面做的最好的是梦魇团长，很遗憾这游戏里没有那么精彩的BOSS  再说缺点 传送机制。传送点虽不多，但实际够用，问题出在和购物机制的融合上。BOSS战挑战中损失的血瓶无法恢复，且较难的BOSS门口大多不给传送。因而想快速稳定回收血瓶再战，必须经历【返回骨片/跑回传送点→传送→跑到商人面前购买→跑回传送点→传送→跑到BOSS门前】这一流程。平白创造太多垃圾时间，以21年12月的作品而言，实在是低级水准 关卡设计。二段跳，空冲，光箭，传送箭的过渡平滑、反馈优良。但从流程中段获得传送箭开始，平台设计就一眼看到头了。自此跳跳乐只有难度在升级，设计感却完全停滞，致使流程跑图越来越无聊。稍微玩出点花样的宇宙，体验上也并不有趣 美术画面。美术本身是下了大功夫的，但光污染非常强。尤其最后一张图污染光明后的黑紫色背景，毫无疑问到了伤眼的程度 流派平衡。前期平A足够处理一切，后期实用的只剩主宰之刃，其他都很鸡肋。[spoiler] 但主宰秒怪确实很爽，通关收尾扫图阶段就该有这种数值 [/spoiler]  整体而言，是款超出我期待的作品。玩之前以为只是单纯的难，但结果来看，没想象中难，比想象中好玩 隔壁买KEY只需10+。以这个价位，能玩到体量和完成度如此高的类银作品，还是不容错过的 推荐给每一个喜欢类银，更喜欢平台跳跃的玩家

TLDR; Buy this game. It's minimum top 5 all time for me.  I would like to address the negative reviews first:  Look guys, I get your complaints. I experienced what you experienced. I also see that you lack any grit to make it past the 5% mark. I think that is the bigger issue here. I apologize to the developers on their behalf. This is why review ratings lack reliability. However, I can advocate for your concerns as well. IF the developers fully addressed the issues/complaints, what you would have would be GOTY material. As it stands now, I honestly think it has every element in place to fulfill that dream. Just clean up the tid bits and be encouraged that you created something amazing.  I would like to address the Hollow Knight Enthusiasts:  This game is the closest you will get to Hollow Knight. For respect points, I was able to complete all the pantheons with the exception of defeating Absolute Radiance. GAH! However, let me be clear in saying this is definitely NOT Hollow knight. It is comparable in some major elements, but this game draws upon so many other inspirations to the point it stands alone. It could be argued this game is just as difficult as Hollow Knight. The one difference that makes Aeterna easier, is your save points are frequent, and you always have one before each boss/platform segment/etc. I really wish Hollow Knight had this, helps to knock out repetition for fast improvement. Because of this, your skills as a platformer will be unrecognizable after you finish this game. Maybe I can beat Absolute Radiance now lol. Wouldn't that be the ultimate indicator?  My Review:  This game was incredibly difficult and this game could not have been made without at least a hint of sadistic tendencies.  You will experience end-game difficulty from start to end. However, there is a way to change the settings at any point to make the challenge easier. I never used it, so I can't say much about it.  There's so much I can say about this game, but a few things stood out for me: 1. Progression in character, mechanics, & skill 2. Exploration & environmental diversity 3. Trials and Rewards  Progression in character, mechanics, & skill The game does a fantastic job at implementing progression. In the beginning, you are shown as a character who is weak, and is weak. There's not much you can do. By the end, you feel like some kind of Dragonball Z character about ready to destroy a planet. The developers did create a storyline. The concept is interesting, and the way it plays itself into the actual design of the game produces some reeeeally interesting environments. There is also a skill tree, and you essentially earn XP through killing monsters. What you can spend your XP on helps quite a bit with enduring enemies and locations. As for mechanics, you will walk into this game as a naive and soft - fingered baby. When you leave, you will have the hardness of armor on your finger tips, the unfaltering focus and dexterity of a ninja, and the grit of a war-torn gladiator.   Exploration & environmental diversity Great god this game has the most vibrant and diversified environments of any game I've played. One area you will be underground surround by lava, in another area in the cosmos jumping from one orbital sphere to the next. The monsters fit the profile of these locations and that adds to the diversity. There is also no sense of where you are actually heading off to, in the same sense as Hollow Knight. You just need to explore and where you go is where you go. Eventually you will have the game mapped out and a better sense of areas. The only thing stopping full exploration may be due to requirements in equipment. Also, the game is HUGE. I'm sure the diversity in environment adds to the perception of said hugeness, but holy smokes I cannot understate how big the overall map is.  Trials and Rewards Along this journey, you gain additional skills and abilities. These skills and abilities are absolutely necessary, and will be used in combination of each other. You cannot progress unless you master the skill and the platforming difficulties that come along with it. There is no forgiveness here either. There were challenges in the beginning that were just as difficult, if not more, than towards the end. Note, I will applaud anyone who can beat this game, but do not expect to be that person. It seems that only 1 in 7.5 people make through the last boss, this does not include other challenges or side quests. I would approach certain challenges and think to myself, "YOU WANT ME TO DO WHAT NOW!? NO EFFING WAY.". Sure enough, I would persevere, my skill would increase, and I would succeed. There was one spot about 60% through the game where I legitimately considered the possibility of defeat. It was a mind-boggling section where I would not blame a single soul for quitting. In return, the game does a tremendous job of rewarding your efforts. Your confidence and perseverance will increase. All items have impact, and nothing is unnecessarily redundant. You will always need coin. Always need health. Skill points are awesome to get, even just one. The dopamine drip this game gives you for succeeding is so, so good.  I could go on, but I wanted to offer a more summary-level review that focused on a few points that impacted me the most. Good luck to anyone considering this game. To those played this game through, congratulations. This was not an easy game.

Could be a great hollow knight clone, but the platforming is very controversial. Basically feels like the cross of Castlevania x Celeste. The combat looks smooth, hitboxes are well refined, the soundtrack is great. The map area is HUGE, it takes a lot of time just to explore it, not to mention backtracking.  The bosses are really hard on nightmare mode, yet they are a piece of cake compared to the other in-game challenges. The endgame platforming is just ridiculous. Being not much of platforming stuff enjoyer, I was really struggling with it. If you like fast paced action and you don't mind some hardcore platforming - this is the game of your choice. The game lacks the atmosphere, but technically it's nice and playble, high impact movements deliver unless you get stuck with another spike room pipeline.  Aside from the mentioned above, it only lacks some minor interface improvements, such as adding quantity of items to the merchant.  7/10 overall, a fine-tuned castlevania with tedious platforming

Если ты фанат\фанатка Hollow Knight и забрел сюда в поисках того, чем можно скрасить ожидание Silksong и временно снять клонуский нос, то стоит определиться ради чего именно стоит поиграть в эту игру и я постараюсь тебе с этим помочь.  Начну с плюсов:  1. Локации и визуальный стиль  В игре просто прекрасный графический стиль, дизайн локаций и, в целом, левел дизайн. По-началу первые локации кажутся серыми и скучными, но позже каждая локация воспринимается как "ВАУ!". Более того некоторые локации поражают своими "механиками" (не совсем спойлер, но будет уровень в космосе), что просто снесло мне крышу от удивления.  2. Геймплей  В игре очень приятный геймлпей и система открытия нового оружия\способностей. Буквально каждая новая абилка\оружие очень удобна в управлении и имеет свои преимущества как в бою, так и в платформинге, особенно после открытия стрел телепортации. По началу она кажется простой "однокнопочной тыкалкой", но стоит дать игре шанс и она сможет удивить интересными способностями.  П.С. Отдельный респект за отсылку к абилке Алукарда из SOTN по телепортации за спину к противнику при ударе и отсылки к другим играм\вселенным на этом не ограничатся.  3. Звуковое сопровождение  Не скажу что выдающийся (как в HK), но достаточно приятный саундтрек, который, в конечном счете, запоминается и надолго отпечатывается в голове.  4. Прокачка и билды  "Билдостроение" в игре скорее ближе к РПГ играм (пусть и не имеет четкого разделения на инт\сил\лвк и т.д.) в плане того, что древо умений ориентируется на определенный стиль игры (можешь создать чисто лучника, толстого танка, быстрого "ниндзя" и так далее) при этом смотреть часовые гайды или читать фолианты в интернете не потребуется, система достаточно дружелюбна и разобраться в ней не сложно. Также присутствует все та же механика "амулетов" из HK, которая, на удивление, может вкорне изменить подход к "билдостроению". Разбираться во всем этом и подбирать под свой стиль игры - одно удовольствие.  По плюсам, пока, пожалуй все, что смог вспомнить (давно проходил). перейдем к минусам  1. Сюжет.  Если ищешь глубокий и проработанный сюжет, то это не сюда. Здесь нет обильного количества лора через записки или интересных персонажей. Завязка достаточно простая и сюжет, в целом, не то чтобы плох, а порой даже интригует. Сюжет здесь можно описать как "простой", но это не плохо. Разочаровывает только концовка (она здесь одна). Если в целом, то неплохой средненький сюжет, как по мне, полностью загублен наспех написанной и вброшенной концовкой и резкими титрами, которые вызывают лишь улыбку и насмешку от недоумения. Словно у разработчиков закончился дедлайн, и они не стали дописывать хорошую развязку, а слепили на скорую руку.  2. Наполненность мира игры  Продолжает тему глубины - её отсутствие. Все персонажи (как по мне, без исключений) - пустые и неинтересные картонки (кроме, разве, что неплохого ГГ). Честно, запомнил из всех только тех, с которыми ты вынужден часто контактировать по сюжету и не то чтобы они шибко выделялись. В виду сего мир игры кажется, порой, пустоватым. Выручает только обильный бестиарий и дизайн локаций.  3. Интерфейс и некоторые геймплейные решения  Обилие "варенья" на экране и неоправданно переосложненный HUD персонажа будут донимать тебя до самого конца игры. Стоит загуглить скриншоты по игре и сразу увидишь, что там, где должно быть здоровье, помимо оного куча непонятно для чего нужной фигни в виде разнообразных индикаторов, которые, по сути и не нужны вовсе. Как пример: наш ГГ может восстанавливать свое здоровье двумя способами и для каждого из них своя индикация. Также и для остальных "способностей, которые, казалось бы, нет особого смысла для отдельного выноса на экран - есть свое обозначение. Тем не менее к этому кое-как привыкнешь, но вот к тому, что с кажого моба после гибели падает куча всякого блестяще-кроваво-темного "варенья" не привыкнешь никогда. Чувство словно ты играешь в платформер по типу Spyro где из врагов падают всякие алмазики и прочее. Это несомненно бьет по мрачной атмосфере, которую нам пытаются создать.  Также хочу отметить неоднозначный пункт, который в интернете, скорее, приравнивают к минусам, но я так не считаю, тут больше на любителя.  - Платформинг.  В зависимости от выбранного уровня сложности меняется и левелдизайн (сам был в шоке, когда увидел). Если вкратце, то на харде (а уровня сложности всего два: "простой" и "харкор") даже в простых, казалось бы, локациях платформинг на уровне "Пути боли" из Белого Дворца в HK. Многие считают, что платформинг в этой игре - самый сложный среди всех метроидваний, которые были созданы, с чем я полностью согласен. В противовес этому идет "простая" сложность, на которой платформинг становится в разы легче. Сложность можно переключать в любой момент и никаких "пенальти" за это не будет, только придется перезагрузиться у чекпоинта, чтобы локация обновилась. Я лично проходил на харде и отключал его только на некоторых боссах, которых ну совсем никак не мог пройти, так как люблю бросающий вызов платформинг.   В общем и целом, подводя итоги, я однозначно рекомендую эту игру к прохождению всем любителям метроидваний. Тем временем я снова надеваю клоунский нос в ожидании Silksong. :smile:

Fantastic Metroidvania. For me, standing alongside with giants such as Ori and Hollow Knight. I might even say that this game could TOP 1 for me in Metroidvania genre. A masterpiece of a metroidvania:  Pros: 1,. Mindblowing platformers (some of the best I've ever seen, along side with Lost Crown).  2. A lot of different collectibles. 3. Huge and visually stunning maps 4. Gem and resetable skill tree systems are pretty nice.  5.  Variety of spells/weapons and mobility upgrades (arrows/dash) make combat interesting.   6. Lots of creative scenarios with unique mechanics(Cosmos, Dream World, Mausoleum, etc) 7. Soundtrack is good   Cons: 1. There is a lot going on on the screen. I died quite some times without even realizing what hit me. In some cases, it's hard to know what's in the background and what's not.  2. Game is so big you'll get lost in what you have to do and backtracking the entire map is absolute madness xD. 3. In the begining, movement seems a bit strang, not very fluid. But you end up getting used to it.

I read so many good things about this game that I had to try it. Yes I know, I didn't give this game a fair shot, I played probably 3 hours total (played on a long flight).  I can't stand the movement, it feels like jumping on the moon, everything in low gravity. The combat so far has been horrible and an annoyance, strikes have no weight, no appealing follow up and monsters have no interesting moves.  Platforming is pixel perfect in a bad way, where literally technical pixels (e. g. Hairs) could make you fail.  The plot tries to be appealing, but it literally tells me at the beginning "you are going through a cycle that's been going on forever" and after 3 hours there was no change to that, so it was very boring.  I'm not sure what else to expect, but my time available is short, so I have to give up.

Aeterna Noctis es un metroidvania de un pequeño estudio español. Es un metroidvaniael cual recomiend y de los pocos que me ha hecho querer acabarlo al 100%.  Aeterna Noctis es cafe para cafeteros, si te gusto Hollow Knight y buscas mas del estilo, Aeterna Noctis es en esencia una copia de Hollow Knight, pero, que sea una copia no lo convierte en mal juego, al contrario, creo que como metroidvania es un juego bueno, con un mapeado grande lleno de secretos, enemigos y biomas muy distintos entre ellos.  Lo mejor del juego es sin duda la banda sonora (muy buena), la estetica visual (sobre todo la parte final del juego me ha parecido increible) y, mencion especial al diseño de niveles y al plataformeo, que hace que el ujuego no se sienta cansino y siempre tenga algo nuevo que ofrecer .  En cuanto a jugabilidad cumple, no esta tan pulida como Hollow Knight pero no es mala, lo peor (que no quiere decir que sea malo) es el combate el cual no responde del todo bien, sobre todo en los bosses mas dificiles donde se necesita estar muy fino es donde mas se nota las costuras, aunque de nuevo, para la mayor parte del juego el combate cumple y hasta puede ser divertido.  Por ultimo mención a la historia, la cual, aunque es un poco cliché en algunos aspectos, me parecio que tenia puntos bastante interesantes y bastante potencial.  En conclusión, si tuviera que ponerle una puntuacion, seria de un 8, es un metroidvania interesante, barato de conseguir y que es lo suficientemente bueno como para mantenerte enganchado en las 30h aprox que dura.

A genuinely unfun experience. There are plenty of ideas (mostly ripped straight from Hollow Knight) in this game worth experiencing, but the devs give such little attention to polish that the overall experience and the moment to moment gameplay is borderline intollerable. Poorly balanced color palettes and constant obstructions in the foreground make keeping track of enemies visually exhausting. Combat has bad feedback, and feels poorly designed from the ground up. The use of the souls-like bloodstain effect in this game, combined with frequent checkpoints designed to make the challenging platforming less agonizing lead to situations where if you get hit, a good third of the time it is correct to just kill yourself and respawn right away. You won't lose ground, and you'll be back at full health. Any game where hurting yourself is optimal (outside of speedrunning or niche strategies) is just a poorly designed system.  Many enemies are copy pasted from zone to zone with only the barest change. Navigating the world is a chore because a given zone will look exactly the same from room to room, with nothing to help you hold the map in your mind.  The best part of the game is the platforming mechanics. As far as I made it into the game (before giving up due to frustrating mechanics), those mechanics are largely ripped from Hollow Knight. Wall jumping with spikes, platforms the break, an air dash. As I understand it, the game's platforming introduces more complex mechanics further along, but I just didn't want to keep frustrating myself with the mountain of QoL issues to make it that far. If you feel like frustrating yourself, go for it.

Besides the fact that the color palette and design sometimes makes it very hard to differentiate what's background and what are platforms, wich is terrible for what seems to want to be a fast paced game, as you can't understand fast what's going on. And the story just seemed very bad to me. But i did not end the game because of what i just said above, so maybe this could change at the end of the game. I received this game as a gift from my Dad, so i really wanted to like it, but it is just bad, unfortunately.

The game starts off by telling you that not only is everything your character does scripted by fate, it's actually a script he's already performed thousands of times and he's completely aware of all of the steps. And it doesn't matter because fate also dictates that it will all get undone later and he'll have to do it all over again.  Every time you meet a character that's there to feed you lore, they just straight up tell you exactly what's going to happen and why they're there, and then your protagonist responds to just be like "Yea I already know, lol, we've met before and you say this every time".  So basically it just feels like there's no point to any of it. You already know the end of the game, you have a companion who could just give you all of your powers but is withholding them because of the prophecy.  This is why any story that involves a prophecy is usually pretty stupid. Really kills any sense of mystery, discovery, or agency.  Maybe there's some huge twist that totally subverts those expectations but I didn't stick around long enough to find out because the mediocre writing does not instill any confidence that you are being led to an interesting place by a competent storyteller.

It's difficult for me to give this game a thumbs down because it is obvious that a lot of effort went into it. But there are two major problems with this game:  1. Visuals: Difficult to discern obstacles and enemy bullets/missiles due to lots of clutter and limited color palette, especially in certain areas.  2. The arrow mechanic: I find it too difficult to aim with the gamepad. This wasn't a huge deal until I got the teleport arrow, but now timing becomes a problem on top of my aiming difficulties.   Admittedly, the second point above is more about my own abilities (or lack thereof), I will try more to "git gud", but it's already become too frustrating and I feel that I am more likely to fail than succeed at this point.  Final Edit: I absolutely do NOT recommend this game.   Uninstalled the game at the doppelganger boss fight. The boss is the same color as the arena, making it very hard to see, the fight requires lightning-fast reflexes, the hitboxes are inaccurate. I am not one to quit easily, but after about a hundred tries or so, I decided that my time is better spent elsewhere.   The makers of this game may have some good ideas here and there but I do not think they know how to design a good, fun game.   (Please ignore the hours played as I usually leave the game running in the background and that seems to inflate this statistic)

This game has potential, but it needs polish.    It feels upon a casual glance like a high school project that's mostly done. The controls are almost there, but combat mostly sucks and doesn't feel very fluid. If you're attacking in a direction and you attempt to dash away, the dash will happen in the direction of your attack regardless of the direction you're holding on your controller, and that gets old quick.  The platforming is decent, controls are fairly solid there, but after fighting the first boss a few times, I realize I don't want to put any more effort into the rest of the game if this is what I'll have to deal with.  It's not a challenge thing either, I'm in love with difficult games, but I refuse to fight controls.  Lovers of Hollow Knight will quickly realize that this game wants to be Hollow Knight and borrows *a lot* from it, but did not hit the mark.

I would rate this game a net positive with the exception of one dungeon which is why it gets a negative review.  I had high hopes for this game.  But don't get it unless you like precision platformers and frustrating traversal/combat.  This is an action-precision platformer with metroidvania bones.  If you like action-precision platformers then I'd recommend this game for sure.  But if you want a metroid vania experience, there are much better options.  First off, game traversal is entirely action platforming.  There's little to no break from the precision aspect of it and even backtracking becomes excruciatingly painstaking.  It's the first time a metroidvania made me not want to explore because of how difficult traversal was.  But it's not really a negative if you like that so it's a neutral point from me.  Secondly. unlike Summun Aeterna (a much better game if you ask me) the Dash mechanic has no invulnerability frames.  Again, not really an issue but Not being able to doge out of the way of a projectile or through an enemy gearing up for an attack is frustrating.  Again, not a knock against this game as some games are like that.  I just personally didn't care for how the mechanic was implemented.  Thirdly, combat is filled with a lot of "Gotchas" that are frustrating.  Almost all enemies behave in ways you don't think they would unless you fight them multiple times.  Every enemy has multiple ways to attack and it's inconsistent sometimes.  All of the above can be solved by me "Getting Gud" and I agree.  So they're not negatives.  The Oracle Dungeon is why this game gets a negative review from me.  It's needlessly complicated.  Crystal arrows are needed to show you the "true" path throughout the dungeon and they can'e be placed on traps (which is most surfaces), don't last long enough, and the whole dungeon is a hassle to traverse with doors leading seemingly random places.  There's a pattern sure but figuring it out sucked the fun outta exploring for me.  The game started to feel like work at this point and I was incredibly frustrated.  Again, I could just suck at the game but the oracle dungeon imo is just bad game design.

烂活，除了体量很大，很难找出优点 地图的设计就非常夸张，类银恶有跳跳乐正常，但你这塞得也太多了吧，应塞尽塞？可以说整个游戏除了正中间的大道，没有一条正常的路，都得带点刺。然而作为一个以跳跳乐为特色的游戏，制作组的想象力却没有跟上，整个流程下来没有出现一个能让人"初见一头雾水，过了直呼精彩"的设计，全部都是所见即所得，最基础的克星刺/双墙穿梭/平台跟随出现了无数次，每张图都有，甚至一张图里能出现好几回，咋的非得反复考验呗。初上宇宙时确实眼前一亮，然而很快又会回归失望，因为并没有诸如星球引力圈重叠之类的创新，跳跳乐设计也是三板斧老一套，纯粹就是变了视角和操作让你不舒服 再联想到游戏中的能力解锁，更是让人绷不住：冲刺、爬墙、二段跳这些属于是个游戏都有的，即便获得后再去之前的跳跳乐关卡，依然有一定难度。然而紧接着，你获得了传送箭，好家伙，全方位长距离瞬移，完美覆盖之前所有能力，把所有前半的跳跳乐全部变成小卡拉米。别的游戏都是不断获得新能力，并且能和之前的能力进行配合，每个能力都不可或缺，到你这一招鲜吃遍天了。奇葩的还在后头呢，前中期就能取得的传送箭，居然就是全游戏最后一个移动能力了(后面还有三段跳，但属于锦上添花)，从此你只能不停地射箭->传送循环，再无新东西 之前说游戏唯一的优点是体量很大，但这其实是包含了很多制作组拖时间的小心思在里面：游戏的飞行小怪和死后自爆小怪多的离谱，至少占一半以上，而且没有韧性系统，小怪想出手就一定能出手，你只能躲远点，导致随便打个小怪都得打一枪换一个地方，打死了再躲自爆；boss也没几个和你打正面的，都是猛出弹幕，很多射弹幕时还打不到，必须要等间隙上去砍，然后重复好几个来回；许多向上爬的地图，就是好多一人宽的小小平台一路往上，只要稍微精神不集中没踩到，就掉到底下整个重来，完全意义不明；所有收集品前一定是一段跳跳乐，而且想丝血过关然后送死回复活点？不存在的！制作组贴心设计必须带着收集品原路返回才算收集到，回去路上踩刺了物归原位，鬼才呀

The game is good (beat the game on noctis) but it has some big flaws, biggest elephant in the room would be visibility on some maps making it hard to spot platforming elements/enemies/projectiles from background. Another one was that controls were a bit rough and i wish character movements were more fluid (for example you can't dash cancel your attacks which also could make you dash in wrong way) but its nothing you can't get used to thankfully.  Other than that there was lots of obnoxious stuff existing just for the sake of it like: - having to backtrack from most platforming segments  and do them again in reverse after getting the item  - tons of enemies with different trigger on death effects that which mentioned visibility issues would make you lose hp quickly - Game don't explains well what rewards are you getting or what you need to do for a quest  Other than this i had fun with this game even when some locations made me curse the devs (and i would not be surprised if ppl quit cause of them) but i can only recommend this game for platformer enjoyers, if you are not one of them then its good chance you won't like this game even if you like other metroidvanias.

This is indeed my most favorite metroidvania/platforming game. I have never felt so fulfilled and proud after finishing this game with 97.7% in about 50 hours on first try (and yes, ofc I could get 100%).  After clearing this all other metroidvania games seem like a walk in the park and children's games. I wouldn't recommend Aeterna Noctis for metroidvania beginners, it's hardcore endgame stuff. Endgames should be in the endgame because otherwise you will ruin your fun and gaming experience.  Other than that, all those *** saying this game is too hard have no metroidvania skills whatsoever. I say it's pretty forgiving since you can die on purpose and then respawn at the closest lantern (if you lost too much health and are not confident enough to advance further). Penalties are not serious trouble since you can just pick up your soul at point of death, no problem. I never lost xp, not once. Yes, bosses are tough and demanding and have unique mechanics but you will git gud eventually :)  There are 2 difficulties which can be changed at any point. I kept playing on hard (noctis). BUT I really HATED the cosmos area (second last area), because controls are inverted and this really ♥♥♥♥♥♥ my brain. I didn't enjoy it, but persevered.  And I finally know what "platforming" game means. Jumping, dashing, teleporting and wall jumping around like crazy, evading traps, pits and spikes. But in this case on a whole new level. Man, this game is brutal and whoever designed the areas is a maniac and genius. I have never encountered this kind of seriousness and intenseness before. The level design, mechanics and abilites to evolve are without even.  Story, art style, soundtrack, exploration, level design, abilities, combat and every detail is off the charts. It's very fulfilling to hit enemies and controls are sugar smooth. Buy this game no matter what, there's love all over the place. I get some nice Hercules vibes back in the day from Playstation 1 :)

There are two main reasons I cannot recommend Aeterna Noctis:  Firstly this game should not have been structured like a metroidvania. Even on the easy mode the platforming is crazy punishing (fun), but it's very difficult to design crazy punishing platforming sections that can be traversed both directions, and because this is supposed to be a metroidvania that means backtracking through those platforming sections over and over again (not fun).  Secondly, the visuals of this game are all aesthetic no practicality. Many of the glowy attacks have odd hitboxes, often objects in the foreground partially or fully obscure enemies or platforms, attacks and enemies often blend completely into the background (particularly in the fire section), and sometimes the only indication that something is a pickup is that your interaction button will (very briefly if at all) appear over it as you pass it.

The game isn't bad, but it's definitely more style than substance. For intending to be a precision platformer, said platforms are slightly off compared to normal dash and jump heights and distances, leading to a lot of unnecessary microadjustment (i.e., more annoying than anything to have to constantly curtail a dash back to its intended platform rather than flowing from leap to leap). Hitboxes are vague (owing to the art style) and the camera is more for someone watching than someone playing, forcing memorization of sections due to not looking ahead far enough or moving with the player quickly enough. Honestly, there are several other titles I'd recommend ahead of this one.

Просто лютейший метроид. Великолепный визуал, сюжет, секретки, мувмент и продолжительность. Но есть нюансы. В обзорах активно шутят, что вся игра это как белый замок из полого рыцаря и люди не далеки от истины. Вся игра, будь то просто перемещение по локациям, поиск секреток или битва с боссами - это лютейшая хардкорная дрочь лавирований между шипами, лазерами, снарядами и т.д. Это не просто сложно, для обычного геймера это невыполнимо. А ведь в этой игре есть еще сложный режим!!! Я имея опыт таких игр словил кайф и не сильно горел. Хотел уже писать что казаулы разнылись, а потом дошел до Императора и Гарибальди, и эти два босса просто сломали меня к чертям. Император это адский ад, три фазы первая норм, на второй вам требуются рефлексы кошки и паучье чутье, а на третей еще и помощь всевышнего. Да я на Радана в DLC к элден рингу меньше нервов убил! Гарибальди же по тактике сравнимо легче, но то количество дамага которую в эту тварь надо влить про сто катострофическое, из-за продожительности боя руки начинает сводить. Но всеже игра мне понравилась, этот челендж я запомню надолго.  Из прям минусов могу назвать только проблему с засчитыванием ачивок. Слизня, робота и арену гоблинов мне не засчитали а ноты и коллекционера засчитали хотя я их не все собрал

Platforming is really good here which complements the exploration. (+) Various weapons and builds to choose from. (+) Interesting biomes that really keep things fresh (+) SO MUCH CONTENT! Seriously, there are so many collectibles, quests, etc in here. (+) I enjoyed the graphics! (+) So many references to other games, tv shows, movies, etc. (+) Platforming here can be hard but it is never unfair. Had a blast doing it.  (0) Really slow start. The initial 3 biomes are kinda boring but it gets better later on and the best movement abilities unlock a little later. (0) Some of the optional content are a chore (collector's quest) (0) Some puzzles are really obnoxious that I had to go get a guide. (0) Fast travel is given really early but the map is so huge that backtracking is still a chore. (0) Music is OK as is the story. (-) SO MUCH CONTENT. The game and the map are huge, I believe some of it could be severly trimmed to get a better experience. (-) Performance issues when playing on Steam Deck. Had to put most of the options to performance and still the game couldn't keep a locked FPS.

I recommend it, but only for both metroidvania fans and hard platforming fans.  The game is a flawed gem, with a lot of aspects which are great:  Platforming, level design (but not world design), respawn places, combat, bosses, lot of content including optional content, difficulty slider which changes the level design (first game that does this maybe?)  Some which are ok:  Story, art  And some which are a bit bad for the metroidvania standard:  Quests design which are sometimes grindy and obscure. It would have been better to describe things acurately in the journal for starters, or some kind of clue of where to look. The game forces you to double check everything which is tedious in a big, not connected metroidvania as this one.  That is another key point, the game is more a hub of worlds than an interconnected metroidvania. It has top notch levels such as Cosmos, the Palace... some not great ones, but they are not connected at all. You will find no shortcuts unblocked by abilities here, only shortcuts in the same "level". At least twice I had to do a difficult platforming challenge only to find out I needed another ability to get the item at the end of the path. So I had to backtrack and come back later, wasting much time. The map does not fill properly, stores are weirdly implemented, some platforming challenges feel more random than challenging, backtracking is tedious specially at end game.  I do not want to end in a sour note, this is a hard game. Despite everything I listed it has very memorable bosses and you feel acomplished after beating most of the platforming challenges. Maybe they should have just made an action game with more packed level design, shorter. But there are plenty of reasons why this game is recommended so much by metroidvania fans.

To anyone thinking about playing this, you should know that Aeterna Noctis is a long, frustrating, and exhausting platformer. It is a genuinely difficult game that involves sections of extended try-and-die precision, with extra levels of BS in the later regions (If you are interested in reading about this, check negative reviews mentioning "dream", "cosmos", "teleport arrow"). The experience has a chance to be infuriating instead of amazing. Normally, I would say to try it anyways, since it is good to experience negative emotions. However, with a 10 hour commitment needed before you get to the meat and bones, I think its better to err on the safe side.   Nevertheless, I found my experience with this game to be one of the best (top 15) I've had with games, personally.  Starting out mellow and serviceable (with a good amount of it feeling like a hollow knight clone), Aeterna Noctis hits its stride after the first third of the game. The ambition and passion, along with the abject cruelty of the challenge, kept me enthralled. The game lacks a lot of polish, but I was blown away by the later regions of the game. I don't really want to spoil anything, but these are the most impressive parts of the game.  This game definitely leans more into its platforming than anything else. The combat was forgettable, but acceptable. The bosses were difficult, but they can be easily brute forced through damage immunity windows and some of them encourage this. Often, a boss will have very few windows for landing attacks, but will stand immobile and vulnerable during a "bullet hell" phase. I found much more success in walloping the boss and tanking attacks here.  In the end, the reason I enjoyed this game so much was that it was daring with its beautiful ideas. But it didn't hurt that when I was subjected anything truly demented, my reaction was to smirk in amusement and relish the mental pain. I would love to see this studio produce a more focused and confident game in the future.

This is a hard one to actually post as there needs to be a 'Mixed' or 'Good with Flaws' sort of review.  On the end of it, though, I feel I have more negative than positive but there is a lot to like about this game and I am in a paradoxical place of wanting to say that while this game isn't it for me, I will be playing the other games made by this studio as what I do like they clearly nailed and what I don't like are things that are fixable or hopefully addressed in their next titles.  Art is good, music is atmospheric. Controls... 99% of the time are there.   As for the bad... Well, Critical Path platforming? Just about right. Fun. Getting all the side collectables and such? It feels actively antagonistic against you to where this frustration from trying and dying over and over (played first on the harder difficulty and swapped to 'normal' around the Infinite Sea zone and while this was easier this was still present.) actively hampered my enjoyment so much as to tilt the scales enough to leave this negative review. Tolerances can become punishingly tight and while the art is fantastic, the somewhat 'lumpy' areas of death zones in some specific areas make otherwise acceptable trajectories and jumps something much harder to judge.  Platforms in the King's Trials, also, feel poorly communicated... Like the edges are too fuzzy and many times it feels I should be landing on the edge only to fall to another failure as the visuals seem to extend a tiny bit further out than the existing collision to cause this.   Also the range of the crystal arrow teleport feels... Too short and the size of the projectile has resulted in many times triggering the teleport too early and just instantly failing back to a checkpoint because of it. That is to say, I am unsure if it's the tip or the end of the arrow or somewhere in the middle where it is safe to trigger the teleport on and never managed to figure that out.   Also I kinda wish the dash had innate 'dodge' to it rather than being relegated to a limited gem slot but... That's just a personal preference. It's fine.  The size of the game itself is fantastic at first but.. After a while, such a scale almost counts against it. Travel stops being fun and more just a slog to get place to place when revisiting areas for secrets. I think there is a happy medium between tight areas and rooms and vast open spaces that trigger my OCD to uncover ever dark corner of the map that exists so I can say there's nothing left to look for even if I know it's empty space.  For those wondering, I have completed the game at 93% total completion, killed Garibaldi before the light arrows which I had no idea was even a thing at the time (got a shiny cheevo for it, apparently), and found a lot to enjoy overshadowed by how much frustration and annoyance it took to get to the fun bits. Maybe that's your thing in which case have fun but for most, try something else.   That said, I am immediately going to look at the Eden Genesis they just released and give that a play. What was good was *very* good and I hope that they learned a few lessons on the difference between hard and punitive when it comes to platforming.

Ok, so Steam has stolen about 12 hours of total gameplay from my stats: I'm at 30% and this is my GOTY. I can see from videos that some elements have changed with each version release, but this is an outstanding game. Gorgeous. Music is thrilling (especially the forge and the Phoenix theme). I love everything about it--I grew up on Strider, Bionic Commando, the original Castlevania, Castlevania II, Zelda II: A Link to the Past and Metroid. I fell in love with RPGS as a young woman, but now in my 40s I have explored this amalgam genre of Metroidvania and this game. THIS GAME. Yes I get frustrated, yes I get delirious with joy when I beat a hard boss or a tricky setting. I got nothing to prove, boys, and achievements are secondary to the sheer pleasure of timing, fighting, and exploring. You can't make a jump? Die 1000 times. Equip the gem that ends the corpse runs. Turn down the difficulty. I can't wait to see where the story goes. So what they stole from Hollow Knight? Theft is flattery. I will play everything this studio makes, because this is a 5 gig ONLY labor of true love. Respect.

I've been a gamer for years, and I’ve seen my share of both good and bad games, but this one has been particularly disappointing. I went into it with an open mind, but the sheer number of flaws made it impossible to enjoy.  First off, the combat feels clunky and unpolished. It’s almost as if the developers didn’t bother testing it thoroughly. The questline is a confusing mess with zero guidance, leaving you to wander aimlessly. As for the dialogue, it’s laughably bad—there’s no immersion, just a series of awkward and poorly written lines.  The checkpoints are another major issue. Whoever placed them either didn’t care or didn’t understand basic game design principles. How did that even make it past quality control?  Also a frustrating aspects is the lack of an inventory system for quest items. You’re left scrambling, trying to remember what you picked up and where it’s supposed to go. And the boss battles? They don’t even have health bars, making the damage points feel utterly pointless. It’s like they forgot the basics of what makes boss fights exciting.  Then there are the platforming sections. The jump patterns are unnecessarily difficult, almost as if the developers got bored and decided to crank up the difficulty just for the sake of it. It’s frustrating rather than challenging, and it feels like a cheap way to add artificial difficulty to an already flawed game.  Finally, the story—it’s a mess. It doesn’t flow, it doesn’t engage, and by the end, I was left wondering what the point of it all was.  I really wanted to like this game, but it’s clear that it’s riddled with amateur mistakes. If this is the direction they’re heading, it’s no surprise that people are hesitant to buy their new releases.

Because of some design decisions I cannot really recommend this game. Often it feels not like I'm fighting the enemies or evironments in the game, but more the game and controls themselves.  QoL and movement features on highly restricted slots is a terrible decision. You decide if you want your movement to feel fun or if you want to be effective in combat. (no changing except on actual save points) The interact button on the same button as an attack. I cannot count the number of times I have accidentally read a terminal or activated doors or whatever mid fight because the enemy moved near the interactable.  The aiming stick being the same as the movement stick. So many accidental moves or misaims. Also, you move while aiming. So if you want to aim right you move right, too. You can stop that by pressing yet another button, the interact and attack button, even more accidental misspresses.  Not recommending this game was hard for me. I really liked the aesthetics, the general feel and the metroidvania aspects. But too often was I frustrated by the controls and other design decisions that it felt more frustrating than enjoyable.

没见过这么夸张的设计，不推荐类银重度爱好者和抖M以外的任何人玩，但我还是给好评，毕竟游戏难是游戏的特点，我菜是我的问题。 一开始的体验还好，往后打着越发不对劲，你在平地上好好走着路遇到的跳跳乐都是白宫水平的，打到机械区实在是受不了了出来给了不推荐，然后把游戏改成了普通难度。 游戏内大量让人无法理解的设定：解锁技能的那个神庙，跳过去拿到技能后没有捷径，还得原路跳回来，神庙外很多地方也是这样；光塔，我不明白设计师是觉得边打小怪边跳没难度太无聊了所以加了个一会打一发的轨道炮吗？关键是这玩意也没有增加太多难度，就是逼着你跳一阵得贴边躲一下，这纯浪费时间恶心人的东西加他干嘛呢；进机械区之前的矿车那块，他甚至没有给我放弃的机会，我跳到一半觉得有点难想先缓缓回头干点别的再过来，结果发现没有回去的车我必须跳到底才能走；机械区进去就是一个巨大房间，你要在地图没开也不知道地图商人在哪的情况下找路，然后这个房里各种逆天小怪不要钱一样地刷，高速冲撞电球追踪激光，制导导弹能在你屏幕外面朝着你射，躲着小怪攻击往下一掉又立马被一个史莱姆俩机器人包围，好不容易把这些恶心东西清完死一次立马全部刷新回来；梦境王国各房间连接情况地图上不显示，初见可能还有点意思但后面回头补收集的时候不知道怎么去特定房间十分折磨。地图方面总体还好，中后期会显得有点空，边境和天界一个不留神掉到下面就要重新跳一遍，各种收集物藏在犄角旮旯的地方不买详细地图很容易漏，两张图很相似也没什么新意；宇宙图刚进去的时候觉得很有意思但很快也会腻，整个游戏唯二的两场追逐战从演出效果到关卡设计都十分平庸甚至烂。支线任务的描述十分简略，也有天界龙这样不看攻略就只能把图重舔一遍的逆天东西，部分支线的奖励很重要不做会卡护符槽之类的。 还有不知道是游戏问题还是我的设置问题，我把移动的WASD改成了右边小键盘旁边的上下左右，现在光箭瞄准按键盘没反应，鼠标瞄那个瞄准线跟得了癫痫一样稍微动一下就疯狂抽搐手感依托答辩，希望这游戏搞出这种瞄准的家伙，不管你是设计师还是程序，希望你下辈子能和做ori2瞄准的那位一起投胎到一个只存在手柄的世界，别来祸害用键鼠的了谢谢你。 如果上述内容你都能接受而且不觉得折磨的话那这个你可以试试，抛开上面这些这游戏也确实有做得不错的地方，世界观比较宏大，叙事比较碎但不谜语人，地图看着很大内容也是量大管饱，地图商人那里可以购买揭示隐藏的道具方便后期做全收集（但是好贵），boss也很有设计感，死上几次就差不多能摸到门道，跟推图相比打boss体验好太多，喜欢挑战自己的可以慢慢磨无伤，手残也可以把回血一次两格复活镰刀强化点出来站撸不耽误剧情推进。总的来说我觉得瑕不掩瑜（虽然瑕的部分可能很夸张很夸张），如果你买了游戏前两个小时能撑下来那玩完应该也可以，两个小时内就被折磨得受不了了就赶紧退款吧没必要死怼这东西。 额外再说一下那个DLC地图，那几个跳跳乐不说多难起码不短，费劲巴拉跳完就俩升级点一个黑暗材料一个拉中拉宝石很失望。

Under a bunch of negative reviews of this game, the developer says stuff like "if you play this game 10 hours or more, it will start to open up and you will enjoy it". Well why didn't you start the game at the point it's enjoyable, then? People have better things to do than take it on faith that a game might become enjoyable eventually. If it's bad at the start, it's bad.

Beautiful visuals, excellent movement/traversal (with some very difficult but enjoyable platforming), alright combat. There's actually a halfway decent and explicitly told story as well which isn't always the case with Metroidvanias.  My main issue with it is that it can get a bit confusing as to where to go or what to do - I had to consult a walkthrough on a bunch of occasions. I also sometimes found myself wishing it had less combat and more platforming, but the game switches between the two often enough.  The demo should be a good indicator for if you'll enjoy the game or not. Beware though that you'll need to copy save files from the demo folder to the main folder if you want to keep your progress.  Also note that the "Noctis" difficulty is the way the game is "meant to be played" (as it is the original difficulty before they added the easier mode) - I'm surprised this isn't made explicit. You can swap at any time though, which I've done a few times when I got bored of dying to some bosses.

Geschafft. Nach 49 Stunden habe ich das beste Metroidvania aller Zeiten durchgespielt. Damit meine ich die "Pit of the Damned"-Version. Vielleicht war das ursprüngliche Aeterna Noctis wirklich haarsträubend schwierig. Die aktuellste Version hat auf jeden Fall genau den richtigen Schwierigkeitsgrad. Keine Stelle unlösbar oder frustrierend. Tolles Plattforming, wenn auch ausbaufähige Kämpfe. Der "Kosmos" war unglaublich cool. Zwei Dinge trüben den Spielspaß geringfügig: 1.) Aus irgendeinem Grund dashed der King of Darkness manchmal in die falsche Richtung! Warum das passiert mir rätselhaft, aber es hat nie zu echten Problemen geführt. Und ich möchte erwähnen, dass die Steuerung ansonsten hier wirklich präzise ist (im Gegensatz zu dem so hochgelobten HK, bei dem die Steuerung einfach nur eine Katastrophe ist!). 2.) Der allerletzte Level im Palast ist reichlich überladen mit Gegnern. Hier bin ich nur noch durchgerannt. Aber wir reden hier von einer halben Stunde des Spiels.

I'm assuming you're probably a fan of metroidvanias if you're considering this game, so I'd describe this game as an interesting blend of Ori platforming combined with Hollow Knight bosses.  First things first - I played on Noctis (hard) difficult, and this game is very hard. As someone who's played through and beaten Hollow Knight, Celeste, Blaspehmous 1 and 2, Cuphead and many more, this probably stands out as the hardest I have played. New areas will really force you to learn how to maximise platforming abilities, learn enemy attack patterns and triggers, and navigate the map well. With that out the way here's some pros and cons:  Pros: - Amazing boss fights - they will destroy you over and over until you learn their attack patterns, but never in a way that makes you feel like you're forced into taking the fight excessively slowly as you might find in soulslike games. - Genuine open ended exploration - I have no idea what the standard way to explore the world is, but I know for a fact that my route of exploration was only one of many. If you like non-linear metroidvanias this is perfect for you. - Quest log - unlike some metroidvanias, this game logs quests and quest progress in a quest log which means you won't lose track of all the tasks you can complete, and gives you helpful direction while navigating the open world. - Responsive controls - when I first see metroidvanias I'm considering getting, my first thought is to how the controls feels. Aeterna Noctis feels great to play, and I have almost never felt like the controls were floaty or hard to control (some free aiming is required on controller which isn't always perfect but I can only think of one instance where this felt annoying to use). - Great soundtrack - I don't care for reviews that overstate soundtracks, but this game had a good soundtrack that brought out the right feelings at the right times. - Lots of unique feelings areas - each area you enter feels like it poses new levels of difficulty and challenge with different motifs. The variation in setting makes for an amazing game that stays fresh.  Cons: Frustrating inventory system - unfortunately, you cannot view your inventory in a logical way in this game. If you pick up a key to a door, you cannot see what keys you have to direct your search for the lock. Sometimes you pick up a collectible and you're not told what it is. This isn't usually a big issue, but it can be really hard to tell what you have collected without a system that lets you actually search what you have collected. - Map is unclear and a pain to navigate at times - While exploring, your map is painted in with places you have been. However, for some reason the amount of map that is painted in does not align with what you can see on your screen. As a result, I've backtracked to rooms, assuming there may be an unexplored area of the map, only to find that it was the corner of a room I've stood in with nothing in it at all. In some cases, in order to properly record a full room on your map, you're forced to jump into every corner of said room such that the minimap properly shows what you've explored. - Quite a lot of open space in the map - This is mostly an issue when first exploring an area, but I found that some areas had a lot of rooms that did nothing for the game besides add volume. They didn't lead anywhere, they contained a single optional reward such as a chest containing gold etc. You can argue this is good if you like exploring for the sake of exploring, but at times it felt like I was running between huge cavernous rooms only to find nothing worthy of note. - Some optional challenges are underwhelming - I've tried to challenge myself to complete all the optional parts of this game, and unfortunately some of the rewards for this feel a bit weak - i.e. a chest containing currency and nothing else. Considering some of these are behind very difficult platforming challenges, this can feel a bit underwhelming.  Overall a really good game to play, despite its drawbacks. It took a while to click but once it did it became an instant classic in my mind.   I'd give it a solid 8.5/10

Probablement le jeu le plus difficile que j'ai été capable de terminer et un des meilleurs metroidvania / plateformer que j'ai jamais fait. La satisfaction de terminer un tel jeu est énorme :)  En négatif je noterais que le jeu est réellement exténuant car très long et difficile pour les nerfs. Il faut être patient et persévérant, j'ai 60h de jeu et il me reste 2/3 boss optionnels et quelques quêtes annexes. La quête principale je pense avoir mis plus de 40h. C'est pas un jeu sur lequel on peut s'attarder 20 minutes par ci par là... J'ai subi quelques bugs mais dans l'ensemble tout marche très bien.  En positif le jeu est varié ! Le mélange metroidvania / plateformer est excellent, au fur et à mesure on débloque de nouveaux skills pour débloquer de nouveaux chemins et ainsi de suite. Les environnements sont très jolis pour la plupart avec des thèmes propres à chacun (musique, style, mobs, etc). Chaque fois que je pensais avoir atteinds le pic de difficulté ils ont toujours été capable de rajouter une nouvelle idée pour renouveler le gameplay.   Mention spéciale pour les flèches de téléportation qui vont vous en faire voir de toutes les couleurs, la créativité autour de ce skill est géniale vraiment. L'astuce pour bien y arriver est de ne surtout pas oublier qu'on peut refaire l'affectation des points de compétences à l'infinie sans rien payer, donc mettre tous les points de compétences dans l'arc pour certaines plateformes sera indispensable !   La frustration du côté die and retry est beaucoup atténuée par des temps de chargement très rapide et des checkpoint très très régulier.  La sensation de montée en puissance au fur et à mesure est excellente, on galère beaucoup au début et ensuite on roule sur les mobs, ou presque. (pas les boss qui reste difficile pour ceux qui jouent en difficulté noctis).  L'histoire est suffisamment intéressante pour s'y laisser entraînée et donner envie de faire la suite !   Je suis pas prêt de l'oublier ce jeu, ni mes nerfs !

Esse jogo é mt desafiador em mt sentidos.  Ele é um jogo bem completo que nao deixa a desejar. Mas esteja avisado que se vc nao curti desafios entao esse jogo nao é pra vc. Lutas de bosses sao sempre epicas. Cada boss é bem unico e alguns deles tem uma segunda versao q é bem mais dificil. O jogo tem 2 modos de dificuldade o normal e o dificil, onde somente no dificil vc consgue completar todos os achievements.(tem gente q vai chorar por isso, mas eu acho mt justo) Acredito q a principal diferença dos modos é q os inimigos tem menos HP. Nunca joguei o modo normal. As fases tb sao bem desafiadoras. Como disse é um jogo bem completo, entao em momentos vc passara desafios de ambiente outros desafios sao com inimigos e claro os bosses. Entao nao existe monotonia nesse jogo, é açao o tempo todo. Existem algumas quests q da uma adiçao a mais no jogo,(cereja do bolo) O personagem principal conta com varias armas e habilidades, no caso desse jogos as armas dao a vc novas habilidades. Temos pontos de atributos para distribuir no tabuleiro de habilidades passivas e temos as habilidades de joias q podem ser equipadas para dar, modificar ou fazer upgrades de habilidade passiva ou ativas. O progresso do jogo é mt bom, durante a aventura vc irá correr atras de mais poder, seja uma arma nova, ou uma joias encontrada, liberaçao de mais slots para as joias, aumento de HP, dentre outras habilidades necessarias para avançar no jogo e dar o poder de acesso a area inacessiveis. Simplesmente esse jogo é um metroidvanias mais complexo e bem elaborado de todos os tempos. Por favor nao seguem review negativas, pois essas pessoas simplesmente nao conseguiram lidar com o grau de exigencia do jogo. Sem duvida esse jogo nao é para crianças.
